mednick_s_flat_vs_steep_associational_hi.name: Mednick's flat vs steep associational hierarchy theory
mednick_s_flat_vs_steep_associational_hi.description: Mednick's (1962) theory posits that creative individuals have richer, more flexible associative networks characterized by 'flat' (broader) rather than 'steep' (few, common) associational hierarchies. This serves as the theoretical backdrop for the study.
mednick_s_flat_vs_steep_associational_hi.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

novel_computational_method_for_semantic_.name: Novel computational method for semantic network analysis via association correlations
novel_computational_method_for_semantic_.description: The authors implement a computational approach in which concepts are related by their 'association clouds'—the overlap of similar associative responses—allowing semantic networks to be constructed from free association data.
novel_computational_method_for_semantic_.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

decision_tree_classification_of_low_and_.name: Decision tree classification of low and high creative groups
decision_tree_classification_of_low_and_.description: A large sample of participants completed several creativity measurements, and a decision tree approach was used to divide the sample into low and high creative groups for subsequent network analysis.
decision_tree_classification_of_low_and_.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

semantic_network_rigidity_difference_bet.name: Semantic network rigidity difference between low and high creative individuals
semantic_network_rigidity_difference_bet.description: The semantic memory network of low creative individuals is more rigid than that of high creative individuals, in that it is more spread out and breaks apart into more sub-parts (i.e., shows lower cohesion).
semantic_network_rigidity_difference_bet.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

accord_between_network_rigidity_finding_.name: Accord between network rigidity finding and Mednick's theory
accord_between_network_rigidity_finding_.description: The authors argue that their finding of greater network rigidity in low creative individuals is consistent with and extends Mednick's (1962) theory of flat versus steep associational hierarchies.
accord_between_network_rigidity_finding_.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

feasibility_of_network_science_paradigms.name: Feasibility of network science paradigms for studying high-level cognition
feasibility_of_network_science_paradigms.description: The study argues for the feasibility of using network science approaches to investigate high-level cognitive phenomena such as creativity and semantic memory.
feasibility_of_network_science_paradigms.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

replication_of_rigidity_finding_across_i.name: Replication of rigidity finding across independent samples
replication_of_rigidity_finding_across_i.description: The finding that low creative individuals have more rigid semantic networks has been demonstrated in a single study sample; independent replication is needed to confirm robustness.
replication_of_rigidity_finding_across_i.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

mechanistic_explanation_for_semantic_net.name: Mechanistic explanation for semantic network rigidity in low creative individuals
mechanistic_explanation_for_semantic_net.description: The study documents a structural difference in semantic networks but does not establish the cognitive or neural mechanisms by which low creativity produces or is produced by greater network rigidity.
mechanistic_explanation_for_semantic_net.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

generalization_of_association_cloud_meth.name: Generalization of association-cloud method to other cognitive domains
generalization_of_association_cloud_meth.description: The novel computational method based on association correlations was developed for creativity-related semantic networks; its applicability to other high-level cognitive domains remains to be demonstrated.
generalization_of_association_cloud_meth.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

causal_direction_between_network_structu.name: Causal direction between network structure and creativity
causal_direction_between_network_structu.description: The study establishes an association between semantic network structure and creativity level but does not determine whether network structure causes creative differences or vice versa, or whether both are driven by a third factor.
causal_direction_between_network_structu.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

optimal_creativity_measurement_battery_f.name: Optimal creativity measurement battery for network studies
optimal_creativity_measurement_battery_f.description: The study uses multiple creativity measures combined via a decision tree, but the optimal set of measurements for reliably classifying creative ability in network research contexts is not established.
optimal_creativity_measurement_battery_f.paper: Investigating the structure of semantic networks in low and high creative persons

network_science_as_quantitative_framewor.name: Network science as quantitative framework for cognitive representation
network_science_as_quantitative_framewor.description: The review argues that network science provides a powerful quantitative approach to represent cognitive systems, particularly semantic and lexical networks, enabling formal study of cognitive structure.
network_science_as_quantitative_framewor.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

network_structure_and_process_interactio.name: Network structure and process interaction explains behavioral phenomena
network_structure_and_process_interactio.description: The review argues that understanding cognition requires capturing how underlying network structure and processes operating on that structure interact to produce behavioral phenomena, offering deeper insight than either alone.
network_structure_and_process_interactio.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

network_dynamics_operationalized_as_stru.name: Network dynamics operationalized as structural changes across timescales
network_dynamics_operationalized_as_stru.description: The review frames cognitive dynamics as structural changes in cognitive networks across different timescales and resolutions, providing a quantitative modeling framework for cognitive change.
network_dynamics_operationalized_as_stru.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

scope_of_cognitive_network_science_appli.name: Scope of cognitive network science applications so far limited
scope_of_cognitive_network_science_appli.description: The review notes that despite cognitive theories being strongly network-based, the application of network science methodologies to quantitatively study cognition has so far been limited in scope.
scope_of_cognitive_network_science_appli.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

key_milestones_for_cognitive_network_sci.name: Key milestones for cognitive network science maturation
key_milestones_for_cognitive_network_sci.description: The review highlights key milestones that the field of cognitive network science needs to achieve as it matures in order to provide continued insights into cognitive structures and processes.
key_milestones_for_cognitive_network_sci.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

focus_on_semantic_and_lexical_networks_a.name: Focus on semantic and lexical networks as primary domain
focus_on_semantic_and_lexical_networks_a.description: The review draws primarily on literature concerning semantic and lexical networks as the focus domain for demonstrating cognitive network science approaches.
focus_on_semantic_and_lexical_networks_a.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

generalization_of_cognitive_network_scie.name: Generalization of cognitive network science beyond semantic and lexical networks
generalization_of_cognitive_network_scie.description: While the review focuses on semantic and lexical networks, broader application to other cognitive domains remains an aspiration implied by the framing of network science as a general framework for cognition.
generalization_of_cognitive_network_scie.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

network_science_uniquely_addresses_compl.name: Network science uniquely addresses complexity of cognitive systems
network_science_uniquely_addresses_compl.description: The review argues that network science can uniquely address important questions related to the complexity of cognitive systems and the processes that occur within them, beyond what other approaches offer.
network_science_uniquely_addresses_compl.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

integration_of_cognitive_network_science.name: Integration of cognitive network science findings into unified theoretical framework
integration_of_cognitive_network_science.description: As the field matures, integrating diverse findings across cognitive network science into a coherent theoretical framework is an implied open goal of the review.
integration_of_cognitive_network_science.paper: Cognitive Network Science: A Review of Research on Cognition through the Lens of Network Representations, Processes, and Dynamics

role_of_semantic_memory_in_creativity_th.name: Role of semantic memory in creativity theoretically assumed but understudied
role_of_semantic_memory_in_creativity_th.description: The source opens by noting that while semantic memory's role in creativity is theoretically assumed, it remains far from understood, motivating the review and the application of network science tools.
role_of_semantic_memory_in_creativity_th.paper: A Semantic Network Cartography of the Creative Mind.

computational_network_science_tools_appl.name: Computational network science tools applied to semantic memory and creativity
computational_network_science_tools_appl.description: Recent studies have applied computational network science tools to investigate the role of semantic memory in creativity, yielding quantitative insights via measures of connectivity, distance, and structure.
computational_network_science_tools_appl.paper: A Semantic Network Cartography of the Creative Mind.

quantitative_insights_on_semantic_memory.name: Quantitative insights on semantic memory structure and creativity via network measures
quantitative_insights_on_semantic_memory.description: The reviewed studies provide quantitative insights on how semantic memory structure relates to creativity through network measures of connectivity, distance, and structure.
quantitative_insights_on_semantic_memory.paper: A Semantic Network Cartography of the Creative Mind.

full_mechanistic_understanding_of_semant.name: Full mechanistic understanding of semantic memory's role in creativity
full_mechanistic_understanding_of_semant.description: Despite progress from network science applications, the source frames a complete mechanistic understanding of how semantic memory enables creativity as a goal not yet achieved.
full_mechanistic_understanding_of_semant.paper: A Semantic Network Cartography of the Creative Mind.

associative_thinking_as_core_mechanism_o.name: Associative thinking as core mechanism of creativity
associative_thinking_as_core_mechanism_o.description: The review argues that creativity fundamentally involves associative processes in memory—connecting concepts to form ideas, inventions, and artworks—and that this associative thinking is central rather than peripheral to creative cognition.
associative_thinking_as_core_mechanism_o.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

historical_difficulty_studying_associati.name: Historical difficulty studying associative thinking due to modeling limitations
historical_difficulty_studying_associati.description: Associative thinking has been hard to study because prior models of memory structure and retrieval processes were insufficient to capture how people navigate semantic space when forming associations.
historical_difficulty_studying_associati.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

computational_models_of_semantic_memory_.name: Computational models of semantic memory enabling associative thinking research
computational_models_of_semantic_memory_.description: Recent advances in computational models of semantic memory now allow researchers to examine how people navigate a semantic space of concepts when forming associations, overcoming prior methodological barriers.
computational_models_of_semantic_memory_.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

key_search_strategies_in_semantic_space_.name: Key search strategies in semantic space associated with creativity
key_search_strategies_in_semantic_space_.description: Research synthesized in the review reveals specific search strategies people use when navigating semantic memory that are associated with creative performance.
key_search_strategies_in_semantic_space_.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

distinction_between_free_and_goal_direct.name: Distinction between free and goal-directed association
distinction_between_free_and_goal_direct.description: The review explicitly distinguishes between free association (unconstrained) and goal-directed association (purposeful), treating this as a meaningful differentiation for understanding creativity.
distinction_between_free_and_goal_direct.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

role_of_associative_thinking_in_the_arts.name: Role of associative thinking in the arts
role_of_associative_thinking_in_the_arts.description: The review illustrates how associative thinking operates specifically in artistic creativity, providing examples or analyses connecting associative processes to artistic production.
role_of_associative_thinking_in_the_arts.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

link_between_associative_thinking_and_br.name: Link between associative thinking and brain systems for semantic and episodic memory
link_between_associative_thinking_and_br.description: The review connects associative thinking to neural systems supporting both semantic memory (general knowledge) and episodic memory (personal experience), offering an integrative neuroscience perspective on creativity.
link_between_associative_thinking_and_br.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

new_perspective_on_a_longstanding_creati.name: New perspective on a longstanding creativity theory
new_perspective_on_a_longstanding_creati.description: By linking associative thinking to both semantic and episodic memory brain systems, the review offers a new perspective on an existing (longstanding) creativity theory, though the specific theory is not named in the abstract.
new_perspective_on_a_longstanding_creati.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

identification_of_the_longstanding_creat.name: Identification of the longstanding creativity theory being reframed
identification_of_the_longstanding_creat.description: The abstract states the review offers a new perspective on 'a longstanding creativity theory' without specifying which theory; clarifying which theory and how the new perspective modifies it is important for evaluating the contribution.
identification_of_the_longstanding_creat.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

synthesis_of_cognitive_computational_and.name: Synthesis of cognitive, computational, and neuroscience research on creativity
synthesis_of_cognitive_computational_and.description: The review integrates findings across three research traditions—cognitive psychology, computational modeling, and neuroscience—to construct a unified account of creativity and associative thinking.
synthesis_of_cognitive_computational_and.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_spac.name: Mechanistic account of how semantic space navigation produces creativity
mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_spac.description: While search strategies in semantic space are identified as associated with creativity, a full mechanistic explanation of how specific navigation patterns causally produce creative outputs remains to be established.
mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_spac.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

extension_of_associative_thinking_framew.name: Extension of associative thinking framework to diverse creative domains beyond arts
extension_of_associative_thinking_framew.description: The review illustrates associative thinking in the arts, but whether the framework generalizes to scientific creativity, invention, and other domains is not established by the abstract.
extension_of_associative_thinking_framew.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

integration_of_episodic_memory_into_crea.name: Integration of episodic memory into creativity research frameworks
integration_of_episodic_memory_into_crea.description: By linking associative thinking to episodic memory brain systems alongside semantic memory, the review expands the standard semantic-memory-focused account of creativity to include episodic memory as a key component.
integration_of_episodic_memory_into_crea.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

empirical_validation_of_episodic_memory_.name: Empirical validation of episodic memory's role in associative creativity
empirical_validation_of_episodic_memory_.description: While the review links associative thinking to episodic memory brain systems theoretically and via synthesis, direct empirical validation of episodic memory's causal contribution to creative association formation remains open.
empirical_validation_of_episodic_memory_.paper: Associative thinking at the core of creativity.

openness_to_experience_predicts_dn_globa.name: Openness to Experience predicts DN global efficiency
openness_to_experience_predicts_dn_globa.description: Across two studies, Openness to Experience predicted the global efficiency of a functional network comprised of default network nodes and edges, explaining 18% of variance in Study 2 even after controlling for intelligence, age, gender, and other personality variables.
openness_to_experience_predicts_dn_globa.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

biological_basis_of_openness_to_experien.name: Biological basis of Openness to Experience in brain network architecture
biological_basis_of_openness_to_experien.description: The findings are interpreted as pointing to a biological basis of Openness to Experience, suggesting that this normally distributed personality trait affects the intrinsic architecture of large-scale brain systems.
biological_basis_of_openness_to_experien.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

dn_activity_associated_with_spontaneous_.name: DN activity associated with spontaneous and self-generated cognition
dn_activity_associated_with_spontaneous_.description: Prior fMRI research associates default network activity with mind-wandering, episodic memory retrieval, future thinking, mental simulation, theory of mind reasoning, and creative cognition, motivating study of individual differences in DN functioning.
dn_activity_associated_with_spontaneous_.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

limited_knowledge_of_normal_personality_.name: Limited knowledge of normal personality variation in DN functioning
limited_knowledge_of_normal_personality_.description: Despite large literatures on developmental and disease-related influences on the default network, surprisingly little is known about the factors that impact normal variation in DN functioning, motivating the present investigation.
limited_knowledge_of_normal_personality_.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

replication_of_openness_dn_efficiency_li.name: Replication of Openness–DN efficiency link across independent samples
replication_of_openness_dn_efficiency_li.description: The paper reports findings across two studies, but broader independent replication of the Openness–DN global efficiency relationship in larger or more diverse samples remains an open aspiration for solidifying the finding.
replication_of_openness_dn_efficiency_li.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

mechanistic_explanation_for_openness_dn_.name: Mechanistic explanation for Openness–DN efficiency relationship
mechanistic_explanation_for_openness_dn_.description: While the paper establishes that Openness predicts DN global efficiency, the causal or mechanistic pathway by which a personality trait shapes intrinsic brain network architecture is not explained.
mechanistic_explanation_for_openness_dn_.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

generalization_of_personality_brain_netw.name: Generalization of personality–brain network findings beyond the DN
generalization_of_personality_brain_netw.description: The study focuses on the default network; whether Openness or other personality traits similarly predict efficiency in other large-scale brain networks (e.g., salience, executive control) is not addressed.
generalization_of_personality_brain_netw.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

causal_direction_between_openness_and_dn.name: Causal direction between Openness and DN efficiency
causal_direction_between_openness_and_dn.description: The correlational and SEM design cannot establish whether Openness drives DN efficiency, DN efficiency enables Openness-related cognition, or both are products of a common underlying factor.
causal_direction_between_openness_and_dn.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

structural_equation_modeling_and_graph_t.name: Structural equation modeling and graph theoretical analysis applied to resting-state fMRI
structural_equation_modeling_and_graph_t.description: The study uses structural equation modeling combined with graph theoretical analysis of resting-state fMRI data to derive global efficiency metrics for the default network and relate them to personality scores.
structural_equation_modeling_and_graph_t.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

openness_to_experience_as_a_predictor_of.name: Openness to Experience as a predictor of creative and imaginative cognition
openness_to_experience_as_a_predictor_of.description: Openness to Experience is characterized as a normally distributed personality trait reflecting a tendency to engage in imaginative, creative, and abstract cognitive processes, making it a theoretically motivated predictor of DN functioning.
openness_to_experience_as_a_predictor_of.paper: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency

forward_flow_as_a_quantitative_measure_o.name: Forward flow as a quantitative measure of free thought
forward_flow_as_a_quantitative_measure_o.description: The paper introduces 'forward flow,' a metric using latent semantic analysis to capture how much present thoughts diverge from past thoughts in streams of free association, resolving the quantification problem for free thought research.
forward_flow_as_a_quantitative_measure_o.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_coll.name: Forward flow predicts creativity in college students
forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_coll.description: Study 1 demonstrates that forward flow predicts creative performance in a college student sample, even when controlling for intelligence.
forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_coll.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_a_re.name: Forward flow predicts creativity in a representative population sample
forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_a_re.description: Study 2 shows that forward flow predicts creativity in a nationally representative sample of Americans, controlling for intelligence.
forward_flow_predicts_creativity_in_a_re.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicts_membership_in_real.name: Forward flow predicts membership in real-world creative groups
forward_flow_predicts_membership_in_real.description: Studies 3–5 show that forward flow predicts membership in performance majors, professional acting, and entrepreneurship groups, even controlling for divergent thinking task performance.
forward_flow_predicts_membership_in_real.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_in_social_media_predicts_ce.name: Forward flow in social media predicts celebrity creative achievement
forward_flow_in_social_media_predicts_ce.description: Study 6 demonstrates that forward flow measured from celebrities' Twitter posts predicts their level of creative achievement in the real world.
forward_flow_in_social_media_predicts_ce.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

quantification_of_free_thought_streams.name: Quantification of free thought streams
quantification_of_free_thought_streams.description: A major open question in free thought research was how streams of thought could be quantified for scientific use; the paper resolves this via the forward flow metric.
quantification_of_free_thought_streams.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

whether_free_thought_streams_predict_psy.name: Whether free thought streams predict psychological phenomena
whether_free_thought_streams_predict_psy.description: A second open question was whether streams of thought captured by free association actually predict meaningful psychological outcomes; the paper resolves this by showing forward flow predicts creativity.
whether_free_thought_streams_predict_psy.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicting_mental_illness.name: Forward flow predicting mental illness
forward_flow_predicting_mental_illness.description: The paper proposes that forward flow may help predict mental illness, but this application has not been empirically demonstrated within the paper.
forward_flow_predicting_mental_illness.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicting_emotional_experi.name: Forward flow predicting emotional experience
forward_flow_predicting_emotional_experi.description: The authors suggest forward flow may help predict emotional experience, but no empirical studies on this link are reported.
forward_flow_predicting_emotional_experi.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicting_leadership_abili.name: Forward flow predicting leadership ability
forward_flow_predicting_leadership_abili.description: The paper proposes forward flow as a potential predictor of leadership ability, but does not provide supporting empirical data.
forward_flow_predicting_leadership_abili.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicting_adaptability.name: Forward flow predicting adaptability
forward_flow_predicting_adaptability.description: The authors propose that forward flow may predict adaptability, but no empirical studies are reported on this relationship.
forward_flow_predicting_adaptability.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicting_neural_dynamics.name: Forward flow predicting neural dynamics
forward_flow_predicting_neural_dynamics.description: The paper suggests forward flow may relate to neural dynamics, pointing toward future neuroimaging research but providing no brain data here.
forward_flow_predicting_neural_dynamics.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicting_group_productivi.name: Forward flow predicting group productivity
forward_flow_predicting_group_productivi.description: The authors propose that forward flow may help predict group productivity, but this is not empirically tested within the paper.
forward_flow_predicting_group_productivi.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

forward_flow_predicting_cultural_success.name: Forward flow predicting cultural success
forward_flow_predicting_cultural_success.description: Cultural success is proposed as a domain where forward flow may have predictive utility, but no empirical studies on this topic are reported.
forward_flow_predicting_cultural_success.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

generalization_of_forward_flow_validity_.name: Generalization of forward flow validity beyond creativity
generalization_of_forward_flow_validity_.description: While forward flow is validated as a creativity predictor, its utility for the many other proposed domains (mental illness, leadership, emotion, neural dynamics, etc.) remains untested and open.
generalization_of_forward_flow_validity_.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

utility_of_free_association_as_a_scienti.name: Utility of free association as a scientific method
utility_of_free_association_as_a_scienti.description: The paper argues that free association is a technique that captures streams of thought but its scientific utility was previously undermined by quantification and predictive validity questions, both of which the paper addresses.
utility_of_free_association_as_a_scienti.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

incremental_validity_of_forward_flow_ove.name: Incremental validity of forward flow over divergent thinking
incremental_validity_of_forward_flow_ove.description: Studies 3–5 show that forward flow predicts real-world creative group membership even when controlling for standard divergent thinking task performance, demonstrating incremental validity.
incremental_validity_of_forward_flow_ove.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

replication_of_forward_flow_creativity_l.name: Replication of forward flow–creativity link across diverse samples and methods
replication_of_forward_flow_creativity_l.description: While the paper reports six studies, the samples and methods vary considerably; independent replication of the forward flow–creativity relationship by other research groups has not occurred within this work.
replication_of_forward_flow_creativity_l.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

mechanistic_account_of_why_forward_flow_.name: Mechanistic account of why forward flow predicts creativity
mechanistic_account_of_why_forward_flow_.description: The paper establishes that forward flow predicts creativity but does not provide a detailed mechanistic explanation for why divergent semantic evolution of thought leads to creative output.
mechanistic_account_of_why_forward_flow_.paper: "Forward flow": A new measure to quantify free thought and predict creativity.

standard_creativity_definition_as_novel_.name: Standard creativity definition as novel and useful describes products not processes
standard_creativity_definition_as_novel_.description: The widely used 'novel and useful' definition characterizes creative products rather than the processes that constitute creativity, creating a misalignment that contributes to operationalization challenges.

process_definition_of_creativity_as_inte.name: Process definition of creativity as internal attention constrained by a generative goal
process_definition_of_creativity_as_inte.description: The authors propose that creativity is defined as internal attention constrained by a generative goal, comprising three criteria: internally directed attention, attentional operations constrained by a goal state, and the goal state being generative (not already in memory).

distinction_between_creativity_as_proces.name: Distinction between creativity-as-process and creative-ness as an attribute
distinction_between_creativity_as_proces.description: The paper distinguishes creativity as a process from 'creative-ness' as an attribute of persons or outputs, arguing researchers should specify which they are studying.

alignment_of_process_definition_with_cre.name: Alignment of process definition with creativity neuroscience
alignment_of_process_definition_with_cre.description: The authors illustrate how the three definitional process elements (internal attention, goal constraint, generativity) align with insights from creativity neuroscience.

ontological_distinction_of_creativity_pr.name: Ontological distinction of creativity process from mind-wandering and retrieval
ontological_distinction_of_creativity_pr.description: The process definition is used to clarify how creativity differs ontologically from related cognitive phenomena such as mind-wandering and memory retrieval.

relation_of_process_definition_to_existi.name: Relation of process definition to existing process-based models
relation_of_process_definition_to_existi.description: The paper relates the new process definition to existing process-based models of creativity, situating it within the broader theoretical landscape.

empirical_validation_of_the_process_defi.name: Empirical validation of the process definition across creativity paradigms
empirical_validation_of_the_process_defi.description: While the process definition is theoretically developed and illustrated, its empirical adequacy across diverse creativity research paradigms and tasks has not yet been systematically validated.

recommendation_to_include_process_focuse.name: Recommendation to include process-focused assessments in creativity research
recommendation_to_include_process_focuse.description: The authors call on researchers to consider including process-focused assessments alongside or instead of product or attribute measures, but whether this practice will be adopted is open.

scope_of_non_attentional_contributors_to.name: Scope of non-attentional contributors to creativity within the process definition
scope_of_non_attentional_contributors_to.description: The process definition sets minimal necessary and sufficient criteria but explicitly does not exclude perceptual, emotional, and other elements that contribute to creativity; the relationship of these elements to the core process definition remains underspecified.

semantic_distance_as_a_factor_in_creativ.name: Semantic distance as a factor in creative idea generation
semantic_distance_as_a_factor_in_creativ.description: The farther one moves from a conventional idea in semantic space, the more creative the resulting idea is likely to be. This relationship is treated as intuitive and foundational but only indirectly examined until recently.
semantic_distance_as_a_factor_in_creativ.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

historical_indirect_examination_of_seman.name: Historical indirect examination of semantic distance in creativity
historical_indirect_examination_of_seman.description: Despite the intuitive importance of semantic distance to creativity, its role has historically been studied only indirectly due to measurement challenges. The source frames this as a limitation the field is now beginning to address.
historical_indirect_examination_of_seman.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

recent_application_of_quantitative_seman.name: Recent application of quantitative semantic distance measures to creativity research
recent_application_of_quantitative_seman.description: Recent studies have begun applying quantitative measures of semantic distance in creativity research, moving beyond purely subjective assessments. The source identifies this as an emerging trend it aims to overview.
recent_application_of_quantitative_seman.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

quantitative_semantic_distance_as_comple.name: Quantitative semantic distance as complement to subjective creativity measures
quantitative_semantic_distance_as_comple.description: Quantitative measures of semantic distance complement standard subjective measures of creativity by providing objective assessments of creative output. The source frames this complementarity as a key advantage.
quantitative_semantic_distance_as_comple.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

quantitative_measures_enabling_more_dire.name: Quantitative measures enabling more direct examination of semantic memory role in creativity
quantitative_measures_enabling_more_dire.description: By applying quantitative semantic distance measures, researchers can more directly examine the role of semantic memory—and distance within it—in creativity, addressing a gap left by indirect methods.
quantitative_measures_enabling_more_dire.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

comprehensive_empirical_validation_of_qu.name: Comprehensive empirical validation of quantitative semantic distance measures across creativity paradigms
comprehensive_empirical_validation_of_qu.description: While approaches are overviewed and advantages discussed, the source does not itself demonstrate empirical validation of these quantitative measures across diverse creativity paradigms. This remains a target for the field.
comprehensive_empirical_validation_of_qu.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

full_mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic.name: Full mechanistic account of how semantic distance produces creativity
full_mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic.description: Although semantic distance is linked to creativity, the source does not establish a mechanistic explanation of why or how traversing greater semantic distance leads to more creative outcomes. This remains open.
full_mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic.paper: What can quantitative measures of semantic distance tell us about creativity?

three_psychological_dimensions_of_aesthe.name: Three psychological dimensions of aesthetic response to architectural interiors
three_psychological_dimensions_of_aesthe.description: Using PNA and PCA on ratings from 798 participants across 200 images, three components—coherence, fascination, and hominess—explained 90% of the variance in aesthetic responses to architectural interiors.
three_psychological_dimensions_of_aesthe.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

replication_of_three_component_structure.name: Replication of three-component structure in independent sample
replication_of_three_component_structure.description: In Experiment 2 (n=614), the PCA results from Experiment 1 were replicated in an independent sample, confirming the robustness of coherence, fascination, and hominess as dimensions of architectural aesthetic response.
replication_of_three_component_structure.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

hominess_as_a_novel_aesthetic_dimension_.name: Hominess as a novel aesthetic dimension specific to architectural interiors
hominess_as_a_novel_aesthetic_dimension_.description: Hominess—the extent to which a scene reflects a personal space—emerged as a new dimension not previously identified in aesthetic response frameworks for natural scenes or visual art.
hominess_as_a_novel_aesthetic_dimension_.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

fascination_covaries_with_right_lingual_.name: Fascination covaries with right lingual gyrus activity regardless of task
fascination_covaries_with_right_lingual_.description: fMRI reanalysis (n=18) showed that fascination ratings covaried with neural activity in the right lingual gyrus during both beauty judgment and approach-avoidance decision tasks.
fascination_covaries_with_right_lingual_.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

coherence_covaries_with_left_inferior_oc.name: Coherence covaries with left inferior occipital gyrus activity during beauty judgment only
coherence_covaries_with_left_inferior_oc.description: Coherence ratings covaried with neural activity in the left inferior occipital gyrus, but only when participants made beauty judgments, not during approach-avoidance decisions.
coherence_covaries_with_left_inferior_oc.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

hominess_covaries_with_left_cuneus_activ.name: Hominess covaries with left cuneus activity during approach-avoidance decisions only
hominess_covaries_with_left_cuneus_activ.description: Hominess ratings covaried with neural activity in the left cuneus, but only when participants made approach-avoidance decisions, not during beauty judgment.
hominess_covaries_with_left_cuneus_activ.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

neural_activation_not_driven_by_global_i.name: Neural activation not driven by global image properties
neural_activation_not_driven_by_global_i.description: The neural covariation patterns for coherence, fascination, and hominess were distinct from global image properties including self-similarity and complexity scores, ruling out low-level explanations.
neural_activation_not_driven_by_global_i.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

task_specificity_of_neural_correlates_fo.name: Task-specificity of neural correlates for architectural aesthetic dimensions
task_specificity_of_neural_correlates_fo.description: The study demonstrated that valuation of architectural processing in visual cortices varies both by psychological dimension and by the nature of the evaluative task (beauty vs. approach-avoidance).
task_specificity_of_neural_correlates_fo.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

generalization_of_coherence_and_fascinat.name: Generalization of coherence and fascination dimensions from natural scenes to architectural interiors
generalization_of_coherence_and_fascinat.description: Coherence and fascination are described as well-established dimensions in response to natural scenes and visual art, and the study confirms their presence in architectural interior contexts.
generalization_of_coherence_and_fascinat.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_psy.name: Causal or mechanistic account of how psychological dimensions map onto neural regions
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_psy.description: The study demonstrates covariation between aesthetic dimensions and neural activity but does not establish the causal direction or underlying neural mechanism linking these psychological dimensions to specific visual cortex regions.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_psy.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

generalization_of_three_dimension_struct.name: Generalization of three-dimension structure beyond laboratory image ratings
generalization_of_three_dimension_struct.description: The study identifies three aesthetic dimensions from image ratings in a controlled research setting; it remains open whether these dimensions generalize to real-world architectural experience or to other cultural contexts.
generalization_of_three_dimension_struct.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

individual_and_cultural_variation_in_arc.name: Individual and cultural variation in architectural aesthetic dimensions
individual_and_cultural_variation_in_arc.description: The study does not address whether the three-component structure or the neural correlates vary as a function of individual differences (e.g., expertise, personality) or cultural background.
individual_and_cultural_variation_in_arc.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

neural_basis_of_hominess_dimension_in_la.name: Neural basis of hominess dimension in larger or higher-powered fMRI samples
neural_basis_of_hominess_dimension_in_la.description: The fMRI experiment relied on n=18 participants, limiting statistical power; replication of the neural correlates of hominess and coherence in larger samples is needed.
neural_basis_of_hominess_dimension_in_la.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

psychometric_network_analysis_applied_to.name: Psychometric Network Analysis applied to aesthetic response data
psychometric_network_analysis_applied_to.description: PNA was used alongside PCA to identify the dimensional structure of aesthetic responses, offering a network-theoretic complement to traditional factor/component methods.
psychometric_network_analysis_applied_to.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

parametric_fmri_reanalysis_of_existing_a.name: Parametric fMRI reanalysis of existing architectural beauty and approach-avoidance data
parametric_fmri_reanalysis_of_existing_a.description: Experiment 3 reanalyzed an existing fMRI dataset in which participants made beauty judgments and approach-avoidance decisions to the same 200 architectural interior images, using parametric modulation by aesthetic dimension scores.
parametric_fmri_reanalysis_of_existing_a.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

extension_of_aesthetic_dimension_framewo.name: Extension of aesthetic dimension framework to predict behavioral responses to built environments
extension_of_aesthetic_dimension_framewo.description: The paper aims to link psychological dimensions of architectural aesthetics (coherence, fascination, hominess) to real-world behavioral responses such as approach-avoidance, but this is addressed only via a laboratory judgment task.
extension_of_aesthetic_dimension_framewo.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

design_feature_sensitivity_of_aesthetic_.name: Design feature sensitivity of aesthetic response dimensions
design_feature_sensitivity_of_aesthetic_.description: The first hypothesis tested is that aesthetic responses to architectural interiors reduce to key psychological dimensions that are sensitive to design features, implying the dimensions vary systematically with identifiable architectural design properties.
design_feature_sensitivity_of_aesthetic_.paper: Psychological and neural responses to architectural interiors.

small_world_network_features_of_the_hebr.name: Small-World Network features of the Hebrew mental lexicon
small_world_network_features_of_the_hebr.description: Analysis of Hebrew free association norms revealed that the Hebrew lexicon exhibits Small-World Network properties, specifically a high clustering coefficient and a scale-free degree distribution.
small_world_network_features_of_the_hebr.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

association_correlations_as_a_method_for.name: Association correlations as a method for examining global lexical structure
association_correlations_as_a_method_for.description: The study uses correlations between target words based on similarity of their association responses to investigate global features of the Hebrew lexicon, enabling words to be grouped into semantically related 'free categories'.
association_correlations_as_a_method_for.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

association_dependencies_as_a_method_for.name: Association dependencies as a method for examining local lexical structure
association_dependencies_as_a_method_for.description: Association dependencies capture the influence words exert on other words within the network, allowing investigation of how words facilitate or inhibit the spread of activation locally.
association_dependencies_as_a_method_for.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

spreading_activation_dynamics_shaped_by_.name: Spreading activation dynamics shaped by Hebrew lexical network topology
spreading_activation_dynamics_shaped_by_.description: The identified Small-World and scale-free properties of the Hebrew lexicon are argued to influence how activation spreads through the semantic network during cognitive semantic search.
spreading_activation_dynamics_shaped_by_.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

remote_associate_test_performance_explai.name: Remote Associate Test performance explained by Hebrew lexical network features
remote_associate_test_performance_explai.description: The paper discusses how the identified network properties—particularly facilitation and inhibition of spreading activation—shed light on performance in the Remote Associates Test as a semantic search task.
remote_associate_test_performance_explai.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

underrepresentation_of_non_english_langu.name: Underrepresentation of non-English languages in semantic memory research
underrepresentation_of_non_english_langu.description: The vast majority of semantic memory research has focused on English, leaving languages such as Hebrew substantially understudied in terms of lexical network structure.
underrepresentation_of_non_english_langu.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

limited_research_on_search_processes_wit.name: Limited research on search processes within semantic networks
limited_research_on_search_processes_wit.description: Despite the prevalence of semantic search in cognitive phenomena, little prior research has directly investigated search processes within semantic networks before this study.
limited_research_on_search_processes_wit.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

generalization_of_hebrew_lexical_network.name: Generalization of Hebrew lexical network findings to other non-English languages
generalization_of_hebrew_lexical_network.description: It remains open whether the Small-World, scale-free, and association dependency properties found in the Hebrew lexicon generalize to the mental lexicons of other languages.
generalization_of_hebrew_lexical_network.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

empirical_testing_of_network_based_predi.name: Empirical testing of network-based predictions for semantic search task performance
empirical_testing_of_network_based_predi.description: While the paper discusses the RAT in light of network findings, direct empirical validation of the claim that lexical network topology predicts RAT performance has not been conducted in the study.
empirical_testing_of_network_based_predi.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

hebrew_free_association_norms_dataset_as.name: Hebrew free association norms dataset as an empirical resource
hebrew_free_association_norms_dataset_as.description: The study makes use of a unique dataset of Hebrew free association norms, providing an empirical basis for network analysis of the Hebrew mental lexicon.
hebrew_free_association_norms_dataset_as.paper: Global and Local Features of Semantic Networks: Evidence from the Hebrew Mental Lexicon

nct_application_to_structural_brain_imag.name: NCT application to structural brain imaging for creativity and intelligence
nct_application_to_structural_brain_imag.description: The study applies network control theory (NCT) to diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) data from a large sample to quantitatively examine how cognitive control contributes to individual differences in creativity and intelligence.
nct_application_to_structural_brain_imag.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

intelligence_linked_to_ripl_driving_brai.name: Intelligence linked to rIPL driving brain to easy-to-reach states
intelligence_linked_to_ripl_driving_brai.description: Intelligence is associated with the ability of the right inferior parietal lobe (rIPL) to drive the brain into easy-to-reach neural states, as identified through NCT analysis.
intelligence_linked_to_ripl_driving_brai.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

intelligence_linked_to_lower_integration.name: Intelligence linked to lower integration in left retrosplenial cortex
intelligence_linked_to_lower_integration.description: Lower integration abilities in the left retrosplenial cortex (lRSC) are associated with intelligence, as revealed by NCT analysis of DTI data.
intelligence_linked_to_lower_integration.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

creativity_linked_to_rdlpfc_driving_brai.name: Creativity linked to rDLPFC driving brain to difficult-to-reach states
creativity_linked_to_rdlpfc_driving_brai.description: Creativity is associated with the ability of the right dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (inferior frontal junction) to drive the brain into difficult-to-reach neural states, based on NCT.
creativity_linked_to_rdlpfc_driving_brai.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

creativity_linked_to_higher_integration_.name: Creativity linked to higher integration in sensorimotor areas
creativity_linked_to_higher_integration_.description: Higher integration abilities in sensorimotor areas are associated with creativity, as identified through NCT analysis of structural brain data.
creativity_linked_to_higher_integration_.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

distinct_nct_profiles_for_creativity_fac.name: Distinct NCT profiles for creativity facets: fluency, flexibility, and originality
distinct_nct_profiles_for_creativity_fac.description: The three facets of creativity—fluency, flexibility, and originality—relate to broadly similar but not identical network controllability processes, suggesting both shared and unique neural control mechanisms.
distinct_nct_profiles_for_creativity_fac.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

nct_as_quantitative_framework_for_cognit.name: NCT as quantitative framework for cognitive control in high-level cognition
nct_as_quantitative_framework_for_cognit.description: Network control theory, built on a model of brain dynamics propagated along structural connectivity, is used to characterize each brain region's potential role in regulating whole-brain function based on its anatomical fingerprint and simplified node dynamics.
nct_as_quantitative_framework_for_cognit.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

replication_of_nct_creativity_and_intell.name: Replication of NCT creativity and intelligence findings across independent samples
replication_of_nct_creativity_and_intell.description: The NCT-based findings linking specific brain regions and controllability metrics to creativity and intelligence have not yet been replicated in independent samples, leaving their generalizability uncertain.
replication_of_nct_creativity_and_intell.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

causal_account_of_how_network_controllab.name: Causal account of how network controllability produces creativity and intelligence
causal_account_of_how_network_controllab.description: The study identifies associations between NCT metrics and creativity/intelligence but does not establish whether network controllability causally drives these cognitive outcomes or vice versa.
causal_account_of_how_network_controllab.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

mechanistic_account_linking_structural_c.name: Mechanistic account linking structural controllability to cognitive processes underlying creativity
mechanistic_account_linking_structural_c.description: While the study maps NCT metrics onto creativity measures, the cognitive and computational mechanisms by which structural controllability of specific regions (e.g., rDLPFC) translates into creative thought remain unspecified.
mechanistic_account_linking_structural_c.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

integration_of_nct_findings_with_general.name: Integration of NCT findings with general theories of creativity and intelligence
integration_of_nct_findings_with_general.description: The authors relate their NCT findings to existing general theories of intelligence and creativity, grounding the computational results in the broader theoretical literature.
integration_of_nct_findings_with_general.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

generalization_of_nct_controllability_fi.name: Generalization of NCT controllability findings to other cognitive constructs beyond creativity and intelligence
generalization_of_nct_controllability_fi.description: The current NCT analysis focuses on creativity and intelligence; whether the framework generalizes to other high-level cognitive constructs remains an open question.
generalization_of_nct_controllability_fi.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

role_of_cognitive_control_processes_in_m.name: Role of cognitive control processes in mediating dynamic neural network interactions for creativity
role_of_cognitive_control_processes_in_m.description: The study positions cognitive control processes as central to guiding dynamic interactions across neural network systems that underpin creativity and intelligence, consistent with recent neurocognitive research.
role_of_cognitive_control_processes_in_m.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

differential_neural_control_architecture.name: Differential neural control architecture for creativity versus intelligence
differential_neural_control_architecture.description: Creativity and intelligence are associated with distinct regional controllability profiles—creativity with difficult-to-reach states and sensorimotor integration, intelligence with easy-to-reach states and lower lRSC integration—suggesting separable neural control architectures.
differential_neural_control_architecture.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

functional_significance_of_easy_to_reach.name: Functional significance of easy-to-reach versus difficult-to-reach neural states for cognition
functional_significance_of_easy_to_reach.description: The study uses the distinction between easy-to-reach and difficult-to-reach neural states as a key NCT construct, but the functional cognitive meaning of these state categories remains to be fully characterized.
functional_significance_of_easy_to_reach.paper: Driving the brain towards creativity and intelligence: A network control theory analysis

semantic_network_underdevelopment_in_chi.name: Semantic network underdevelopment in children with cochlear implants
semantic_network_underdevelopment_in_chi.description: Children with CIs showed a less-developed semantic network structure compared to age-matched NH peers, with smaller average shortest path length (ASPL) and smaller network diameter in the CI group relative to NH peers.
semantic_network_underdevelopment_in_chi.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

animal_verbal_fluency_task_as_a_probe_fo.name: Animal verbal fluency task as a probe for semantic network structure
animal_verbal_fluency_task_as_a_probe_fo.description: A timed animal verbal fluency task was administered to 27 CI and 27 NH children (ages 7–10), and responses were analyzed using correlation and network methodologies to extract and compare semantic network structure.
animal_verbal_fluency_task_as_a_probe_fo.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

sample_matched_and_word_matched_correlat.name: Sample-matched and word-matched correlation network comparison methodology
sample_matched_and_word_matched_correlat.description: The study derived two types of correlation networks: sample-matched correlation networks (SMCN) from each group's generated animal names, and word-matched correlation networks (WMCN) from common animal names generated by both groups, enabling controlled structural comparison.
sample_matched_and_word_matched_correlat.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

larger_aspl_and_diameter_in_nh_versus_ci.name: Larger ASPL and diameter in NH versus CI semantic networks
larger_aspl_and_diameter_in_nh_versus_ci.description: The average shortest path length and network diameter were larger for the NH group than for the CI group, indicating NH children have a more expansive and differentiated semantic network structure for the animal category.
larger_aspl_and_diameter_in_nh_versus_ci.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

practical_and_clinical_implications_of_s.name: Practical and clinical implications of semantic network findings for CI children
practical_and_clinical_implications_of_s.description: The authors state they discuss the practical and clinical implications of the semantic network findings, but specific intervention recommendations or validated clinical tools are not yet established.
practical_and_clinical_implications_of_s.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

generalization_of_ci_semantic_network_fi.name: Generalization of CI semantic network findings beyond the animal category
generalization_of_ci_semantic_network_fi.description: The study focused exclusively on the animal category in a verbal fluency task; whether the semantic network underdevelopment in CI children generalizes to other semantic categories or broader lexical domains remains untested.
generalization_of_ci_semantic_network_fi.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

developmental_trajectory_of_semantic_net.name: Developmental trajectory of semantic networks in CI children over time
developmental_trajectory_of_semantic_net.description: The study provides a cross-sectional snapshot of semantic network structure in CI children ages 7–10 but does not track how these networks develop longitudinally relative to NH children.
developmental_trajectory_of_semantic_net.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

mechanisms_underlying_semantic_network_u.name: Mechanisms underlying semantic network underdevelopment in CI children
mechanisms_underlying_semantic_network_u.description: The study identifies that CI children have less-developed semantic networks but does not explain the causal or mechanistic reasons — whether due to reduced auditory input quality, delayed language exposure, impoverished word learning contexts, or other factors.
mechanisms_underlying_semantic_network_u.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

replication_of_semantic_network_underdev.name: Replication of semantic network underdevelopment finding in larger or diverse CI samples
replication_of_semantic_network_underdev.description: The study used 27 CI children; replication in larger, more diverse samples including children with varying implantation ages, device types, or language backgrounds would strengthen the generalizability of the findings.
replication_of_semantic_network_underdev.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

application_of_network_science_to_semant.name: Application of network science to semantic memory in clinical pediatric populations
application_of_network_science_to_semant.description: This study extends computational network science methods for analyzing semantic memory — previously applied mainly in typical adult populations — to a clinical pediatric population (children with CIs), demonstrating feasibility and clinical relevance.
application_of_network_science_to_semant.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

link_between_hearing_impairment_and_sema.name: Link between hearing impairment and semantic network organization
link_between_hearing_impairment_and_sema.description: The study claims that severe to profound hearing impairment, even when remediated by cochlear implants, is associated with less-developed semantic network structure relative to NH peers during middle childhood.
link_between_hearing_impairment_and_sema.paper: Semantic organization in children with cochlear implants: computational analysis of verbal fluency

verbal_creativity_linked_to_flexible_sem.name: verbal_creativity_linked_to_flexible_semantic_memory_structure
verbal_creativity_linked_to_flexible_sem.description: Verbal creativity was associated with higher connectivity, shorter distances between concepts, and lower modularity in individual semantic networks, replicating and extending prior findings on semantic network flexibility and creative thinking.
verbal_creativity_linked_to_flexible_sem.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

no_relation_between_figural_creativity_a.name: no_relation_between_figural_creativity_and_semantic_memory_structure
no_relation_between_figural_creativity_a.description: Unlike verbal creativity, figural creativity showed no significant relationship with semantic memory network structure, suggesting domain-specificity in how semantic memory supports creative thinking.
no_relation_between_figural_creativity_a.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

associative_abilities_mediate_semantic_m.name: associative_abilities_mediate_semantic_memory_structure_and_verbal_creativity
associative_abilities_mediate_semantic_m.description: Associative abilities mediated the relationship between semantic memory structure and verbal creativity, suggesting that flexible semantic memory facilitates verbal creative thinking via more efficient associative processing.
associative_abilities_mediate_semantic_m.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

individual_based_semantic_network_constr.name: individual_based_semantic_network_construction_methodology
individual_based_semantic_network_constr.description: Individual semantic networks were constructed for each participant using cognitive tests and network science methodologies to enable person-level analysis of semantic memory structure.
individual_based_semantic_network_constr.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

domain_specificity_of_semantic_memory_ro.name: domain_specificity_of_semantic_memory_role_in_creativity
domain_specificity_of_semantic_memory_ro.description: The finding that semantic memory structure predicts verbal but not figural creativity implies that the role of semantic memory is domain-specific rather than a general creativity resource.
domain_specificity_of_semantic_memory_ro.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

generalization_of_semantic_memory_creati.name: generalization_of_semantic_memory_creativity_link_to_figural_domain
generalization_of_semantic_memory_creati.description: It remains an open question whether any aspect of semantic memory structure or associative processing might predict figural creativity under different paradigms, populations, or measurement approaches.
generalization_of_semantic_memory_creati.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

replication_of_verbal_creativity_semanti.name: replication_of_verbal_creativity_semantic_network_flexibility_finding
replication_of_verbal_creativity_semanti.description: The current study's finding that verbal creativity relates to flexible semantic network structure warrants replication in independent samples using diverse methodologies to establish robustness.
replication_of_verbal_creativity_semanti.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

mechanistic_account_of_associative_media.name: mechanistic_account_of_associative_mediation_pathway
mechanistic_account_of_associative_media.description: While associative abilities were found to mediate the semantic memory–verbal creativity relationship, the precise cognitive and neural mechanism by which flexible semantic networks enable more efficient associative processing remains unspecified.
mechanistic_account_of_associative_media.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

extension_of_associative_theory_of_creat.name: extension_of_associative_theory_of_creativity_via_semantic_network_findings
extension_of_associative_theory_of_creat.description: The study supports and extends the associative theory of creativity by linking semantic memory network structure to associative abilities and verbal creativity, providing a more granular account of the associative creativity mechanism.
extension_of_associative_theory_of_creat.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

whether_associative_mediation_pathway_ge.name: whether_associative_mediation_pathway_generalizes_to_figural_creativity
whether_associative_mediation_pathway_ge.description: Given that associative abilities mediated the semantic memory–verbal creativity link, it is open whether a parallel or distinct pathway might connect these constructs to figural creativity.
whether_associative_mediation_pathway_ge.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

multi_modal_creativity_assessment_combin.name: multi_modal_creativity_assessment_combining_verbal_and_figural_measures
multi_modal_creativity_assessment_combin.description: The study assessed both verbal and figural creative thinking alongside associative abilities and semantic network metrics, enabling comparison across creativity modalities.
multi_modal_creativity_assessment_combin.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

relationship_between_associative_abiliti.name: relationship_between_associative_abilities_and_figural_creativity
relationship_between_associative_abiliti.description: It is unclear from the study whether associative abilities relate to figural creativity independently of semantic memory structure, representing an open empirical question.
relationship_between_associative_abiliti.paper: The relation between semantic memory structure, associative abilities, and verbal and figural creativity

semantic_induction_increases_idea_origin.name: Semantic induction increases idea originality in divergent thinking
semantic_induction_increases_idea_origin.description: At the behavioral level, semantic retrieval orientation prior to divergent thinking was associated with increased idea originality as measured by computational estimates of semantic distance between concepts.
semantic_induction_increases_idea_origin.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

semantic_induction_engages_left_ag_pcc_a.name: Semantic induction engages left AG, PCC, and left anterior IPL during divergent thinking
semantic_induction_engages_left_ag_pcc_a.description: RSA revealed that multivoxel patterns during semantic induction and subsequent idea generation were more similar within the left angular gyrus, posterior cingulate cortex, and left anterior inferior parietal lobe compared to episodic induction.
semantic_induction_engages_left_ag_pcc_a.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

episodic_induction_engages_left_parahipp.name: Episodic induction engages left parahippocampal gyrus and right anterior IPL during divergent thinking
episodic_induction_engages_left_parahipp.description: RSA revealed that multivoxel patterns during episodic induction and subsequent idea generation were more similar within the left parahippocampal gyrus and right anterior IPL, dissociating this from the semantic condition.
episodic_induction_engages_left_parahipp.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

dissociable_default_network_contribution.name: Dissociable default network contributions to episodic versus semantic memory in creative cognition
dissociable_default_network_contribution.description: The study demonstrates that distinct subregions of the default network support episodic versus semantic memory processes during divergent thinking, rather than the default network acting as a uniform system.
dissociable_default_network_contribution.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

rsa_as_a_method_to_dissociate_memory_con.name: RSA as a method to dissociate memory contributions to creative cognition
rsa_as_a_method_to_dissociate_memory_con.description: Representational similarity analysis of multivoxel patterns was used to identify neural similarity across induction and idea generation phases, enabling dissociation of episodic and semantic contributions to creativity.
rsa_as_a_method_to_dissociate_memory_con.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

induction_of_episodic_vs_semantic_retrie.name: Induction of episodic vs. semantic retrieval orientations prior to divergent thinking
induction_of_episodic_vs_semantic_retrie.description: Participants were induced into episodic or semantic retrieval orientations before performing a divergent thinking task during fMRI, allowing experimental manipulation of memory processes contributing to creative idea generation.
induction_of_episodic_vs_semantic_retrie.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

generalization_of_dissociable_default_ne.name: Generalization of dissociable default network memory findings across diverse creative tasks
generalization_of_dissociable_default_ne.description: It remains open whether the dissociable contributions of episodic and semantic memory within the default network replicate across other creative paradigms beyond the specific divergent thinking task used here.
generalization_of_dissociable_default_ne.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

causal_role_of_episodic_versus_semantic_.name: Causal role of episodic versus semantic memory processes in creative originality
causal_role_of_episodic_versus_semantic_.description: The study establishes neural co-occurrence of memory processes and creativity but does not establish whether episodic or semantic memory causally drives creative performance differences.
causal_role_of_episodic_versus_semantic_.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_memo.name: Mechanistic account of how semantic memory processes in left AG and PCC produce creative originality
mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_memo.description: While semantic induction was linked to left AG and PCC activity and higher originality, the specific computational or representational mechanisms by which these regions support creative idea generation remain unspecified.
mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_memo.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

individual_differences_in_reliance_on_ep.name: Individual differences in reliance on episodic versus semantic memory for creativity
individual_differences_in_reliance_on_ep.description: The study used a group-level experimental manipulation; whether individual differences in tendency to recruit episodic versus semantic memory explain variance in everyday creative ability is not addressed.
individual_differences_in_reliance_on_ep.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

replication_of_episodic_semantic_dissoci.name: Replication of episodic-semantic dissociation in default network across independent samples
replication_of_episodic_semantic_dissoci.description: The dissociable default network findings for episodic and semantic induction were obtained in a single sample; independent replication is needed to confirm robustness.
replication_of_episodic_semantic_dissoci.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis
