integration_of_episodic_memory_role_alon.name: Integration of episodic memory role alongside semantic memory in creativity frameworks
integration_of_episodic_memory_role_alon.description: The study provides neuroimaging evidence that both episodic and semantic memory processes contribute to creative cognition, informing theoretical frameworks that have primarily focused on semantic memory.
integration_of_episodic_memory_role_alon.paper: Default Network Contributions to Episodic and Semantic Processing During Divergent Creative Thinking: A Representational Similarity Analysis

semna_pipeline_as_a_comprehensive_semant.name: SemNA pipeline as a comprehensive semantic network analysis resource
semna_pipeline_as_a_comprehensive_semant.description: The paper presents a start-to-finish pipeline covering preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks, paired with an R tutorial, to reduce barriers for researchers unfamiliar with the approach.
semna_pipeline_as_a_comprehensive_semant.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

semnetdictionaries_and_semnetcleaner_for.name: SemNetDictionaries and SemNetCleaner for efficient reproducible preprocessing
semnetdictionaries_and_semnetcleaner_for.description: Two R packages, SemNetDictionaries and SemNetCleaner, are introduced to make linguistic data preprocessing efficient, reproducible, and transparent, addressing the tedious nature of semantic data preparation.
semnetdictionaries_and_semnetcleaner_for.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

semnet_package_for_network_estimation_an.name: SemNeT package for network estimation and statistical comparison via GUI
semnet_package_for_network_estimation_an.description: The SemNeT R package provides methods and measures for estimating and statistically comparing semantic networks through a point-and-click graphical user interface, lowering the technical barrier for researchers.
semnet_package_for_network_estimation_an.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

limited_scope_of_semantic_network_method.name: Limited scope of semantic network methodology application in psychology
limited_scope_of_semantic_network_method.description: The paper identifies that the application of semantic network methodologies to study cognitive processes in psychological phenomena has so far been limited in scope, partly due to a lack of accessible resources.
limited_scope_of_semantic_network_method.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

broader_adoption_of_semantic_network_ana.name: Broader adoption of semantic network analysis across psychological research
broader_adoption_of_semantic_network_ana.description: The paper aims to enable wider use of semantic network analysis by researchers across psychology through accessible tooling and tutorials, beyond the current limited scope of application.
broader_adoption_of_semantic_network_ana.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

tedious_preprocessing_as_a_barrier_to_se.name: Tedious preprocessing as a barrier to semantic network research
tedious_preprocessing_as_a_barrier_to_se.description: Even researchers already knowledgeable about semantic network methods face a barrier due to the laborious nature of preprocessing linguistic data, which the pipeline and packages aim to address.
tedious_preprocessing_as_a_barrier_to_se.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

validation_of_semna_pipeline_across_dive.name: Validation of SemNA pipeline across diverse cognitive and psychological phenomena
validation_of_semna_pipeline_across_dive.description: While a real-world demonstration is provided, broader empirical validation of the pipeline's utility across diverse psychological phenomena and research questions remains to be established.
validation_of_semna_pipeline_across_dive.paper: Semantic network analysis (SemNA): A tutorial on preprocessing, estimating, and analyzing semantic networks.

age_related_semantic_network_flexibility.name: Age-related semantic network flexibility difference via percolation analysis
age_related_semantic_network_flexibility.description: Across all 3 independent datasets (total N=78 younger, 78 older adults), younger adults had larger percolation integrals than older adults, indicating older adults' semantic networks are less flexible and break down faster under simulated attacks.
age_related_semantic_network_flexibility.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

percolation_analysis_as_a_quantitative_m.name: Percolation analysis as a quantitative measure of semantic network flexibility
percolation_analysis_as_a_quantitative_m.description: Percolation analysis examines how a semantic memory network resists breaking apart under 'attacks,' providing a quantitative index of network flexibility via the percolation integral.
percolation_analysis_as_a_quantitative_m.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

stability_of_semantic_knowledge_despite_.name: Stability of semantic knowledge despite reduced network flexibility in older adults
stability_of_semantic_knowledge_despite_.description: The study notes that despite older adults having a broader vocabulary and stable semantic knowledge across the lifespan, their semantic network flexibility is diminished, dissociating storage from retrieval/structural flexibility.
stability_of_semantic_knowledge_despite_.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

semantic_network_flexibility_as_a_contri.name: Semantic network flexibility as a contributing factor to age-related language production differences
semantic_network_flexibility_as_a_contri.description: The authors propose that reduced semantic network flexibility in older adults may be one contributing factor to observed age-related differences in language production.
semantic_network_flexibility_as_a_contri.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

verbal_fluency_as_a_probe_for_semantic_n.name: Verbal fluency as a probe for semantic network structure in aging
verbal_fluency_as_a_probe_for_semantic_n.description: Semantic networks were generated from verbal fluency performance data for younger and older adults, following existing approaches to network construction from fluency tasks.
verbal_fluency_as_a_probe_for_semantic_n.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

generalization_of_semantic_network_flexi.name: Generalization of semantic network flexibility findings to broader age ranges or clinical populations
generalization_of_semantic_network_flexi.description: The study focuses on healthy younger and older adults; whether percolation-based flexibility differences generalize to other age groups, clinical populations, or larger samples remains unaddressed.
generalization_of_semantic_network_flexi.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

mechanistic_account_linking_reduced_sema.name: Mechanistic account linking reduced semantic network flexibility to age-related retrieval decline
mechanistic_account_linking_reduced_sema.description: While older adults show reduced vocabulary retrieval despite preserved semantic knowledge, the mechanism by which reduced network flexibility causes or reflects retrieval difficulty is not established.
mechanistic_account_linking_reduced_sema.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

relationship_between_network_flexibility.name: Relationship between network flexibility and other cognitive or creativity measures in aging
relationship_between_network_flexibility.description: The study does not address whether age-related differences in semantic network flexibility relate to creativity, cognitive control, or other higher-order cognitive abilities in older adults.
relationship_between_network_flexibility.paper: Quantifying Flexibility in Thought: The Resiliency of Semantic Networks Differs Across the Lifespan

associative_fluency_task_based_on_polyse.name: Associative fluency task based on polysemous words as a probe for semantic search
associative_fluency_task_based_on_polyse.description: The authors designed a novel associative fluency task using polysemous words to elicit and distinguish two semantic search components: clustering (within-meaning search) and switching (between-meaning search). This task operationalizes semantic memory search in a way that maps onto distinct cognitive and neural correlates.
associative_fluency_task_based_on_polyse.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

clustering_correlates_with_divergent_thi.name: Clustering correlates with divergent thinking
clustering_correlates_with_divergent_thi.description: In the polysemous word associative fluency task, the clustering component (persistent search within a meaning) was found to correlate with divergent thinking performance. This links intra-meaning search behavior to the ability to generate multiple creative ideas.
clustering_correlates_with_divergent_thi.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

switching_correlates_with_remote_associa.name: Switching correlates with remote associate test performance
switching_correlates_with_remote_associa.description: The switching component (shifting between different meanings of polysemous words) correlated with performance on remote associates tasks, suggesting that between-meaning transitions capture the ability to combine distantly related concepts.
switching_correlates_with_remote_associa.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

switching_correlates_with_semantic_memor.name: Switching correlates with semantic memory structure and executive abilities
switching_correlates_with_semantic_memor.description: Switching in the polysemous word task was associated with both individual differences in semantic memory structure and executive function abilities, indicating that between-meaning search reflects an interplay of memory organization and cognitive control.
switching_correlates_with_semantic_memor.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

switching_predicted_by_connectivity_betw.name: Switching predicted by connectivity between default, control, and salience networks
switching_predicted_by_connectivity_betw.description: Neural connectivity among the default network, control network, and salience network predicted individual differences in switching behavior, suggesting that exploratory semantic search relies on large-scale network interactions.
switching_predicted_by_connectivity_betw.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

clustering_relies_on_control_salience_an.name: Clustering relies on control, salience, and attentional network interactions
clustering_relies_on_control_salience_an.description: In contrast to switching, the clustering component was associated with interactions among the control, salience, and attentional neural networks, suggesting that focused, persistent search within a meaning engages attentional control mechanisms.
clustering_relies_on_control_salience_an.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

dissociable_neurocognitive_profiles_of_c.name: Dissociable neurocognitive profiles of clustering versus switching in semantic search
dissociable_neurocognitive_profiles_of_c.description: Clustering and switching show distinct cognitive correlates (divergent thinking vs. remote associates) and distinct neural network signatures, establishing them as separable components of semantic memory search underlying creativity.
dissociable_neurocognitive_profiles_of_c.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

alternation_between_exploratory_search_a.name: Alternation between exploratory search and focused attention supports creativity
alternation_between_exploratory_search_a.description: The authors propose that creativity is supported by dynamic alternation between exploratory semantic search (switching) and focused attentional persistence (clustering), rather than either process alone.
alternation_between_exploratory_search_a.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

mechanism_by_which_switching_captures_me.name: Mechanism by which switching captures memory-structure and control process interaction
mechanism_by_which_switching_captures_me.description: While switching correlates with semantic memory structure and executive abilities, the precise mechanism by which memory organization and cognitive control jointly guide between-meaning transitions remains unclear and is not fully established.
mechanism_by_which_switching_captures_me.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

causal_direction_between_semantic_search.name: Causal direction between semantic search components and creative ability
causal_direction_between_semantic_search.description: The study reports correlations between clustering/switching and creativity measures, but the causal direction — whether search profile shapes creativity or creative ability shapes search behavior — is not established.
causal_direction_between_semantic_search.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

generalization_of_polysemous_word_fluenc.name: Generalization of polysemous word fluency task findings to other semantic search contexts
generalization_of_polysemous_word_fluenc.description: The clustering and switching findings are based on a single novel task using polysemous words; it is unknown whether these search components and their correlates generalize to other semantic fluency or associative tasks.
generalization_of_polysemous_word_fluenc.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

replication_of_clustering_divergent_thin.name: Replication of clustering-divergent thinking and switching-remote associates dissociation in independent samples
replication_of_clustering_divergent_thin.description: The dissociable creativity correlates of clustering and switching are reported in a single sample; independent replication is needed to establish the robustness of this finding.
replication_of_clustering_divergent_thin.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

neural_basis_of_clustering_as_attentiona.name: Neural basis of clustering as attentional controlled persistence
neural_basis_of_clustering_as_attentiona.description: The neural data suggest clustering reflects attentionally controlled, persistent search within a semantic domain, implicating control-salience-attentional network interactions as distinct from the default network involvement in switching.
neural_basis_of_clustering_as_attentiona.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

unclear_mechanisms_underlying_semantic_n.name: Unclear mechanisms underlying semantic network underpinnings of switching versus clustering
unclear_mechanisms_underlying_semantic_n.description: The study does not fully elucidate how the structural properties of semantic memory (e.g., network flexibility or topology) specifically enable switching between polysemous meanings, leaving the semantic memory mechanism underspecified.
unclear_mechanisms_underlying_semantic_n.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

role_of_salience_network_in_mediating_be.name: Role of salience network in mediating between default and control network interactions for creativity
role_of_salience_network_in_mediating_be.description: The salience network appears in both clustering and switching neural signatures, yet its specific functional role in coordinating between default and control networks during creative semantic search is not explained.
role_of_salience_network_in_mediating_be.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

integration_of_semantic_search_component.name: Integration of semantic search component findings into broader creativity neuroscience frameworks
integration_of_semantic_search_component.description: The authors suggest their findings contribute to understanding creativity neuroscience, but full integration of clustering/switching neural profiles with existing frameworks (e.g., default-executive coupling accounts) remains to be achieved.
integration_of_semantic_search_component.paper: An investigation of the cognitive and neural correlates of semantic memory search related to creative ability

individual_semantic_memory_network_prope.name: Individual semantic memory network properties predict real-life creativity
individual_semantic_memory_network_prope.description: Semantic memory network properties estimated from individual participants' semantic relatedness judgments significantly predicted their real-life creativity scores, providing empirical support for associative theories of creativity.
individual_semantic_memory_network_prope.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

task_based_functional_connectivity_patte.name: Task-based functional connectivity patterns predict creativity-related semantic memory network properties
task_based_functional_connectivity_patte.description: Using connectome predictive modeling, the study identified patterns of task-based fMRI functional connectivity (during a semantic relatedness judgment task) that predicted individual differences in creativity-related semantic memory network properties.
task_based_functional_connectivity_patte.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

semantic_memory_structure_mediates_the_r.name: Semantic memory structure mediates the relationship between functional connectivity and real-life creativity
semantic_memory_structure_mediates_the_r.description: Semantic memory network properties mediated the relationship between task-based functional connectivity patterns and real-life creativity, establishing a pathway from neural substrate to cognitive structure to behavior.
semantic_memory_structure_mediates_the_r.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

multi_echo_fmri_with_semantic_relatednes.name: Multi-echo fMRI with semantic relatedness judgment task as method for estimating individual semantic networks
multi_echo_fmri_with_semantic_relatednes.description: The study acquired multi-echo fMRI data while participants performed a semantic relatedness judgment task; these behavioral ratings were used to construct individual-level semantic memory networks.
multi_echo_fmri_with_semantic_relatednes.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

connectome_predictive_modeling_as_a_meth.name: Connectome predictive modeling as a method for linking FC to semantic network properties
connectome_predictive_modeling_as_a_meth.description: Connectome predictive modeling (CPM) was applied to identify brain connectivity patterns that predicted creativity-relevant semantic memory network features, demonstrating a cross-level computational approach.
connectome_predictive_modeling_as_a_meth.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

computational_network_science_coupling_b.name: Computational network science coupling behavioral, cognitive, and neural levels of analysis
computational_network_science_coupling_b.description: The study demonstrates how computational network science tools can be used to link behavioral measures, cognitive (semantic memory) structure, and neural connectivity in a unified analytic framework.
computational_network_science_coupling_b.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

causal_direction_of_the_fc_semantic_netw.name: Causal direction of the FC–semantic network–creativity pathway
causal_direction_of_the_fc_semantic_netw.description: The mediation results establish an associative pathway from functional connectivity through semantic memory structure to creativity, but the causal direction of this chain remains unclear given the correlational and cross-sectional design.
causal_direction_of_the_fc_semantic_netw.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

generalization_of_fc_semantic_network_cr.name: Generalization of FC–semantic network–creativity mediation to diverse samples
generalization_of_fc_semantic_network_cr.description: The mediation of real-life creativity by semantic memory structure and its neural substrates was demonstrated in one sample; whether it generalizes across different populations, cultures, or creativity domains remains open.
generalization_of_fc_semantic_network_cr.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

mechanistic_account_of_how_specific_fc_p.name: Mechanistic account of how specific FC patterns shape semantic memory network structure
mechanistic_account_of_how_specific_fc_p.description: While FC patterns were shown to predict semantic network properties, the mechanistic explanation for why particular connectivity patterns produce particular semantic memory architectures is not established.
mechanistic_account_of_how_specific_fc_p.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

generalization_of_mediation_findings_bey.name: Generalization of mediation findings beyond verbal creativity measures
generalization_of_mediation_findings_bey.description: The study focuses on real-life creativity broadly, but whether the semantic memory mediation pathway extends to figural or domain-specific creativity remains untested.
generalization_of_mediation_findings_bey.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

neural_substrates_underlying_individual_.name: Neural substrates underlying individual differences in semantic memory network structure
neural_substrates_underlying_individual_.description: The study identifies task-based functional connectivity patterns associated with creativity-relevant semantic memory network properties, partly characterizing the neural substrates of semantic memory structure.
neural_substrates_underlying_individual_.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_a_.name: Semantic relatedness judgment task as a probe for individual semantic memory structure
semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_a_.description: Participants' ratings on a semantic relatedness judgment task were used to estimate their individual semantic memory networks, validating this task as a window into cognitive network architecture.
semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_a_.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

replication_of_fc_semantic_network_creat.name: Replication of FC–semantic network–creativity mediation in independent samples
replication_of_fc_semantic_network_creat.description: The mediation findings linking functional connectivity to real-life creativity via semantic memory structure should be replicated in independent cohorts to establish robustness.
replication_of_fc_semantic_network_creat.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

extension_of_semantic_network_mediation_.name: Extension of semantic network mediation framework to clinical populations
extension_of_semantic_network_mediation_.description: The framework linking brain connectivity, semantic memory structure, and real-life creativity could be applied to clinical populations (e.g., those with language or memory disorders) to understand creativity-relevant cognitive differences.
extension_of_semantic_network_mediation_.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

role_of_specific_semantic_network_graph_.name: Role of specific semantic network graph-theoretic properties in mediating creativity
role_of_specific_semantic_network_graph_.description: The study shows semantic network properties mediate the FC–creativity link, but which specific graph-theoretic properties (e.g., clustering, path length, modularity) drive the mediation is a matter requiring further characterization.
role_of_specific_semantic_network_graph_.paper: Brain connectivity–based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

motor_system_role_in_creative_thought_ge.name: motor_system_role_in_creative_thought_generation
motor_system_role_in_creative_thought_ge.description: The paper argues that generating creative output—not just executing it—is deeply embedded in motor processes, with motor simulations of possible actions partially implemented in motor cortex regions themselves.
motor_system_role_in_creative_thought_ge.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

embodied_grounded_cognition_framework_ap.name: embodied_grounded_cognition_framework_applied_to_creativity
embodied_grounded_cognition_framework_ap.description: The paper applies the embodied/grounded cognition framework to creativity research, proposing that motor simulations of tools and their use partially serve creative thinking tasks such as alternative uses or musical improvisation.
embodied_grounded_cognition_framework_ap.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

neglect_of_motor_cortex_in_mechanistic_c.name: neglect_of_motor_cortex_in_mechanistic_creativity_models
neglect_of_motor_cortex_in_mechanistic_c.description: Current neurocognitive models of creative thought generation typically overlook the role of the motor cortex, representing a gap the paper aims to address.
neglect_of_motor_cortex_in_mechanistic_c.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

motor_simulation_without_overt_action_su.name: motor_simulation_without_overt_action_supports_higher_order_creativity
motor_simulation_without_overt_action_su.description: Action simulations—reactivating the motor system—do not result in overt action but instead support higher-order cognitive goals such as generating creative uses or improvising, according to the grounded cognition account.
motor_simulation_without_overt_action_su.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

causal_or_necessary_role_of_motor_system.name: causal_or_necessary_role_of_motor_system_in_creativity
causal_or_necessary_role_of_motor_system.description: The paper highlights 'some evidence' for a functionally necessary role of the motor system in creative output generation, suggesting more than mere correlation, though the evidence base is described as partial.
causal_or_necessary_role_of_motor_system.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

generalization_of_motor_simulation_accou.name: generalization_of_motor_simulation_account_beyond_tool_use_creativity
generalization_of_motor_simulation_accou.description: It remains open whether motor simulations support creative cognition beyond tool-based tasks (e.g., alternative uses for objects) to other creative domains not involving direct action or tool use.
generalization_of_motor_simulation_accou.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

integration_of_motor_system_findings_int.name: integration_of_motor_system_findings_into_unified_creativity_neuroscience_models
integration_of_motor_system_findings_int.description: The paper aspires to prompt incorporation of motor cortex contributions into comprehensive mechanistic models of creativity, which currently focus on default, control, and salience networks.
integration_of_motor_system_findings_int.paper: The role of the motor system in generating creative thoughts

curiosity_creativity_aesthetics_share_ps.name: curiosity_creativity_aesthetics_share_psychological_and_neural_mechanisms
curiosity_creativity_aesthetics_share_ps.description: The paper advances the hypothesis that curiosity, creativity, and aesthetic experience share overlapping psychological and neural mechanisms, rather than being fully independent phenomena. This shared ground is grounded in information-seeking behavior, Openness to Experience, semantic memory, and the mesolimbic dopaminergic reward system.
curiosity_creativity_aesthetics_share_ps.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

curiosity_as_driver_of_creative_and_aest.name: curiosity_as_driver_of_creative_and_aesthetic_experience
curiosity_as_driver_of_creative_and_aest.description: The paper proposes that creative and aesthetic experiences depend on curiosity as a driver of information-seeking and exploratory behavior, because they both involve knowledge gaps, problem finding, and problem solving. This positions curiosity as motivationally prior to creativity and aesthetics.
curiosity_as_driver_of_creative_and_aest.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

openness_to_experience_as_shared_persona.name: openness_to_experience_as_shared_personality_trait_across_curiosity_creativity_and_aesthetics
openness_to_experience_as_shared_persona.description: The paper argues that Openness to Experience is a critical personality trait that enhances curious behaviors as well as creative and aesthetic acts, serving as a common personality-level link across all three domains.
openness_to_experience_as_shared_persona.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

semantic_memory_role_in_curiosity_driven.name: semantic_memory_role_in_curiosity_driven_knowledge_acquisition
semantic_memory_role_in_curiosity_driven.description: The paper highlights semantic memory as playing a key role in information-seeking behavior that underlies curiosity, creativity, and aesthetics, with curiosity driving the acquisition and organization of semantic knowledge.
semantic_memory_role_in_curiosity_driven.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

mesolimbic_dopaminergic_reward_system_as.name: mesolimbic_dopaminergic_reward_system_as_neural_substrate_for_curiosity_creativity_and_aesthetics
mesolimbic_dopaminergic_reward_system_as.description: The paper examines the mesolimbic dopaminergic reward system as the shared neurobiological underpinning of curiosity, creativity, and aesthetic experience in the context of information-seeking.
mesolimbic_dopaminergic_reward_system_as.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

stages_of_art_viewing_and_making_related.name: stages_of_art_viewing_and_making_related_to_curiosity
stages_of_art_viewing_and_making_related.description: The paper discusses how the stages of art viewing and art making relate to curiosity, treating the artistic process as an instance of curiosity-driven, information-seeking behavior.
stages_of_art_viewing_and_making_related.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

typical_separate_study_of_curiosity_crea.name: typical_separate_study_of_curiosity_creativity_and_aesthetics_as_a_gap
typical_separate_study_of_curiosity_crea.description: Despite all three constructs sharing links to Openness to Experience and information-seeking, they are typically studied in isolation and the extent of their shared mechanisms is not well understood.
typical_separate_study_of_curiosity_crea.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

empirical_validation_of_integrated_curio.name: empirical_validation_of_integrated_curiosity_creativity_aesthetics_framework
empirical_validation_of_integrated_curio.description: The paper's integrated theoretical framework linking curiosity, creativity, and aesthetics via information-seeking, Openness, semantic memory, and dopaminergic reward requires direct empirical testing across all three domains simultaneously.
empirical_validation_of_integrated_curio.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

causal_role_of_curiosity_in_producing_cr.name: causal_role_of_curiosity_in_producing_creative_and_aesthetic_outcomes
causal_role_of_curiosity_in_producing_cr.description: While the paper hypothesizes that curiosity motivates creative and aesthetic activities, the causal direction and necessity of curiosity for these outcomes has not been empirically established.
causal_role_of_curiosity_in_producing_cr.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

generalization_of_mesolimbic_reward_acco.name: generalization_of_mesolimbic_reward_account_to_creativity_and_aesthetics
generalization_of_mesolimbic_reward_acco.description: Although the mesolimbic dopaminergic system is well established in curiosity and reward, its specific role in creativity and aesthetic experience as instances of information-seeking requires further empirical grounding beyond the review's proposal.
generalization_of_mesolimbic_reward_acco.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

individual_differences_in_curiosity_as_m.name: individual_differences_in_curiosity_as_moderator_of_semantic_memory_and_creativity_link
individual_differences_in_curiosity_as_m.description: The paper connects curiosity, semantic memory, and creativity at the theoretical level but leaves open whether individual differences in curiosity moderate or mediate the relationship between semantic memory structure and creative outcomes.
individual_differences_in_curiosity_as_m.paper: A Thirst for Knowledge: Grounding Curiosity, Creativity, and Aesthetics in Memory and Reward Neural Systems

associative_theory_of_creativity_as_orga.name: Associative theory of creativity as organizing framework
associative_theory_of_creativity_as_orga.description: The paper centers on the associative theory of creativity, arguing that higher creative individuals have richer semantic memory structures that facilitate broader associative search, leading to combination of remote concepts into novel and appropriate ideas.
associative_theory_of_creativity_as_orga.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

role_of_semantic_memory_in_high_level_co.name: Role of semantic memory in high-level cognition as research focus
role_of_semantic_memory_in_high_level_co.description: The author's research program investigates the role of knowledge (semantic memory) in high-level cognition, with particular emphasis on creativity, associative thinking, and memory search.
role_of_semantic_memory_in_high_level_co.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

computational_tools_enabling_quantificat.name: Computational tools enabling quantification of previously subjective creativity constructs
computational_tools_enabling_quantificat.description: The paper asserts that network science, NLP, and machine learning tools now allow quantification of constructs—such as remoteness of ideas, associative thinking, and flexible semantic memory structure—that were previously studied subjectively.
computational_tools_enabling_quantificat.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

direct_empirical_testing_of_classic_crea.name: Direct empirical testing of classic creativity theories via quantitative semantic tools
direct_empirical_testing_of_classic_crea.description: A stated aim of the research program is to use computational tools to directly study classic theories of creativity, offering quantitative pathways where only subjective methods existed before.
direct_empirical_testing_of_classic_crea.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

integration_of_cognitive_neural_and_comp.name: Integration of cognitive, neural, and computational levels in creativity research
integration_of_cognitive_neural_and_comp.description: The research program couples computational tools (network science, NLP, machine learning) with empirical cognitive and neural research to study semantic memory and creativity at multiple levels of analysis.
integration_of_cognitive_neural_and_comp.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

application_of_network_science_to_semant.name: Application of network science to semantic memory and creativity in clinical populations
application_of_network_science_to_semant.description: The paper identifies clinical populations as a scope of the research program, applying semantic memory network tools beyond typical populations to understand creativity and cognition in clinical contexts.
application_of_network_science_to_semant.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

future_research_directions_advancing_und.name: Future research directions advancing understanding of creativity complexity
future_research_directions_advancing_und.description: The author describes past, current, and future lines of research, indicating ongoing and planned work aimed at propelling forward understanding of creativity's complexity through quantitative methods.
future_research_directions_advancing_und.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

remoteness_of_ideas_as_a_quantifiable_co.name: Remoteness of ideas as a quantifiable construct in creativity research
remoteness_of_ideas_as_a_quantifiable_co.description: The paper highlights that the remoteness of ideas—a core concept in associative theories of creativity—can now be operationalized and measured quantitatively using computational semantic tools.
remoteness_of_ideas_as_a_quantifiable_co.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

richer_semantic_memory_structure_in_high.name: Richer semantic memory structure in higher creative individuals
richer_semantic_memory_structure_in_high.description: The associative theory as articulated holds that higher creative individuals possess a richer (broader, more flexible) semantic memory structure compared to lower creative individuals.
richer_semantic_memory_structure_in_high.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

broader_associative_search_as_mechanism_.name: Broader associative search as mechanism linking semantic memory to creativity
broader_associative_search_as_mechanism_.description: The paper claims that richer semantic memory facilitates broader associative search processes, which in turn enable combination of remote concepts into novel and appropriate ideas.
broader_associative_search_as_mechanism_.paper: The Role of Knowledge in Creative Thinking

mind_as_network_metaphor_utility_and_lim.name: mind_as_network_metaphor_utility_and_limits
mind_as_network_metaphor_utility_and_lim.description: The paper argues that network science is a powerful tool for representing cognitive structure but is ultimately incomplete as a total account: the mind is not a network, but may contain them.
mind_as_network_metaphor_utility_and_lim.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

map_and_vehicle_framework_as_dominant_co.name: map_and_vehicle_framework_as_dominant_cognitive_network_metaphor
map_and_vehicle_framework_as_dominant_co.description: The paper identifies 'maps and vehicles' as the prevailing metaphor in cognitive network science, where networks represent cognitive structures (maps) and processes operate over them (vehicles).
map_and_vehicle_framework_as_dominant_co.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

pitfalls_of_one_network_process_to_rule_.name: pitfalls_of_one_network_process_to_rule_them_all_assumption
pitfalls_of_one_network_process_to_rule_.description: The paper identifies a prevalent assumption in cognitive network science that a single network or process can explain all cognitive phenomena, flagging this as a conceptual pitfall.
pitfalls_of_one_network_process_to_rule_.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

new_metaphors_for_cognitive_network_scie.name: new_metaphors_for_cognitive_network_science_wormholes_skyhooks_generators
new_metaphors_for_cognitive_network_scie.description: The paper proposes three new metaphors—wormholes, skyhooks, and generators—to elaborate on and move beyond the map-and-vehicle framework in cognitive network science.
new_metaphors_for_cognitive_network_scie.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

open_question_capturing_network_change_o.name: open_question_capturing_network_change_over_time
open_question_capturing_network_change_o.description: The paper identifies the challenge of capturing how cognitive networks change as a major open question, pointing to the difficulty of modeling dynamic or temporal network structure.
open_question_capturing_network_change_o.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

open_question_appropriate_edge_definitio.name: open_question_appropriate_edge_definition_for_cognitive_networks
open_question_appropriate_edge_definitio.description: The paper poses the open question of what cognitive network edges should be made of—what the appropriate relational substrate is for linking nodes in cognitive network models.
open_question_appropriate_edge_definitio.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

open_question_aggregated_versus_individu.name: open_question_aggregated_versus_individual_based_networks
open_question_aggregated_versus_individu.description: The paper raises the open question of whether cognitive networks should be constructed at the group-aggregate level or based on individual participants, identifying tensions between these approaches.
open_question_aggregated_versus_individu.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

network_science_making_cognitive_assumpt.name: network_science_making_cognitive_assumptions_more_visible
network_science_making_cognitive_assumpt.description: A key lesson drawn in the paper is that applying network science to cognition has made implicit theoretical assumptions more explicit and has generated new research questions.
network_science_making_cognitive_assumpt.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

incompleteness_of_network_approach_as_to.name: incompleteness_of_network_approach_as_total_theory_of_mind
incompleteness_of_network_approach_as_to.description: The paper argues that while useful, the mind-as-network approach is incomplete as a comprehensive theory; it is a powerful tool but not a full account of the mind.
incompleteness_of_network_approach_as_to.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

elaboration_of_wormhole_skyhook_generato.name: elaboration_of_wormhole_skyhook_generator_metaphors_into_testable_frameworks
elaboration_of_wormhole_skyhook_generato.description: The new metaphors introduced (wormholes, skyhooks, generators) are presented as prospective elaborations; their full development into testable empirical frameworks remains an open aspiration.
elaboration_of_wormhole_skyhook_generato.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

scope_of_cognitive_structures_proposed_t.name: scope_of_cognitive_structures_proposed_to_have_network_form
scope_of_cognitive_structures_proposed_t.description: The paper asserts that language, semantic memory, episodic memory, and perceptual systems have all been proposed as having network-representable structure.
scope_of_cognitive_structures_proposed_t.paper: Is the Mind a Network? Maps, Vehicles, and Skyhooks in Cognitive Network Science

cue_set_size_as_index_of_semantic_richne.name: cue_set_size_as_index_of_semantic_richness_for_aut
cue_set_size_as_index_of_semantic_richne.description: The study operationalizes semantic richness via cue set size, reflecting the average number of elements associated with a given concept, and uses this index to manipulate AUT cue properties across four studies.
cue_set_size_as_index_of_semantic_richne.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

low_association_sparse_cues_benefit_orig.name: low_association_sparse_cues_benefit_originality_at_cost_of_fluency
low_association_sparse_cues_benefit_orig.description: Across four studies, AUT cues with low cue set size (sparse semantic knowledge) produced higher originality but lower fluency compared to high-association, rich cues, demonstrating a trade-off between idea quality and quantity as a function of semantic richness.
low_association_sparse_cues_benefit_orig.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

high_association_rich_cues_benefit_fluen.name: high_association_rich_cues_benefit_fluency_at_cost_of_originality
high_association_rich_cues_benefit_fluen.description: Rich AUT cues with many associations support greater idea quantity (fluency) but come at the cost of lower idea originality, indicating a complementary side of the fluency-originality trade-off.
high_association_rich_cues_benefit_fluen.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

fluid_intelligence_moderates_originality.name: fluid_intelligence_moderates_originality_for_low_association_cues
fluid_intelligence_moderates_originality.description: An interaction between individual differences in fluid intelligence and cue set size was found for low-association AUT cues, suggesting that cognitive control can overcome the constraints imposed by sparse semantic knowledge to facilitate idea production.
fluid_intelligence_moderates_originality.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

semantic_richness_differentially_impacts.name: semantic_richness_differentially_impacts_quality_versus_quantity_of_generated_ideas
semantic_richness_differentially_impacts.description: The authors claim that semantic memory structure has both costs and benefits for creative idea generation, with richness facilitating fluency but constraining originality, and sparseness doing the reverse.
semantic_richness_differentially_impacts.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

cognitive_control_facilitates_idea_produ.name: cognitive_control_facilitates_idea_production_under_sparse_semantic_knowledge
cognitive_control_facilitates_idea_produ.description: When conceptual knowledge is limited (sparse cues), cognitive control processes indexed by fluid intelligence can mitigate the cost to fluency, indicating a top-down intervention role.
cognitive_control_facilitates_idea_produ.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

replication_of_cue_set_size_originality_.name: replication_of_cue_set_size_originality_fluency_trade_off_across_four_studies
replication_of_cue_set_size_originality_.description: The finding that sparse cues benefit originality while rich cues benefit fluency was examined across four studies, supporting robustness of the trade-off effect.
replication_of_cue_set_size_originality_.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

generalization_of_cue_set_size_effects_b.name: generalization_of_cue_set_size_effects_beyond_the_aut
generalization_of_cue_set_size_effects_b.description: It remains open whether the costs and benefits of semantic richness indexed by cue set size extend to creative tasks beyond the Alternate Uses Task, such as other divergent thinking or real-world creative production measures.
generalization_of_cue_set_size_effects_b.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

mechanistic_account_of_why_sparse_cues_p.name: mechanistic_account_of_why_sparse_cues_produce_higher_originality
mechanistic_account_of_why_sparse_cues_p.description: While the authors propose that limited semantic associations force broader or more remote search, the precise cognitive mechanism by which sparse semantic knowledge leads to more original ideas is not fully established.
mechanistic_account_of_why_sparse_cues_p.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

causal_role_of_cue_set_size_in_creative_.name: causal_role_of_cue_set_size_in_creative_output
causal_role_of_cue_set_size_in_creative_.description: The current studies establish a correlational relationship between cue set size and creative output, but a causal demonstration via experimental manipulation of semantic richness (e.g., training) has not been reported.
causal_role_of_cue_set_size_in_creative_.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

individual_differences_in_semantic_memor.name: individual_differences_in_semantic_memory_structure_as_moderator_of_cue_effects
individual_differences_in_semantic_memor.description: Beyond fluid intelligence, it remains open whether individual differences in participants' own semantic network structure moderate how cue set size affects originality and fluency.
individual_differences_in_semantic_memor.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

practical_implications_of_cue_set_size_f.name: practical_implications_of_cue_set_size_findings_for_creativity_training_or_assessment
practical_implications_of_cue_set_size_f.description: The finding that cue semantic richness shapes creative output quality and quantity has potential applied implications for designing creativity assessments and interventions, but these applications remain to be developed.
practical_implications_of_cue_set_size_f.paper: Semantic Memory and Creativity: The Costs and Benefits of Semantic Memory Structure in Generating Original Ideas

montessori_education_associated_with_mor.name: Montessori_education_associated_with_more_flexible_semantic_network_structure
montessori_education_associated_with_mor.description: Children in Montessori education showed higher connectivity, shorter paths between concepts, and less modularity in their semantic networks compared to traditionally educated peers matched on socioeconomic factors and nonverbal intelligence.
montessori_education_associated_with_mor.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

montessori_education_associated_with_hig.name: Montessori_education_associated_with_higher_creative_thinking_scores
montessori_education_associated_with_hig.description: Children with Montessori educational backgrounds scored higher on creative thinking tests relative to traditionally educated children in the matched sample.
montessori_education_associated_with_hig.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

educational_experience_shapes_semantic_m.name: Educational_experience_shapes_semantic_memory_structure
educational_experience_shapes_semantic_m.description: The study concludes that differences in educational background impact not only which concepts children learn but how those concepts are represented in semantic memory, suggesting education influences knowledge organization.
educational_experience_shapes_semantic_m.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

semantic_network_flexibility_as_mechanis.name: Semantic_network_flexibility_as_mechanism_linking_education_to_creative_thinking
semantic_network_flexibility_as_mechanis.description: The co-occurrence of more flexible semantic networks and higher creative thinking in Montessori children suggests that educational experience affects creative thinking through its impact on semantic memory structure.
semantic_network_flexibility_as_mechanis.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

causal_role_of_educational_type_in_produ.name: Causal_role_of_educational_type_in_producing_semantic_network_differences
causal_role_of_educational_type_in_produ.description: The study is cross-sectional and observational; it remains open whether Montessori education causally produces more flexible semantic networks or whether pre-existing differences in families or children selecting Montessori schooling account for the finding.
causal_role_of_educational_type_in_produ.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

generalization_of_education_semantic_net.name: Generalization_of_education_semantic_network_creativity_finding_beyond_Swiss_Montessori_sample
generalization_of_education_semantic_net.description: The sample consists of 67 Swiss schoolchildren from one Montessori and one traditional school; it is unknown whether the findings replicate across different countries, educational systems, larger samples, or other Montessori implementations.
generalization_of_education_semantic_net.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

developmental_trajectory_of_education_dr.name: Developmental_trajectory_of_education_driven_semantic_network_differences
developmental_trajectory_of_education_dr.description: The study provides a cross-sectional snapshot; it is unknown how education-related differences in semantic network structure emerge over developmental time and whether they persist, grow, or diminish across school years.
developmental_trajectory_of_education_dr.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

computational_network_science_tools_appl.name: Computational_network_science_tools_applied_to_educational_psychology
computational_network_science_tools_appl.description: The study applies computational network science methods to characterize hidden semantic knowledge structures in schoolchildren, extending this methodology to questions about the impact of educational background on cognition.
computational_network_science_tools_appl.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

matched_comparison_of_montessori_and_tra.name: Matched_comparison_of_Montessori_and_traditional_schoolchildren_on_semantic_and_creative_measures
matched_comparison_of_montessori_and_tra.description: 67 Swiss schoolchildren from Montessori and traditional schools were matched on socioeconomic factors and nonverbal intelligence, and assessed on semantic network structure and creative thinking to isolate the role of educational experience.
matched_comparison_of_montessori_and_tra.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

mechanisms_underlying_montessori_educati.name: Mechanisms_underlying_Montessori_education_effects_on_semantic_network_flexibility
mechanisms_underlying_montessori_educati.description: It is unclear which specific features of Montessori pedagogy (e.g., self-directed learning, mixed-age classrooms, hands-on materials) drive differences in semantic network structure relative to traditional schooling.
mechanisms_underlying_montessori_educati.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

extension_of_semantic_network_flexibilit.name: Extension_of_semantic_network_flexibility_creativity_link_to_child_populations
extension_of_semantic_network_flexibilit.description: The study demonstrates that the association between flexible semantic network structure and higher creative thinking, previously documented in adult research, is also present in schoolchildren differing by educational background.
extension_of_semantic_network_flexibilit.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

practical_educational_implications_of_se.name: Practical_educational_implications_of_semantic_network_and_creativity_findings
practical_educational_implications_of_se.description: The findings raise the question of whether educational interventions designed to promote flexible semantic knowledge organization could be developed and evaluated to enhance children's creative thinking capabilities.
practical_educational_implications_of_se.paper: Education shapes the structure of semantic memory and impacts creative thinking

simulated_psychedelic_vr_exposure_enhanc.name: Simulated psychedelic VR exposure enhances cognitive flexibility
simulated_psychedelic_vr_exposure_enhanc.description: Participants exposed to DeepDream-generated hallucinatory-like VR videos showed enhanced cognitive flexibility compared to standard panoramic video exposure, as measured by behavioral tasks.
simulated_psychedelic_vr_exposure_enhanc.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

flexible_semantic_network_structure_foll.name: Flexible semantic network structure following simulated psychedelic exposure
flexible_semantic_network_structure_foll.description: Semantic network analysis revealed a more flexible network structure in participants after exposure to the hallucinatory-like altered VR videos compared to standard videos.
flexible_semantic_network_structure_foll.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

attenuated_automatic_processing_and_chao.name: Attenuated automatic processing and chaotic dynamics after simulated psychedelic exposure
attenuated_automatic_processing_and_chao.description: Following exposure to hallucinatory-like VR videos, individuals showed reduced contribution of automatic cognitive processes and attenuated chaotic dynamics underlying decision-making.
attenuated_automatic_processing_and_chao.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

deepdream_algorithm_as_a_tool_for_genera.name: DeepDream algorithm as a tool for generating hallucinatory-like VR stimuli
deepdream_algorithm_as_a_tool_for_genera.description: The DeepDream algorithm was applied to panoramic VR videos to create hallucinatory-like counterparts that simulate altered perceptual phenomenology without actual psychedelic drug administration.
deepdream_algorithm_as_a_tool_for_genera.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

phenomenological_similarity_between_simu.name: Phenomenological similarity between simulated and actual psychedelic perception
phenomenological_similarity_between_simu.description: The study premises that artificially-induced (VR-based) and actual psychedelic altered perceptions share phenomenological similarities, providing the rationale for using simulated stimuli.
phenomenological_similarity_between_simu.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

cognitive_flexibility_modulation_via_vr_.name: Cognitive flexibility modulation via VR-simulated psychedelics lacks prior experimental evidence
cognitive_flexibility_modulation_via_vr_.description: Despite phenomenological similarity between simulated and actual psychedelic states, experimental evidence on whether simulated altered perception can modulate cognitive flexibility was previously lacking.
cognitive_flexibility_modulation_via_vr_.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

reorganization_of_cognitive_dynamics_as_.name: Reorganization of cognitive dynamics as mechanism linking simulated psychedelics to flexibility
reorganization_of_cognitive_dynamics_as_.description: The authors propose that simulated altered perceptual phenomenology enhances cognitive flexibility through a reorganization of cognitive dynamics that facilitates exploration of uncommon decision strategies and inhibits automated choices.
reorganization_of_cognitive_dynamics_as_.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

replication_of_vr_psychedelic_flexibilit.name: Replication of VR psychedelic flexibility enhancement in independent samples
replication_of_vr_psychedelic_flexibilit.description: The finding that simulated psychedelic VR exposure enhances cognitive flexibility needs replication in larger and more diverse samples to establish generalizability.
replication_of_vr_psychedelic_flexibilit.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

generalization_of_simulated_psychedelic_.name: Generalization of simulated psychedelic flexibility findings beyond semantic network measures
generalization_of_simulated_psychedelic_.description: It is unclear whether the cognitive flexibility enhancement observed via semantic network structure and decision-process dynamics extends to other cognitive flexibility measures or domains.
generalization_of_simulated_psychedelic_.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

causal_role_of_simulated_altered_percept.name: Causal role of simulated altered perception in cognitive flexibility enhancement
causal_role_of_simulated_altered_percept.description: The study demonstrates an association between simulated psychedelic VR exposure and cognitive flexibility, but causal direction and mechanisms remain to be established.
causal_role_of_simulated_altered_percept.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

practical_and_clinical_implications_of_v.name: Practical and clinical implications of VR-simulated psychedelic flexibility enhancement
practical_and_clinical_implications_of_v.description: Understanding whether non-pharmacological, VR-based simulations of psychedelic states can reliably enhance cognitive flexibility has potential practical and clinical relevance for intervention development.
practical_and_clinical_implications_of_v.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

comparison_of_vr_simulated_psychedelics_.name: Comparison of VR simulated psychedelics with actual psychedelic drug effects on cognitive flexibility
comparison_of_vr_simulated_psychedelics_.description: It remains to be directly tested whether the magnitude and quality of cognitive flexibility changes induced by VR simulation are comparable to those produced by actual psychedelic substances.
comparison_of_vr_simulated_psychedelics_.paper: Simulated visual hallucinations in virtual reality enhance cognitive flexibility

age_related_differences_in_individual_ba.name: Age-related differences in individual-based semantic network structure
age_related_differences_in_individual_ba.description: Older adults showed significantly lower clustering coefficient and global efficiency, and higher modularity, in their individual-based semantic memory networks compared to younger adults. This replicates and extends prior group-average findings to the individual level.
age_related_differences_in_individual_ba.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

vocabulary_knowledge_relates_to_semantic.name: Vocabulary knowledge relates to semantic network structure
vocabulary_knowledge_relates_to_semantic.description: Vocabulary knowledge was significantly associated with semantic network measures: higher vocabulary correlated with lower clustering coefficient and global efficiency, and higher modularity, mirroring the pattern observed for age effects.
vocabulary_knowledge_relates_to_semantic.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

vocabulary_knowledge_increases_with_age.name: Vocabulary knowledge increases with age
vocabulary_knowledge_increases_with_age.description: Vocabulary knowledge was found to increase significantly with age across participants, suggesting that accumulated knowledge shapes the structure of semantic memory over the lifespan.
vocabulary_knowledge_increases_with_age.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

knowledge_accumulation_as_a_mechanism_sh.name: Knowledge accumulation as a mechanism shaping semantic network structure with age
knowledge_accumulation_as_a_mechanism_sh.description: The authors propose that the accumulation of vocabulary knowledge with age plays a critical role in producing age-related changes in semantic memory network structure. This is offered as an interpretive account rather than a directly tested causal mechanism.
knowledge_accumulation_as_a_mechanism_sh.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

individual_differences_moderate_age_rela.name: Individual differences moderate age-related disparities in semantic network structure
individual_differences_moderate_age_rela.description: The study demonstrates that individual-level differences in semantic network properties exist and are related to vocabulary knowledge, highlighting the importance of moving beyond group averages in aging research.
individual_differences_moderate_age_rela.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

linking_individual_based_semantic_networ.name: Linking individual-based semantic network measures to language production (verbal fluency)
linking_individual_based_semantic_networ.description: Individual-based semantic network measures (CC, global efficiency, modularity) were related to verbal fluency performance, extending the network-language production link to individual-difference analyses in aging.
linking_individual_based_semantic_networ.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

generalization_of_age_vocabulary_network.name: Generalization of age-vocabulary-network findings beyond the current sample
generalization_of_age_vocabulary_network.description: The current findings are based on one sample; it remains unclear whether the relationship between vocabulary accumulation, semantic network structure, and age generalizes across diverse populations, languages, or cultural contexts.
generalization_of_age_vocabulary_network.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

causal_or_longitudinal_account_of_how_li.name: Causal or longitudinal account of how life experience shapes aging semantic networks
causal_or_longitudinal_account_of_how_li.description: While the study shows that diverse life experiences and knowledge accumulation correlate with semantic network structure, no longitudinal or experimental evidence is provided to establish directionality or causality.
causal_or_longitudinal_account_of_how_li.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

role_of_network_structure_in_mediating_t.name: Role of network structure in mediating the relationship between vocabulary and verbal fluency
role_of_network_structure_in_mediating_t.description: It remains an open question whether semantic network structure formally mediates the relationship between vocabulary knowledge and language production ability, or whether these are parallel correlates of aging.
role_of_network_structure_in_mediating_t.paper: Age differences in semantic network structure: Acquiring knowledge shapes semantic memory.

aging_reduces_perceived_facial_attractiv.name: aging reduces perceived facial attractiveness
aging_reduces_perceived_facial_attractiv.description: Empirical testing confirmed that with increasing age, faces are perceived as less attractive, supporting the hypothesis that older faces are rated lower in attractiveness than younger faces.
aging_reduces_perceived_facial_attractiv.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

perceiver_age_moderates_aging_effect_on_.name: perceiver age moderates aging effect on facial attractiveness
perceiver_age_moderates_aging_effect_on_.description: The reduction in attractiveness with face age was less pronounced when judgments were made by older perceivers compared to younger and middle-aged perceivers.
perceiver_age_moderates_aging_effect_on_.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

perceiver_sex_moderates_aging_effect_on_.name: perceiver sex moderates aging effect on facial attractiveness
perceiver_sex_moderates_aging_effect_on_.description: The aging effect on attractiveness was more pronounced for male perceivers, especially for female faces, than for female perceivers.
perceiver_sex_moderates_aging_effect_on_.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

attractiveness_judgment_type_modulates_a.name: attractiveness judgment type modulates aging effect
attractiveness_judgment_type_modulates_a.description: Attractive older faces were perceived as elegant more than beautiful or gorgeous, indicating that the specific dimension of attractiveness being judged matters for how aging effects manifest.
attractiveness_judgment_type_modulates_a.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

network_analyses_reveal_older_faces_are_.name: network analyses reveal older faces are more similar in attractiveness and segregated from younger faces
network_analyses_reveal_older_faces_are_.description: Computational network science analyses showed that older faces cluster together in attractiveness space and are segregated from younger faces, suggesting categorical processing of older faces.
network_analyses_reveal_older_faces_are_.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

categorical_processing_of_older_faces_in.name: categorical processing of older faces in attractiveness judgments
categorical_processing_of_older_faces_in.description: The network segregation and similarity findings indicate that perceivers tend to process older faces as a category when making attractiveness judgments, rather than evaluating them individually.
categorical_processing_of_older_faces_in.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

attractiveness_as_a_multidimensional_con.name: attractiveness as a multidimensional construct varying by age, sex, and judgment type
attractiveness_as_a_multidimensional_con.description: The study concludes that attractiveness is not monolithic but varies by the age and sex of both perceiver and target, and by the specific attractiveness dimension being judged.
attractiveness_as_a_multidimensional_con.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

generalization_of_aging_attractiveness_f.name: generalization of aging-attractiveness findings beyond the current sample
generalization_of_aging_attractiveness_f.description: The findings on how perceiver age and sex moderate aging effects on attractiveness ratings were demonstrated in one sample; it is unclear whether these results generalize to diverse cultural, demographic, or geographic populations.
generalization_of_aging_attractiveness_f.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

causal_account_of_why_aging_reduces_faci.name: causal account of why aging reduces facial attractiveness
causal_account_of_why_aging_reduces_faci.description: The study demonstrates that aging reduces perceived attractiveness but does not establish the causal mechanisms (e.g., evolutionary, cultural, perceptual) underlying this effect.
causal_account_of_why_aging_reduces_faci.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

generalization_of_elegant_over_beautiful.name: generalization of elegant-over-beautiful dimension to other cultural or linguistic contexts
generalization_of_elegant_over_beautiful.description: The finding that older attractive faces are rated as elegant rather than beautiful or gorgeous may be specific to the language and cultural context of the study; generalization remains untested.
generalization_of_elegant_over_beautiful.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

application_of_network_science_to_facial.name: application of network science to facial attractiveness perception
application_of_network_science_to_facial.description: The study applies computational network science methods alongside empirical attractiveness ratings to analyze the structure of facial attractiveness judgments across age, providing a novel methodological framework for this domain.
application_of_network_science_to_facial.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

neural_or_cognitive_mechanisms_underlyin.name: neural or cognitive mechanisms underlying categorical processing of older faces
neural_or_cognitive_mechanisms_underlyin.description: The study identifies categorical processing of older faces via network segregation but does not explain the neural or cognitive mechanisms driving this categorization.
neural_or_cognitive_mechanisms_underlyin.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

whether_individual_differences_in_percei.name: whether individual differences in perceiver characteristics beyond age and sex moderate attractiveness judgments
whether_individual_differences_in_percei.description: The study examined perceiver age and sex as moderators, but other individual difference variables (e.g., personality, cultural background, openness to experience) were not assessed as moderators.
whether_individual_differences_in_percei.paper: The effect of aging on facial attractiveness: An empirical and computational investigation

flexible_semantic_network_supports_creat.name: flexible_semantic_network_supports_creative_metaphor_production
flexible_semantic_network_supports_creat.description: High-metaphor-producing individuals had more flexible, clustered, and less rigid semantic networks than low-metaphor-producing individuals, as estimated from verbal fluency responses. Results replicated across both semantic categories.
flexible_semantic_network_supports_creat.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

creative_metaphor_production_task_as_pro.name: creative_metaphor_production_task_as_probe_for_figurative_language_creativity
creative_metaphor_production_task_as_pro.description: Participants completed a creative metaphor production task scored for creativity, and were divided into equal high and low groups to compare semantic network structure. Verbal fluency tasks provided the network estimation data.
creative_metaphor_production_task_as_pro.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

extension_of_semantic_network_flexibilit.name: extension_of_semantic_network_flexibility_creativity_link_to_metaphor_production
extension_of_semantic_network_flexibilit.description: The study provides first evidence that flexible, clustered, and less rigid semantic memory structure relates to figurative language production, extending prior work on semantic networks and creativity to the metaphor production domain.
extension_of_semantic_network_flexibilit.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

prior_evidence_that_higher_creative_indi.name: prior_evidence_that_higher_creative_individuals_better_comprehend_novel_metaphors
prior_evidence_that_higher_creative_indi.description: Previous studies established that higher creative individuals show better comprehension of novel metaphors, potentially due to more flexible semantic memory network structure supporting remote conceptual combination.
prior_evidence_that_higher_creative_indi.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_p.name: cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_production_poorly_understood
cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_p.description: Despite metaphor being a common form of creative language, the cognitive mechanisms underlying figurative language production remain poorly understood, motivating the present investigation.
cognitive_basis_of_figurative_language_p.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

replication_of_semantic_network_metaphor.name: replication_of_semantic_network_metaphor_production_finding_across_independent_samples
replication_of_semantic_network_metaphor.description: The current study replicated its findings across two semantic categories, but replication in fully independent samples with different participant populations remains to be demonstrated.
replication_of_semantic_network_metaphor.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_netw.name: mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_network_flexibility_enables_metaphor_production
mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_netw.description: The study demonstrates an association between flexible semantic network structure and creative metaphor production but does not establish the cognitive or neural mechanism by which network flexibility enables figurative language production.
mechanistic_account_of_how_semantic_netw.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

generalization_of_semantic_network_metap.name: generalization_of_semantic_network_metaphor_production_finding_beyond_verbal_fluency_estimation
