generalization_of_semantic_network_metap.description: Semantic networks were estimated from verbal fluency task responses; it remains open whether the finding holds when networks are estimated through other methods such as free association or semantic relatedness judgments.
generalization_of_semantic_network_metap.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

generalization_of_metaphor_production_se.name: generalization_of_metaphor_production_semantic_network_link_to_other_figurative_language_forms
generalization_of_metaphor_production_se.description: The current findings concern creative metaphor production specifically; it is unknown whether flexible semantic network structure similarly underlies other forms of figurative language such as similes, idioms, or analogies.
generalization_of_metaphor_production_se.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

role_of_semantic_network_clustering_in_m.name: role_of_semantic_network_clustering_in_metaphor_production
role_of_semantic_network_clustering_in_m.description: High-metaphor-producing individuals showed higher clustering in their semantic networks, suggesting that local connectivity structure—not only global flexibility—contributes to creative metaphor production.
role_of_semantic_network_clustering_in_m.paper: Flexible Semantic Network Structure Supports the Production of Creative Metaphor

knowledge_rich_contexts_underrepresented.name: Knowledge-rich contexts underrepresented in CPS research
knowledge_rich_contexts_underrepresented.description: Classic creativity paradigms typically minimize knowledge content, leaving real-world CPS in knowledge-rich contexts understudied. The authors argue this represents a significant gap in the field.
knowledge_rich_contexts_underrepresented.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

reorganization_of_existing_knowledge_as_.name: Reorganization of existing knowledge as core mechanism of real-world CPS
reorganization_of_existing_knowledge_as_.description: The authors assert that real-world CPS fundamentally depends on reorganizing existing knowledge to serve new, problem-relevant functions rather than generating ideas from scratch.
reorganization_of_existing_knowledge_as_.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

creative_analogical_transfer_as_a_promis.name: Creative analogical transfer as a promising paradigm for knowledge-rich CPS
creative_analogical_transfer_as_a_promis.description: Paradigms focusing on creative analogical transfer of knowledge are proposed as reflective of CPS skills applicable to real-world problem solving, offering process-level insights into cognitive and neural characteristics.
creative_analogical_transfer_as_a_promis.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

expansion_of_cps_research_to_include_nov.name: Expansion of CPS research to include novices and experts in specific domains
expansion_of_cps_research_to_include_nov.description: The authors call for CPS research to consider knowledge-rich problem contexts across both novices and domain experts, acknowledging that expertise shapes how knowledge is reorganized for creative ends.
expansion_of_cps_research_to_include_nov.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

role_of_crystallized_knowledge_in_creati.name: Role of crystallized knowledge in creativity
role_of_crystallized_knowledge_in_creati.description: The paper points to multiple avenues for expanding inquiry into how crystallized (accumulated) knowledge contributes to creative problem solving, treating this as an open research frontier.
role_of_crystallized_knowledge_in_creati.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

process_level_cognitive_and_neural_chara.name: Process-level cognitive and neural characterization of knowledge-rich CPS
process_level_cognitive_and_neural_chara.description: Existing analogical transfer paradigms have begun to yield process-level insights into the cognitive and neural characteristics of CPS in knowledge-rich contexts, though the work is nascent.
process_level_cognitive_and_neural_chara.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

generalization_of_knowledge_rich_cps_fin.name: Generalization of knowledge-rich CPS findings across domains and populations
generalization_of_knowledge_rich_cps_fin.description: It remains unclear whether insights from creative analogical transfer paradigms generalize across different knowledge domains, expertise levels, and populations beyond those studied so far.
generalization_of_knowledge_rich_cps_fin.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

argument_for_expanding_cps_research_agen.name: Argument for expanding CPS research agenda to knowledge-rich paradigms
argument_for_expanding_cps_research_agen.description: The paper makes a theoretical and programmatic argument that creativity researchers should prioritize knowledge-rich paradigms, offering a reorientation of the CPS research agenda.
argument_for_expanding_cps_research_agen.paper: Creative problem solving in knowledge-rich contexts.

multiplex_lexical_network_combining_sema.name: multiplex_lexical_network_combining_semantics_and_phonology
multiplex_lexical_network_combining_sema.description: The study constructs a multiplex network representation of the mental lexicon combining phonological and semantic associations (150,000 total), going beyond purely semantic networks to capture multiple types of conceptual similarity simultaneously.
multiplex_lexical_network_combining_sema.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

viable_cluster_as_a_structural_core_of_t.name: viable_cluster_as_a_structural_core_of_the_mental_lexicon
viable_cluster_as_a_structural_core_of_t.description: The multiplex network analysis identifies a viable cluster — a kernel of simpler, more general, concrete words acquired early in language learning — as a structural core within the mental lexicon.
viable_cluster_as_a_structural_core_of_t.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

high_creative_individuals_access_viable_.name: high_creative_individuals_access_viable_cluster_less_frequently
high_creative_individuals_access_viable_.description: Higher creative individuals access the viable cluster less frequently during the semantic fluency task, reach more peripheral words, and cover longer multiplex network distances compared to lower creative individuals.
high_creative_individuals_access_viable_.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

lower_entropy_of_viable_cluster_access_i.name: lower_entropy_of_viable_cluster_access_in_high_creative_individuals
lower_entropy_of_viable_cluster_access_i.description: Higher creative individuals show lower uncertainty/entropy in their viable cluster access patterns, suggesting more directed navigation away from the core lexical cluster.
lower_entropy_of_viable_cluster_access_i.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

machine_learning_classifier_of_creativit.name: machine_learning_classifier_of_creativity_from_multiplex_network_navigation
machine_learning_classifier_of_creativit.description: The study constructs a machine learning classifier distinguishing low and high creative individuals based on their multiplex lexical network navigation patterns, achieving 65.0±0.9% accuracy and AUC of 68.0±0.8%, both above chance (50%).
machine_learning_classifier_of_creativit.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

semantic_fluency_task_modeled_as_navigat.name: semantic_fluency_task_modeled_as_navigation_on_multiplex_lexical_network
semantic_fluency_task_modeled_as_navigat.description: Animal name generation during semantic fluency is modeled as mental navigation on the multiplex lexical network, tracking movement within, outside, and between the viable cluster.
semantic_fluency_task_modeled_as_navigat.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

generalization_of_multiplex_network_crea.name: generalization_of_multiplex_network_creativity_findings_beyond_animal_fluency
generalization_of_multiplex_network_crea.description: The current results are based on a single semantic fluency task (animal names) with a binary creativity classification; it is unclear whether findings generalize to other creativity tasks, domains, or continuous creativity measures.
generalization_of_multiplex_network_crea.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

replication_of_multiplex_network_creativ.name: replication_of_multiplex_network_creativity_classifier_in_independent_samples
replication_of_multiplex_network_creativ.description: The machine learning classifier was trained and evaluated on a sample of 94 individuals (47 low, 47 high creative); independent replication is needed to confirm robustness and generalizability.
replication_of_multiplex_network_creativ.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_hig.name: causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_high_creatives_avoid_viable_cluster
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_hig.description: The study establishes that higher creatives access the viable cluster less and reach more peripheral words, but the cognitive or neural mechanism driving this navigational difference remains unexplained.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_hig.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

integration_of_phonological_layer_contri.name: integration_of_phonological_layer_contribution_to_creativity_prediction
integration_of_phonological_layer_contri.description: The multiplex network combines phonological and semantic layers, but it is not established what unique contribution the phonological layer makes over a purely semantic network in predicting creativity differences.
integration_of_phonological_layer_contri.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

practical_utility_of_multiplex_network_c.name: practical_utility_of_multiplex_network_creativity_classifier_at_scale
practical_utility_of_multiplex_network_c.description: The study highlights the potential of combining psycholinguistic measures with multiplex network models for automatically classifying creativity levels, but practical deployment and utility at scale remain aspirational.
practical_utility_of_multiplex_network_c.paper: Viability in Multiplex Lexical Networks and Machine Learning Characterizes Human Creativity

higher_creative_individuals_faster_at_co.name: higher_creative_individuals_faster_at_comprehending_both_conventional_and_novel_metaphors
higher_creative_individuals_faster_at_co.description: Higher creative individuals demonstrated significantly faster reaction times than lower creative individuals when comprehending both conventional and novel metaphors in a semantic relatedness judgment task.
higher_creative_individuals_faster_at_co.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

higher_creative_individuals_more_accurat.name: higher_creative_individuals_more_accurate_only_for_novel_metaphors
higher_creative_individuals_more_accurat.description: Higher creative individuals were significantly more accurate than lower creative individuals specifically for novel metaphors, but not for conventional metaphors, literal pairs, or meaningless pairs.
higher_creative_individuals_more_accurat.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

flexible_semantic_memory_as_mechanism_fo.name: flexible_semantic_memory_as_mechanism_for_metaphor_comprehension_advantage_in_high_creatives
flexible_semantic_memory_as_mechanism_fo.description: The comprehension advantage of higher creative individuals is attributed to their relatively more flexible semantic memory structure, which enables broader activation of semantic associations needed to integrate weakly related metaphor components.
flexible_semantic_memory_as_mechanism_fo.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

no_group_difference_for_literal_and_mean.name: no_group_difference_for_literal_and_meaningless_word_pairs
no_group_difference_for_literal_and_mean.description: As predicted, lower and higher creative individuals performed similarly on literal and meaningless word pairs, confirming that the group differences are specific to metaphoric processing.
no_group_difference_for_literal_and_mean.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_pr.name: semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_probe_for_metaphor_comprehension_across_creativity_levels
semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_pr.description: A semantic relatedness judgment task using four types of word pairs (novel metaphors, conventional metaphors, literal, and meaningless) was employed to measure metaphor comprehension differences between low and high creative individuals via RTs and error rates.
semantic_relatedness_judgment_task_as_pr.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

replication_of_metaphor_comprehension_cr.name: replication_of_metaphor_comprehension_creativity_link_in_independent_samples
replication_of_metaphor_comprehension_cr.description: The current findings on metaphor comprehension differences between low and high creatives require replication in independent samples to establish robustness.
replication_of_metaphor_comprehension_cr.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

generalization_of_metaphor_comprehension.name: generalization_of_metaphor_comprehension_creativity_link_beyond_verbal_creativity_classification
generalization_of_metaphor_comprehension.description: The study classified individuals as low or high creative, but it is unclear whether the metaphor comprehension advantage generalizes across different creativity measures or domains beyond verbal creativity.
generalization_of_metaphor_comprehension.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

causal_role_of_flexible_semantic_memory_.name: causal_role_of_flexible_semantic_memory_in_metaphor_comprehension_advantage
causal_role_of_flexible_semantic_memory_.description: It remains an open question whether the flexible semantic memory structure of high creative individuals causally drives their metaphor comprehension advantage, or whether other cognitive factors account for the effect.
causal_role_of_flexible_semantic_memory_.paper: Metaphor Comprehension in Low and High Creative Individuals

llm_creative_outputs_are_more_homogeneou.name: LLM creative outputs are more homogeneous than human creative outputs
llm_creative_outputs_are_more_homogeneou.description: The study finds that LLM responses are much more similar to other LLM responses than human responses are to each other, even after controlling for response structure and other key variables. This holds across a broad set of LLMs evaluated using standardized creativity tests.
llm_creative_outputs_are_more_homogeneou.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_as_a_dist.name: Cross-LLM creative homogeneity as a distinct phenomenon from single-LLM narrowing
cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_as_a_dist.description: Prior work showed that interacting with a single LLM narrows creative outputs, but it was unclear whether this was due to individual LLM limitations or LLMs in general. This work establishes that the homogeneity is a cross-LLM phenomenon, not limited to any particular model.
cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_as_a_dist.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

standardized_creativity_tests_as_method_.name: Standardized creativity tests as method for eliciting and comparing LLM versus human creative responses
standardized_creativity_tests_as_method_.description: The study uses standardized creativity tests to elicit responses from both humans and a broad set of LLMs, enabling population-level diversity comparisons across groups.
standardized_creativity_tests_as_method_.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

risk_that_widespread_llm_use_as_creative.name: Risk that widespread LLM use as creative assistant converges users toward a limited creative output space
risk_that_widespread_llm_use_as_creative.description: If LLMs across models produce homogeneous outputs, users who rely on any LLM as a creative partner may be driven toward a narrow and overlapping set of 'creative' outputs regardless of which model they choose.
risk_that_widespread_llm_use_as_creative.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

causal_account_of_why_llms_produce_homog.name: Causal account of why LLMs produce homogeneous creative outputs
causal_account_of_why_llms_produce_homog.description: The study documents cross-LLM creative homogeneity but does not establish why LLMs trained differently still converge on similar outputs. The underlying mechanisms—shared training data, RLHF, or architectural constraints—remain unexplained.
causal_account_of_why_llms_produce_homog.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

generalization_of_cross_llm_creative_hom.name: Generalization of cross-LLM creative homogeneity beyond standardized creativity tests
generalization_of_cross_llm_creative_hom.description: The homogeneity finding is established using standardized creativity tests; whether it extends to open-ended real-world creative tasks (e.g., story writing, ideation in professional contexts) remains untested.
generalization_of_cross_llm_creative_hom.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

effect_of_llm_creative_assistance_on_hum.name: Effect of LLM creative assistance on human creative diversity at the population level
effect_of_llm_creative_assistance_on_hum.description: The paper raises the concern that relying on LLMs may narrow population-level human creativity, but empirical evidence for this downstream effect on human creative behavior is not provided.
effect_of_llm_creative_assistance_on_hum.paper: We're Different, We're the Same: Creative Homogeneity Across LLMs

dmn_ecn_dynamic_switching_predicts_creat.name: DMN-ECN dynamic switching predicts creative ability
dmn_ecn_dynamic_switching_predicts_creat.description: Across 10 independent samples (N=2433), the number of dynamic switches between the Default Mode Network and Executive Control Network reliably predicts divergent thinking ability but not general intelligence.
dmn_ecn_dynamic_switching_predicts_creat.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

inverted_u_relationship_between_dmn_ecn_.name: Inverted-U relationship between DMN-ECN switching balance and creativity
inverted_u_relationship_between_dmn_ecn_.description: Optimal creative performance requires a balanced degree of DMN-ECN switching, with too little or too much switching associated with lower creativity, yielding an inverted-U pattern.
inverted_u_relationship_between_dmn_ecn_.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

task_fmri_validation_of_higher_dmn_ecn_s.name: Task-fMRI validation of higher DMN-ECN switching during creative idea generation
task_fmri_validation_of_higher_dmn_ecn_s.description: An independent task-fMRI validation study (N=31) demonstrates higher DMN-ECN switching during creative idea generation compared to a control condition, replicating the inverted-U relationship.
task_fmri_validation_of_higher_dmn_ecn_s.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

meta_analytic_network_neuroscience_appro.name: Meta-analytic network neuroscience approach as method for large-scale creativity research
meta_analytic_network_neuroscience_appro.description: The study employs a meta-analytic network neuroscience approach combining time-resolved network analysis of resting-state fMRI across 10 independent multi-center samples from five countries to examine creativity.
meta_analytic_network_neuroscience_appro.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

specificity_of_dmn_ecn_switching_to_crea.name: Specificity of DMN-ECN switching to creativity versus general intelligence
specificity_of_dmn_ecn_switching_to_crea.description: The predictive relationship of DMN-ECN dynamic switching is specific to creativity (divergent thinking) and does not extend to general intelligence, suggesting domain-specificity of this neural dynamic.
specificity_of_dmn_ecn_switching_to_crea.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_dmn.name: Causal or mechanistic account of how DMN-ECN switching produces creativity
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_dmn.description: While a robust predictive relationship between DMN-ECN switching and creativity is established, the mechanistic or causal pathway explaining why balanced switching produces optimal creative performance remains unaddressed.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_dmn.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

generalization_of_dmn_ecn_switching_crea.name: Generalization of DMN-ECN switching creativity finding to non-divergent-thinking creativity measures
generalization_of_dmn_ecn_switching_crea.description: The current findings are based on divergent thinking as the creativity measure; whether DMN-ECN switching predicts other facets of creativity (e.g., real-world creative achievement, convergent thinking) remains unknown.
generalization_of_dmn_ecn_switching_crea.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

role_of_other_network_interactions_beyon.name: Role of other network interactions beyond DMN-ECN in creativity
role_of_other_network_interactions_beyon.description: The study focuses on DMN-ECN switching; whether interactions involving other networks (e.g., salience network) also contribute independently to creative ability is not addressed.
role_of_other_network_interactions_beyon.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

largest_and_most_ethnically_diverse_crea.name: Largest and most ethnically diverse creativity neuroscience dataset
largest_and_most_ethnically_diverse_crea.description: The study constitutes the largest and most ethnically diverse creativity neuroscience study to date, combining N=2433 participants from Austria, Canada, China, Japan, and the United States across 10 independent samples.
largest_and_most_ethnically_diverse_crea.paper: Dynamic switching between brain networks predicts creative ability

stereo_eeg_dmn_recruitment_during_mind_w.name: stereo_eeg_dmn_recruitment_during_mind_wandering_and_divergent_thinking
stereo_eeg_dmn_recruitment_during_mind_w.description: Using stereo-EEG in 13 patients, the study found DMN recruitment during both mind wandering and alternate uses tasks, establishing that the DMN supports both spontaneous and divergent thought.
stereo_eeg_dmn_recruitment_during_mind_w.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

task_specific_spatiotemporal_dissociatio.name: task_specific_spatiotemporal_dissociation_in_dmn_dynamics
task_specific_spatiotemporal_dissociatio.description: The two tasks showed different spatiotemporal dynamics within the DMN: the alternate uses task engaged the DMN more during the initial stage, while mind wandering engaged it more during the later stage.
task_specific_spatiotemporal_dissociatio.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

dmn_versus_fpn_gamma_and_theta_band_diss.name: dmn_versus_fpn_gamma_and_theta_band_dissociation
dmn_versus_fpn_gamma_and_theta_band_diss.description: Compared to the fronto-parietal network, DMN activity was characterized by stronger increases in gamma band power (30–70 Hz) coupled with lower theta band power (4–8 Hz), especially pronounced during mind wandering.
dmn_versus_fpn_gamma_and_theta_band_diss.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

lateral_dmn_sites_drive_gamma_power_chan.name: lateral_dmn_sites_drive_gamma_power_changes
lateral_dmn_sites_drive_gamma_power_chan.description: Within the DMN, gamma power changes were mainly driven by lateral DMN sites, revealing a spatial specialization within the network.
lateral_dmn_sites_drive_gamma_power_chan.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

task_specific_theta_power_spatial_effect.name: task_specific_theta_power_spatial_effects_in_dmn
task_specific_theta_power_spatial_effect.description: Theta power showed task-specific spatial patterns: alternate uses task theta changes did not differ spatially within DMN, while mind wandering was associated with early lateral and late dorsomedial DMN engagement.
task_specific_theta_power_spatial_effect.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

causal_role_of_dmn_in_divergent_thinking.name: causal_role_of_dmn_in_divergent_thinking_originality
causal_role_of_dmn_in_divergent_thinking.description: Direct cortical stimulation of DMN regions preferentially decreased the originality of alternate uses task responses without affecting fluency, providing causal evidence for DMN's role in generating original connections among concepts.
causal_role_of_dmn_in_divergent_thinking.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

generalization_of_dmn_causal_role_beyond.name: generalization_of_dmn_causal_role_beyond_small_stereo_eeg_sample
generalization_of_dmn_causal_role_beyond.description: The causal stimulation findings rest on 13 patients with stereo-EEG implants, a highly selected clinical sample; generalization to the broader population remains to be established.
generalization_of_dmn_causal_role_beyond.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

mechanistic_account_of_why_dmn_stimulati.name: mechanistic_account_of_why_dmn_stimulation_selectively_reduces_originality_not_fluency
mechanistic_account_of_why_dmn_stimulati.description: The study shows that DMN stimulation reduces originality but not fluency, but the computational or network-level mechanism by which disrupting DMN activity specifically impairs original idea generation remains unexplained.
mechanistic_account_of_why_dmn_stimulati.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

causal_role_of_dmn_in_mind_wandering.name: causal_role_of_dmn_in_mind_wandering
causal_role_of_dmn_in_mind_wandering.description: Cortical stimulation of DMN regions did not affect mind wandering in the current study; whether DMN plays a causal role in mind wandering, as opposed to merely correlating with it, remains open.
causal_role_of_dmn_in_mind_wandering.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

extension_of_stereo_eeg_dmn_findings_to_.name: extension_of_stereo_eeg_dmn_findings_to_other_creative_cognition_paradigms
extension_of_stereo_eeg_dmn_findings_to_.description: The current findings are based on mind wandering and alternate uses tasks; whether the same DMN gamma-theta dynamics and causal originality role extend to other creativity paradigms (e.g., remote associates, insight, artistic production) is unaddressed.
extension_of_stereo_eeg_dmn_findings_to_.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

stereo_eeg_direct_cortical_stimulation_a.name: stereo_eeg_direct_cortical_stimulation_as_method_for_causal_dmn_manipulation
stereo_eeg_direct_cortical_stimulation_a.description: The study used stereo-EEG in 13 epilepsy patients to obtain high-resolution DMN recordings and applied direct cortical stimulation to causally manipulate DMN activity during creativity tasks.
stereo_eeg_direct_cortical_stimulation_a.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

integration_of_stereo_eeg_dmn_stimulatio.name: integration_of_stereo_eeg_dmn_stimulation_findings_with_broader_dmn_creativity_neuroscience
integration_of_stereo_eeg_dmn_stimulatio.description: The authors note that these findings shed light on neural constructs supporting different forms of cognition, but full integration with existing fMRI-based and network-level theories of DMN and creativity is not yet achieved.
integration_of_stereo_eeg_dmn_stimulatio.paper: Default mode network electrophysiological dynamics and causal role in creative thinking

multilayer_linguistic_network_constructi.name: multilayer_linguistic_network_construction_for_lexical_access
multilayer_linguistic_network_constructi.description: The authors construct a large-scale linguistic multilayer network comprising phonological and semantic layers to study lexical access. This network serves as the empirical and computational foundation for all subsequent analyses.
multilayer_linguistic_network_constructi.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

high_similarity_between_phonological_and.name: high_similarity_between_phonological_and_semantic_network_layers
high_similarity_between_phonological_and.description: The links in the phonological and semantic layers of the multilayer network are found to be highly similar to each other. This suggests a substantial overlap in the organizational principles of phonological and semantic knowledge.
high_similarity_between_phonological_and.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

cross_layer_information_increases_networ.name: cross_layer_information_increases_network_efficiency_by_reducing_distances
cross_layer_information_increases_networ.description: Adding information from one layer to the other increases overall network efficiency by decreasing network distances. The effect specifically affects shorter distances rather than uniformly reducing all path lengths.
cross_layer_information_increases_networ.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

multilayer_architecture_achieves_highest.name: multilayer_architecture_achieves_highest_network_efficiency
multilayer_architecture_achieves_highest.description: The combined multilayer architecture demonstrates higher efficiency than either single-layer network alone. This quantifies the unique cognitive benefit of integrating phonological and semantic representations during lexical access.
multilayer_architecture_achieves_highest.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

multilayer_efficiency_linked_to_weak_sem.name: multilayer_efficiency_linked_to_weak_semantic_relations_between_cue_words
multilayer_efficiency_linked_to_weak_sem.description: The efficiency advantage of the multilayer architecture is shown to relate to weak semantic relations between cue words in the network. This connects structural network properties to theoretical cognitive models of lexical access.
multilayer_efficiency_linked_to_weak_sem.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

quantification_of_theoretical_cognitive_.name: quantification_of_theoretical_cognitive_models_of_lexical_access_via_multilayer_networks
quantification_of_theoretical_cognitive_.description: The multilayer network framework is used to quantify theoretical cognitive models of lexical access by formalizing the interaction between phonological and semantic components. This bridges computational network science and psycholinguistic theory.
quantification_of_theoretical_cognitive_.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_phonolo.name: causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_phonological_semantic_interaction_in_lexical_access
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_phonolo.description: While the paper demonstrates structural similarity and efficiency properties of the multilayer network, the causal or mechanistic account of how phonological and semantic layers interact during real-time lexical access remains open. The debate about whether and how these components causally relate to each other is noted as widely ongoing.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_phonolo.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

generalization_of_multilayer_lexical_net.name: generalization_of_multilayer_lexical_network_findings_beyond_the_current_dataset
generalization_of_multilayer_lexical_net.description: The findings are based on a specific large-scale linguistic dataset; whether the results generalize to other languages, lexicons, or populations remains untested. Cross-linguistic and cross-population validation is not addressed in this study.
generalization_of_multilayer_lexical_net.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

behavioral_or_experimental_validation_of.name: behavioral_or_experimental_validation_of_multilayer_network_efficiency_predictions
behavioral_or_experimental_validation_of.description: The study characterizes network efficiency computationally, but whether the identified efficiency properties predict empirical behavioral outcomes in lexical access tasks (e.g., reaction times, priming effects) is not directly tested. Linking network measures to behavioral data would further validate the cognitive model.
behavioral_or_experimental_validation_of.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

debate_over_whether_phonological_and_sem.name: debate_over_whether_phonological_and_semantic_components_relate_during_lexical_access
debate_over_whether_phonological_and_sem.description: The paper explicitly frames the question of whether and how phonological and semantic components relate to each other during lexical access as widely debated. The multilayer network approach provides new quantitative evidence but does not fully resolve the theoretical debate.
debate_over_whether_phonological_and_sem.paper: Unveiling the nature of interaction between semantics and phonology in lexical access based on multilayer networks

cognitive_psychology_evaluation_of_llms_.name: Cognitive psychology evaluation of LLMs via cognitive tasks
cognitive_psychology_evaluation_of_llms_.description: Evaluation of LLMs via cognitive tasks has propelled adoption of psychological frameworks for understanding these models, drawing on human cognitive biases and limitations as an interpretive lens.
cognitive_psychology_evaluation_of_llms_.paper: Using cognitive psychology to understand GPT-like models needs to extend beyond human biases

llm_cognitive_psychology_research_must_e.name: LLM cognitive psychology research must extend beyond human biases
llm_cognitive_psychology_research_must_e.description: The current approach of understanding GPT-like models solely through the lens of human biases is insufficient; research must extend to broader cognitive psychological frameworks.
llm_cognitive_psychology_research_must_e.paper: Using cognitive psychology to understand GPT-like models needs to extend beyond human biases

multilingual_semantic_distance_validatio.name: multilingual_semantic_distance_validation_on_AUT
multilingual_semantic_distance_validatio.description: A multilab study (N=6,522 participants, 107,672 responses) validated semantic distance computed via deep learning models on the AUT across 12 languages, finding that the top-performing model for each language correlated positively with human creativity ratings at medium-to-large effect sizes.
multilingual_semantic_distance_validatio.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

criterion_validity_of_multilingual_seman.name: criterion_validity_of_multilingual_semantic_distance
criterion_validity_of_multilingual_seman.description: Semantic distance showed small-to-moderate effect sizes for openness to experience, creative behavior/achievement, and creative self-concept, comparable to those obtained from human creativity ratings.
criterion_validity_of_multilingual_seman.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

comparison_of_mbert_and_xlm_roberta_for_.name: comparison_of_mBERT_and_XLM_RoBERTa_for_multilingual_semantic_distance
comparison_of_mbert_and_xlm_roberta_for_.description: The study compared two deep learning-based semantic models—multilingual BERT (mBERT) and cross-lingual RoBERTa (XLM-RoBERTa)—for computing semantic distance across 12 languages on the AUT.
comparison_of_mbert_and_xlm_roberta_for_.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

open_access_multilingual_aut_dataset_and.name: open_access_multilingual_AUT_dataset_and_code
open_access_multilingual_aut_dataset_and.description: The study provides an open-access multilingual dataset of AUT responses and human creativity ratings across 12 languages, along with Python code to compute semantic distance, to facilitate future algorithmic development.
open_access_multilingual_aut_dataset_and.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

limited_prior_multilingual_coverage_of_s.name: limited_prior_multilingual_coverage_of_semantic_distance_in_creativity_research
limited_prior_multilingual_coverage_of_s.description: Semantic distance as an automatic creativity scoring tool has primarily been studied in English-speaking samples, leaving the many other languages of the world largely unvalidated.
limited_prior_multilingual_coverage_of_s.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

labor_intensiveness_and_subjectivity_of_.name: labor_intensiveness_and_subjectivity_of_manual_creativity_scoring
labor_intensiveness_and_subjectivity_of_.description: Manual human scoring of divergent thinking responses is labor-intensive and subjective, adversely impacting reliability and validity of creativity assessments.
labor_intensiveness_and_subjectivity_of_.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

generalization_of_multilingual_semantic_.name: generalization_of_multilingual_semantic_distance_validity_beyond_AUT
generalization_of_multilingual_semantic_.description: The validation was conducted specifically on the alternate uses task; it remains open whether semantic distance performs comparably for other divergent thinking tasks or creative paradigms in these languages.
generalization_of_multilingual_semantic_.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

algorithmic_improvement_of_multilingual_.name: algorithmic_improvement_of_multilingual_semantic_distance_models
algorithmic_improvement_of_multilingual_.description: The open-access dataset is explicitly provided to support future algorithmic development; better-performing models for specific languages remain to be developed.
algorithmic_improvement_of_multilingual_.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

generalization_of_multilingual_semantic_.name: generalization_of_multilingual_semantic_distance_findings_beyond_current_language_sample
generalization_of_multilingual_semantic_.description: The study covers 12 languages but many world languages remain unrepresented; the validity of these approaches for other languages is unknown.
generalization_of_multilingual_semantic_.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

individual_differences_in_semantic_dista.name: individual_differences_in_semantic_distance_validity_across_language_communities
individual_differences_in_semantic_dista.description: It is unclear whether demographic or cultural factors within each language community moderate the validity of semantic distance as a creativity metric.
individual_differences_in_semantic_dista.paper: Multilingual semantic distance: Automatic verbal creativity assessment in many languages.

mesoscale_brain_network_community_struct.name: mesoscale_brain_network_community_structure_relates_to_creative_thinking
mesoscale_brain_network_community_struct.description: Greater capacity for creative thinking was related to increased co-assignment of medial-temporal regions and decreased co-assignment of subcortical regions to the same community, demonstrating that creative capacity is reflected in mesoscale (community-level) organization of resting-state brain networks.
mesoscale_brain_network_community_struct.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

dmn_hub_structure_positively_relates_to_.name: dmn_hub_structure_positively_relates_to_creative_ability
dmn_hub_structure_positively_relates_to_.description: Increased hubness (participation coefficient) of DMN regions was positively related to creative ability, indicating that DMN regions act as inter-community bridges more strongly in highly creative individuals.
dmn_hub_structure_positively_relates_to_.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

medial_temporal_hub_structure_negatively.name: medial_temporal_hub_structure_negatively_relates_to_creative_ability
medial_temporal_hub_structure_negatively.description: Increased hubness of medial-temporal regions was negatively related to creative ability, suggesting that greater medial-temporal inter-community bridging is associated with lower creative capacity.
medial_temporal_hub_structure_negatively.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

data_driven_community_and_hub_analysis_o.name: data_driven_community_and_hub_analysis_of_resting_state_fmri_for_creative_thinking
data_driven_community_and_hub_analysis_o.description: Co-assignment probabilities of brain regions to the same community were computed from resting-state functional imaging data, and participant-specific communities were used to derive participation coefficients as hub measures, all related to individual differences in creative thinking.
data_driven_community_and_hub_analysis_o.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

gap_in_understanding_mesoscale_brain_net.name: gap_in_understanding_mesoscale_brain_network_features_and_creativity
gap_in_understanding_mesoscale_brain_net.description: Prior to this study, research on creativity and brain networks had focused on local (regional) and global levels, leaving it unclear how creative thinking relates to mesoscale features such as community and hub organization.
gap_in_understanding_mesoscale_brain_net.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

replication_of_mesoscale_creativity_find.name: replication_of_mesoscale_creativity_findings_in_independent_samples
replication_of_mesoscale_creativity_find.description: The associations between resting-state community and hub structure and creative ability should be replicated in independent samples to establish robustness and generalizability.
replication_of_mesoscale_creativity_find.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

causal_account_of_mesoscale_network_orga.name: causal_account_of_mesoscale_network_organization_and_creativity
causal_account_of_mesoscale_network_orga.description: It remains unclear whether the observed differences in mesoscale community and hub organization cause differences in creative ability or are a downstream consequence of other neural or cognitive factors.
causal_account_of_mesoscale_network_orga.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

mechanistic_account_of_why_dmn_and_media.name: mechanistic_account_of_why_dmn_and_medial_temporal_hubs_differentially_relate_to_creativity
mechanistic_account_of_why_dmn_and_media.description: The study finds opposite hub-creativity relationships for DMN versus medial-temporal regions, but the mechanism explaining why bridging roles in these systems have opposing effects on creative capacity is not addressed.
mechanistic_account_of_why_dmn_and_media.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

generalization_of_mesoscale_creativity_f.name: generalization_of_mesoscale_creativity_findings_beyond_resting_state_fmri
generalization_of_mesoscale_creativity_f.description: The community and hub organization findings were derived from resting-state fMRI; it is open whether similar mesoscale features emerge during active creative task performance or predict creativity across other neuroimaging modalities.
generalization_of_mesoscale_creativity_f.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

integration_of_mesoscale_network_finding.name: integration_of_mesoscale_network_findings_with_local_and_global_brain_network_creativity_accounts
integration_of_mesoscale_network_finding.description: The paper calls for integrating mesoscale (community/hub) findings with existing local and global brain network accounts of creativity to build a unified multi-scale neural model.
integration_of_mesoscale_network_finding.paper: Community structure of the creative brain at rest

overlap_between_question_asking_and_prom.name: Overlap between question-asking and prompt engineering as creative processes
overlap_between_question_asking_and_prom.description: The paper argues that question-asking and prompt engineering share a common foundation in creativity, critical thinking, and cognitive flexibility, forming a symbiotic relationship rather than being distinct activities.
overlap_between_question_asking_and_prom.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

bloom_s_taxonomy_as_a_framework_for_anal.name: Bloom's taxonomy as a framework for analyzing question and prompt complexity
bloom_s_taxonomy_as_a_framework_for_anal.description: The paper uses Bloom's taxonomy and diverse question types to analyze the complexity of questions and prompts, proposing strategies for efficient and engaging prompt design.
bloom_s_taxonomy_as_a_framework_for_anal.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

creativity_as_a_dynamic_and_central_role.name: Creativity as a dynamic and central role in both question-asking and prompt engineering
creativity_as_a_dynamic_and_central_role.description: The paper theoretically emphasizes that creativity is not peripheral but plays a dynamic, central role in both formulating questions and engineering prompts for language models.
creativity_as_a_dynamic_and_central_role.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

prompt_engineering_as_an_art_requiring_c.name: Prompt engineering as an art requiring cognitive flexibility and critical thinking
prompt_engineering_as_an_art_requiring_c.description: The paper positions prompt engineering as a skill-intensive art form analogous to question-asking, requiring cognitive flexibility and critical thinking beyond simple instruction formulation.
prompt_engineering_as_an_art_requiring_c.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

empirical_validation_of_the_question_ask.name: Empirical validation of the question-asking and prompt engineering overlap framework
empirical_validation_of_the_question_ask.description: The paper is explicitly theoretical, leaving empirical testing of whether the proposed overlap and shared cognitive skills between question-asking and prompt engineering actually predict performance or outcomes as an open matter.
empirical_validation_of_the_question_ask.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

strategies_for_efficient_and_engaging_pr.name: Strategies for efficient and engaging prompt design grounded in question complexity
strategies_for_efficient_and_engaging_pr.description: The paper proposes concrete strategies for prompt design derived from question complexity analysis and Bloom's taxonomy, intended to advance both language model use and communication skills.
strategies_for_efficient_and_engaging_pr.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

advancing_understanding_of_human_machine.name: Advancing understanding of human-machine interaction via creative inquiry framework
advancing_understanding_of_human_machine.description: The paper aspires to advance understanding of human-machine interactions and language models by framing prompt engineering within a creative inquiry and question-asking framework, but this remains an open aspirational contribution pending uptake and validation.
advancing_understanding_of_human_machine.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

cognitive_flexibility_as_a_shared_requir.name: Cognitive flexibility as a shared requirement for question-asking and prompt engineering
cognitive_flexibility_as_a_shared_requir.description: The paper identifies cognitive flexibility as a skill underpinning both effective question-asking and effective prompt engineering, suggesting these domains draw on overlapping cognitive resources.
cognitive_flexibility_as_a_shared_requir.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

generalization_of_the_creative_inquiry_f.name: Generalization of the creative inquiry framework beyond current language models
generalization_of_the_creative_inquiry_f.description: The framework is developed in the context of current AI and NLP methods; whether it generalizes as language models evolve or to non-text modalities remains open.
generalization_of_the_creative_inquiry_f.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

empirical_testing_of_creativity_and_crit.name: Empirical testing of creativity and critical thinking as mediators of prompt engineering quality
empirical_testing_of_creativity_and_crit.description: While the paper claims creativity and critical thinking underlie prompt engineering quality, it does not empirically test whether individual differences in these traits predict prompt effectiveness or creative output quality.
empirical_testing_of_creativity_and_crit.paper: The Art of Creative Inquiry—From Question Asking to Prompt Engineering

inconsistent_creativity_measurement_as_b.name: inconsistent_creativity_measurement_as_barrier_to_progress
inconsistent_creativity_measurement_as_b.description: Different researchers use inconsistent measures of creativity, hindering progress toward shared understanding of cognitive and neural components. This measurement inconsistency makes it difficult to compare findings across studies.
inconsistent_creativity_measurement_as_b.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

construct_measure_mapping_challenge_in_c.name: construct_measure_mapping_challenge_in_creativity_research
construct_measure_mapping_challenge_in_c.description: A central challenge in creativity research is establishing a mapping between constructs and measures—identifying tasks that best capture creative abilities. This challenge parallels broader issues in experimental psychology and cognitive neuroscience.
construct_measure_mapping_challenge_in_c.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

neurally_informed_ontology_of_creativity.name: neurally_informed_ontology_of_creativity_measurement
neurally_informed_ontology_of_creativity.description: The paper aims to develop an ontology of creativity measurement informed by neural data, using meta-analytic structure in neuroimaging to identify which tasks best reflect similarity among creativity-relevant constructs. This is positioned as a long-term aspiration with initial proof-of-concept demonstrated.
neurally_informed_ontology_of_creativity.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

creativity_researcher_survey_of_construc.name: creativity_researcher_survey_of_construct_similarity
creativity_researcher_survey_of_construc.description: Creativity researchers were surveyed to build a model of similarity between creativity-relevant constructs. This serves as the theoretical/expert basis for the construct similarity space used in subsequent analyses.
creativity_researcher_survey_of_construc.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

neurosynth_meta_analytic_neural_activati.name: neurosynth_meta_analytic_neural_activation_maps_for_creativity_tasks
neurosynth_meta_analytic_neural_activati.description: NeuroSynth meta-analytic software was used to generate maps of neural activity robustly associated with tasks measuring creativity-relevant constructs. These maps provide the neural data for the representational similarity analysis.
neurosynth_meta_analytic_neural_activati.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

rsa_based_approach_for_identifying_optim.name: rsa_based_approach_for_identifying_optimal_creativity_task_sets
rsa_based_approach_for_identifying_optim.description: A representational similarity analysis-based approach was used to identify which constructs and tasks positively or negatively impacted model fit between expert-rated construct similarity and neural activation similarity. This operationalizes the construct-measure mapping problem neurally.
rsa_based_approach_for_identifying_optim.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

proof_of_concept_for_neural_meta_analyti.name: proof_of_concept_for_neural_meta_analytic_creativity_ontology_approach
proof_of_concept_for_neural_meta_analyti.description: The study demonstrated initial proof-of-concept that combining researcher-survey-based construct similarity with NeuroSynth meta-analytic neural maps via RSA can identify optimal task subsets for capturing creativity dimensions. This is explicitly framed as initial rather than definitive.
proof_of_concept_for_neural_meta_analyti.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

identification_of_creativity_constructs_.name: identification_of_creativity_constructs_and_tasks_affecting_model_fit
identification_of_creativity_constructs_.description: The RSA approach identified particular constructs—and particular tasks intended to measure them—that positively or negatively impacted the fit between expert construct similarity and neural similarity. This is the core empirical output of the paper.
identification_of_creativity_constructs_.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

long_term_potential_of_approach_to_advan.name: long_term_potential_of_approach_to_advance_creativity_ontology
long_term_potential_of_approach_to_advan.description: The approach is argued to have long-term potential to meaningfully advance the ontological development of creativity research as creativity neuroscience continues to grow. Full realization depends on expansion of neuroimaging data.
long_term_potential_of_approach_to_advan.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

neurosynth_approach_more_immediately_app.name: neurosynth_approach_more_immediately_applicable_to_longer_established_fields
neurosynth_approach_more_immediately_app.description: Because the approach relies on neuroimaging meta-analysis, it has more immediate potential for fields with larger existing neuroimaging datasets than creativity neuroscience currently possesses. This positions creativity neuroscience as an aspirational rather than current application domain.
neurosynth_approach_more_immediately_app.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

replication_of_rsa_creativity_ontology_p.name: replication_of_rsa_creativity_ontology_proof_of_concept_in_independent_samples
replication_of_rsa_creativity_ontology_p.description: The initial proof-of-concept findings need replication in independent samples and with different researcher survey populations to confirm robustness. The paper does not provide such replication.
replication_of_rsa_creativity_ontology_p.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

generalization_of_rsa_creativity_ontolog.name: generalization_of_rsa_creativity_ontology_approach_beyond_current_construct_set
generalization_of_rsa_creativity_ontolog.description: The current approach was applied to a specific set of creativity-relevant constructs identified by surveyed researchers; whether findings generalize to a broader or different set of creativity constructs is unknown.
generalization_of_rsa_creativity_ontolog.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

optimal_creativity_task_battery_identifi.name: optimal_creativity_task_battery_identified_via_neural_ontology
optimal_creativity_task_battery_identifi.description: A key practical aim is to identify optimal sets of tasks to capture elements of creativity (dimensions of similarity space among creativity constructs). The current paper points toward this but does not deliver a finalized task battery.
optimal_creativity_task_battery_identifi.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

aggregation_of_neuroimaging_data_as_enab.name: aggregation_of_neuroimaging_data_as_enabling_resource_for_creativity_ontology
aggregation_of_neuroimaging_data_as_enab.description: The paper highlights that new resources for aggregating neuroimaging data (e.g., NeuroSynth) and methods for identifying structure in multivariate data present new opportunities for construct-measure mapping in creativity research.
aggregation_of_neuroimaging_data_as_enab.paper: Developing a neurally informed ontology of creativity measurement

semantic_memory_structure_predicts_real_.name: semantic_memory_structure_predicts_real_life_creativity
semantic_memory_structure_predicts_real_.description: Individual semantic memory network properties, estimated from a semantic relatedness judgment task during multi-echo fMRI, significantly predicted participants' real-life creativity. This establishes a direct link between the organization of semantic memory and real-world creative behavior.
semantic_memory_structure_predicts_real_.paper: Brain connectivity-based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

task_based_fc_predicts_creativity_relate.name: task_based_fc_predicts_creativity_related_semantic_network_properties
task_based_fc_predicts_creativity_relate.description: Using connectome-based predictive modeling on task-based fMRI data from a semantic relatedness judgment task, specific functional connectivity patterns were identified that predicted creativity-relevant properties of individual semantic memory networks. This links neural architecture to semantic memory organization underlying creativity.
task_based_fc_predicts_creativity_relate.paper: Brain connectivity-based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

semantic_memory_structure_mediates_fc_re.name: semantic_memory_structure_mediates_fc_real_life_creativity_relationship
semantic_memory_structure_mediates_fc_re.description: Semantic memory network properties mediated the relationship between brain functional connectivity patterns and real-life creativity, providing a pathway account from neural connectivity through memory organization to creative behavior.
semantic_memory_structure_mediates_fc_re.paper: Brain connectivity-based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

multi_echo_fmri_semantic_relatedness_tas.name: multi_echo_fmri_semantic_relatedness_task_for_individual_network_estimation
multi_echo_fmri_semantic_relatedness_tas.description: Multi-echo fMRI data were acquired while participants performed a semantic relatedness judgment task; ratings from this task were used to estimate each participant's individual semantic memory network. This method bridges neural measurement and cognitive network construction at the individual level.
multi_echo_fmri_semantic_relatedness_tas.paper: Brain connectivity-based prediction of real-life creativity is mediated by semantic memory structure

artists_show_higher_openness_to_experien.name: artists_show_higher_openness_to_experience_than_scientists_and_controls
artists_show_higher_openness_to_experien.description: Replicating prior work, artists scored reliably higher on openness to experience compared to scientists and a less creative control group across three samples.
artists_show_higher_openness_to_experien.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

openness_has_higher_connectivity_with_ot.name: openness_has_higher_connectivity_with_other_traits_in_artists_than_in_scientists_or_controls
openness_has_higher_connectivity_with_ot.description: Psychometric network analysis revealed that openness to experience shows higher connectivity (co-occurrence) with other personality traits in artists, indicating greater heterogeneity in how openness manifests in highly creative artists.
openness_has_higher_connectivity_with_ot.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

scientists_personality_network_is_more_c.name: scientists_personality_network_is_more_cohesive_than_artists_or_controls
scientists_personality_network_is_more_c.description: Across all three samples, scientists' personality network structure showed greater cohesiveness than that of artists and the control group, indicating greater homogeneity in personality characteristics associated with scientific creative achievement.
scientists_personality_network_is_more_c.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

psychometric_network_analysis_applied_to.name: psychometric_network_analysis_applied_to_creative_personality_structure
psychometric_network_analysis_applied_to.description: A psychometric network approach was used to characterize multidimensional personality structure of highly creative artists and scientists, going beyond univariate associations to reveal co-occurrence patterns among multiple personality factors.
psychometric_network_analysis_applied_to.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

three_sample_replication_of_creative_per.name: three_sample_replication_of_creative_personality_network_findings
three_sample_replication_of_creative_per.description: Analyses were conducted across three samples — college students, a representative adult sample, and the Big-C project of eminent creative professionals — enabling cross-sample replication of personality network findings.
three_sample_replication_of_creative_per.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

constellation_of_traits_underlying_creat.name: constellation_of_traits_underlying_creative_achievement_in_arts_versus_sciences
constellation_of_traits_underlying_creat.description: The study identifies a multidimensional constellation of personality traits that characterize creative achievement differently in the arts and sciences, going beyond single-trait accounts.
constellation_of_traits_underlying_creat.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

limitation_of_univariate_personality_cre.name: limitation_of_univariate_personality_creativity_research
limitation_of_univariate_personality_cre.description: Prior research has largely focused on univariate associations between creativity and personality, potentially obscuring complex interactions among multiple personality factors and rendering an incomplete picture of the creative personality.
limitation_of_univariate_personality_cre.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

causal_account_of_why_openness_is_more_h.name: causal_account_of_why_openness_is_more_heterogeneous_in_artists
causal_account_of_why_openness_is_more_h.description: The study documents that openness shows higher connectivity with other traits in artists but does not explain the causal or mechanistic reasons for this greater heterogeneity.
causal_account_of_why_openness_is_more_h.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

generalization_of_creative_personality_n.name: generalization_of_creative_personality_network_findings_beyond_arts_and_sciences
generalization_of_creative_personality_n.description: The study focuses on artists and scientists as creative groups; whether the personality network patterns generalize to other creative domains (e.g., entrepreneurship, culinary arts) remains untested.
generalization_of_creative_personality_n.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

causal_direction_between_personality_net.name: causal_direction_between_personality_network_structure_and_creative_achievement
causal_direction_between_personality_net.description: The cross-sectional design cannot determine whether distinctive personality network structure produces creative achievement or whether creative experience shapes personality network structure over time.
causal_direction_between_personality_net.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

integration_of_personality_network_findi.name: integration_of_personality_network_findings_with_cognitive_and_neural_creativity_accounts
integration_of_personality_network_findi.description: The personality network findings (e.g., openness heterogeneity in artists, cohesiveness in scientists) need to be linked to cognitive mechanisms and neural substrates of creativity to form a unified account.
integration_of_personality_network_findi.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

replication_of_creative_personality_netw.name: replication_of_creative_personality_network_findings_in_independent_samples
replication_of_creative_personality_netw.description: Although three samples are used within the study, independent external replication of the psychometric network findings for artists and scientists would strengthen confidence in the findings.
replication_of_creative_personality_netw.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

mechanisms_linking_personality_network_c.name: mechanisms_linking_personality_network_cohesiveness_to_scientific_creative_achievement
mechanisms_linking_personality_network_c.description: The finding that scientists have more cohesive personality networks is documented but the mechanism by which personality homogeneity facilitates scientific creativity is not explained.
mechanisms_linking_personality_network_c.paper: Mapping the Creative Personality: A Psychometric Network Analysis of Highly Creative Artists and Scientists

gips_do_not_relate_to_preferences_for_ab.name: GIPs do not relate to preferences for abstract art
gips_do_not_relate_to_preferences_for_ab.description: The study replicated the earlier observation that global image properties (GIPs) do not predict subjective preference ratings for abstract art paintings, confirming that physical image statistics are insufficient to account for aesthetic preferences in this domain.
gips_do_not_relate_to_preferences_for_ab.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

semantic_network_structure_of_abstract_a.name: Semantic network structure of abstract art predicts subjective preference
semantic_network_structure_of_abstract_a.description: A network constructed from verbal descriptions of abstract art paintings predicted subjective preference ratings, whereas a network based on GIPs did not, demonstrating the primacy of semantic content over physical image statistics for aesthetic preference.
semantic_network_structure_of_abstract_a.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

semantic_network_structure_determines_ar.name: Semantic network structure determines art style affiliation
semantic_network_structure_determines_ar.description: Network measures derived from verbal descriptions of abstract art paintings successfully classified images into specific art styles, while GIP-based network measures did not, indicating that semantic content captures style-relevant structure.
semantic_network_structure_determines_ar.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

abstract_art_paintings_should_not_be_tre.name: Abstract art paintings should not be treated as a single homogeneous category
abstract_art_paintings_should_not_be_tre.description: Preference ratings and GIPs differed across sub-groups of abstract art paintings, indicating that treating abstract art as a uniform category obscures meaningful variation and is not appropriate in empirical aesthetics research.
abstract_art_paintings_should_not_be_tre.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

verbal_descriptions_as_a_network_constru.name: Verbal descriptions as a network construction approach for visual stimuli
verbal_descriptions_as_a_network_constru.description: The study estimated the network structure of abstract art paintings using verbal descriptions provided by participants, offering a semantic-level network representation alternative to GIP-based computational networks.
verbal_descriptions_as_a_network_constru.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

gip_based_network_construction_for_visua.name: GIP-based network construction for visual stimuli
gip_based_network_construction_for_visua.description: The study estimated network structure of abstract art paintings using global image properties as the basis for edge weights, providing a low-level perceptual network representation.
gip_based_network_construction_for_visua.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

generalization_of_gip_null_finding_and_s.name: Generalization of GIP null finding and semantic network preference finding beyond current stimulus set
generalization_of_gip_null_finding_and_s.description: The findings that GIPs do not predict preferences and that semantic networks do are based on a specific set of abstract art paintings; it remains open whether these results generalize to other abstract art collections, cultures, or participant populations.
generalization_of_gip_null_finding_and_s.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

mechanisms_underlying_the_verbal_descrip.name: Mechanisms underlying the verbal description advantage in aesthetic preference
mechanisms_underlying_the_verbal_descrip.description: While semantic networks derived from verbal descriptions outperform GIP-based networks in predicting aesthetic preference and art style, the cognitive and perceptual mechanisms by which verbal/semantic representations drive abstract art preferences remain unexplained.
mechanisms_underlying_the_verbal_descrip.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

generalization_of_semantic_network_advan.name: Generalization of semantic network advantage to other visual art categories beyond abstract art
generalization_of_semantic_network_advan.description: It is unclear whether the superiority of semantic over GIP-based networks in predicting preference and style extends to representational art, photography, or other visual aesthetic domains.
generalization_of_semantic_network_advan.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

identification_of_which_sub_groups_of_ab.name: Identification of which sub-groups of abstract art paintings drive differential GIP and preference patterns
identification_of_which_sub_groups_of_ab.description: While sub-group differences in preferences and GIPs are demonstrated, the study leaves open a fuller characterization of what defines these sub-groups and which stimulus features systematically drive the variation.
identification_of_which_sub_groups_of_ab.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

integration_of_verbal_description_networ.name: Integration of verbal description network approach into empirical aesthetics methodology
integration_of_verbal_description_networ.description: The paper argues for the utility of verbal descriptor-based network analysis as a tool in empirical aesthetics, but broader methodological adoption across aesthetics research has not yet occurred.
integration_of_verbal_description_networ.paper: Abstract art paintings, global image properties, and verbal descriptions: An empirical and computational investigation.

sd_patients_show_disproportionate_diverg.name: SD patients show disproportionate divergent thinking impairment relative to bvFTD
sd_patients_show_disproportionate_diverg.description: Semantic dementia patients generated significantly fewer responses on the divergent thinking task than both bvFTD patients and healthy controls, with SD showing disproportionately greater impairment than bvFTD, indicating semantic memory is especially critical for divergent thinking.
sd_patients_show_disproportionate_diverg.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

sd_patients_produce_less_original_and_le.name: SD patients produce less original and less flexible divergent thinking responses
sd_patients_produce_less_original_and_le.description: Both SD and bvFTD patient groups generated responses that were less original and reflected less flexible thinking compared to healthy controls, with originality deficits observed across both clinical groups.
sd_patients_produce_less_original_and_le.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

fluency_in_sd_correlates_with_semantic_a.name: Fluency in SD correlates with semantic association ability
fluency_in_sd_correlates_with_semantic_a.description: In SD patients, the fluency of divergent thinking responses correlated specifically with performance on a measure of semantic association, linking the quantity of ideas generated to intact semantic memory functioning.
fluency_in_sd_correlates_with_semantic_a.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

originality_in_sd_correlates_with_semant.name: Originality in SD correlates with semantic naming and comprehension
originality_in_sd_correlates_with_semant.description: In SD patients, the originality of divergent thinking responses correlated with semantic naming and comprehension ability, underscoring the role of conceptual knowledge in generating high-quality creative ideas.
originality_in_sd_correlates_with_semant.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

originality_in_bvftd_correlates_with_let.name: Originality in bvFTD correlates with letter fluency and response inhibition
originality_in_bvftd_correlates_with_let.description: In bvFTD patients, originality of ideas correlated with letter fluency and response inhibition, suggesting executive and inhibitory control processes underpin creative idea quality in this group rather than semantic processes.
originality_in_bvftd_correlates_with_let.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

left_medial_temporal_lobe_cluster_associ.name: Left medial temporal lobe cluster associated with diminished divergent thinking fluency
left_medial_temporal_lobe_cluster_associ.description: VBM analysis identified a left medial temporal lobe grey matter cluster centred on the left anterior hippocampus as consistently associated with reduced fluency of ideas, implicating this region as essential for divergent thinking.
left_medial_temporal_lobe_cluster_associ.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

left_middle_frontal_gyrus_cluster_associ.name: Left middle frontal gyrus cluster associated with diminished divergent thinking fluency
left_middle_frontal_gyrus_cluster_associ.description: A second VBM cluster in the left middle frontal gyrus was consistently associated with diminished fluency of divergent thinking ideas, highlighting a prefrontal contribution distinct from the temporal lobe cluster.
left_middle_frontal_gyrus_cluster_associ.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

lesion_approach_as_converging_evidence_f.name: Lesion approach as converging evidence for divergent thinking neuroscience
lesion_approach_as_converging_evidence_f.description: The study uses a lesion-based approach (combining patients with neurodegenerative disorders and VBM) to identify brain regions not merely implicated in but essential for divergent thinking, complementing neuroimaging correlational approaches.
lesion_approach_as_converging_evidence_f.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

semantic_processes_as_pivotal_for_creati.name: Semantic processes as pivotal for creative cognition
semantic_processes_as_pivotal_for_creati.description: The study argues that semantic processes—supported by temporal lobe structures—play a pivotal role in creative cognition, as evidenced by disproportionate creativity deficits in SD and correlations between semantic measures and creative performance.
semantic_processes_as_pivotal_for_creati.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

replication_of_sd_divergent_thinking_fin.name: Replication of SD divergent thinking findings in larger or more diverse samples
replication_of_sd_divergent_thinking_fin.description: The study is based on a small sample of 10 SD patients; replication in larger and more diverse clinical samples is needed to confirm the robustness and generalizability of the observed deficits and VBM clusters.
