replication_of_sd_divergent_thinking_fin.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

causal_account_of_how_semantic_memory_de.name: Causal account of how semantic memory degeneration produces divergent thinking deficits
causal_account_of_how_semantic_memory_de.description: While lesion evidence implicates semantic memory structures, the mechanistic pathway by which progressive conceptual knowledge loss reduces the fluency, originality, and flexibility of creative ideas remains to be fully specified.
causal_account_of_how_semantic_memory_de.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

generalization_of_vbm_fluency_clusters_b.name: Generalization of VBM fluency clusters beyond divergent thinking fluency to other creativity dimensions
generalization_of_vbm_fluency_clusters_b.description: The VBM analysis identified clusters associated with fluency but the neural correlates of originality and flexibility deficits via structural grey matter analysis were not reported as significant clusters, leaving open whether distinct regions underlie these other dimensions.
generalization_of_vbm_fluency_clusters_b.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

dissociation_between_semantic_and_execut.name: Dissociation between semantic and executive contributions to divergent thinking originality
dissociation_between_semantic_and_execut.description: The contrasting correlates of originality in SD (semantic naming/comprehension) versus bvFTD (letter fluency/response inhibition) suggest that semantic and executive processes make dissociable contributions to the originality of creative ideas.
dissociation_between_semantic_and_execut.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

extension_of_sd_divergent_thinking_findi.name: Extension of SD divergent thinking findings to other creativity paradigms beyond verbal divergent thinking
extension_of_sd_divergent_thinking_findi.description: The study focuses on a single verbal divergent thinking task; whether SD-related semantic degeneration similarly impairs other creativity measures (e.g., figural, remote associates) remains unknown.
extension_of_sd_divergent_thinking_findi.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

role_of_anterior_hippocampus_in_divergen.name: Role of anterior hippocampus in divergent thinking
role_of_anterior_hippocampus_in_divergen.description: The VBM analysis specifically implicates the left anterior hippocampus within the broader medial temporal lobe cluster, pointing to hippocampal-dependent memory processes as a neural substrate for the generative fluency of creative ideas.
role_of_anterior_hippocampus_in_divergen.paper: The effect of semantic memory degeneration on creative thinking: A voxel-based morphometry analysis

relational_combinations_increase_semanti.name: Relational combinations increase semantic network connectivity and lower structure
relational_combinations_increase_semanti.description: Constructing relational interpretations of novel nominal compounds increased connectivity and lowered structure in participants' semantic memory networks, whereas attributive combinations did not produce this effect.
relational_combinations_increase_semanti.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

dynamic_reconfiguration_of_semantic_memo.name: Dynamic reconfiguration of semantic memory networks via novel conceptual combination
dynamic_reconfiguration_of_semantic_memo.description: The process of interpreting unfamiliar nominal compounds can dynamically reconfigure the relational structure of semantic memory networks, demonstrating that concept use actively reshapes conceptual relationships.
dynamic_reconfiguration_of_semantic_memo.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

distinction_between_attributive_and_rela.name: Distinction between attributive and relational nominal compound interpretation
distinction_between_attributive_and_rela.description: The study operationalizes and contrasts two types of conceptual combination: attributive (one attribute of one concept describes another) and relational (a relation is identified to link two concepts), finding differential effects on semantic network structure.
distinction_between_attributive_and_rela.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

relational_combinations_require_novel_co.name: Relational combinations require novel context generation as mechanism
relational_combinations_require_novel_co.description: The authors propose that constructing relational interpretations requires generating novel contexts, and this is why relational combinations lead to greater restructuring of the semantic network compared to attributive combinations.
relational_combinations_require_novel_co.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

pre_and_post_combination_semantic_networ.name: Pre- and post-combination semantic network comparison methodology
pre_and_post_combination_semantic_networ.description: The study employed a within-participant design measuring semantic network properties (e.g., connectivity) before and after participants interpreted novel nominal compounds, enabling detection of dynamic reconfiguration.
pre_and_post_combination_semantic_networ.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

generalization_of_relational_combination.name: Generalization of relational combination network reconfiguration beyond nominal compounds
generalization_of_relational_combination.description: It remains open whether the dynamic reconfiguration of semantic networks found for novel nominal compounds generalizes to other forms of conceptual combination or creative language use.
generalization_of_relational_combination.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

causal_direction_of_the_combination_netw.name: Causal direction of the combination-network reconfiguration relationship
causal_direction_of_the_combination_netw.description: The study demonstrates an association between relational combination and network reconfiguration, but the causal direction—whether combination drives reconfiguration or pre-existing network structure facilitates combination—remains unestablished.
causal_direction_of_the_combination_netw.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

replication_of_relational_versus_attribu.name: Replication of relational versus attributive network reconfiguration finding in independent samples
replication_of_relational_versus_attribu.description: The differential effect of relational versus attributive combinations on semantic network connectivity and structure requires replication in independent samples to establish robustness.
replication_of_relational_versus_attribu.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

individual_differences_in_susceptibility.name: Individual differences in susceptibility to semantic network reconfiguration via conceptual combination
individual_differences_in_susceptibility.description: The study does not address whether individuals differ systematically in how much their semantic networks are reconfigured by relational combination, nor what cognitive or personality factors might moderate this effect.
individual_differences_in_susceptibility.paper: Novel conceptual combination can dynamically reconfigure semantic memory networks

novel_embodied_coding_scheme_for_diverge.name: Novel embodied coding scheme for divergent thinking responses
novel_embodied_coding_scheme_for_diverge.description: The authors devised a new coding scheme capturing dimensions of action or body simulations in divergent thinking responses, grounded in embodied and neurocognitive perspectives. This scheme aims to characterize cognitive strategies used in creative endeavors.
novel_embodied_coding_scheme_for_diverge.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

reliable_coding_of_embodied_dimensions_b.name: Reliable coding of embodied dimensions by naïve raters
reliable_coding_of_embodied_dimensions_b.description: The study demonstrates that naïve raters can reliably code the novel embodied dimensions in divergent thinking responses, supporting the scheme's inter-rater reliability.
reliable_coding_of_embodied_dimensions_b.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

individual_differences_in_creativity_mod.name: Individual differences in creativity modulate use of embodied dimensions over time in DT
individual_differences_in_creativity_mod.description: The results show that individual differences in creativity influence how different embodied dimensions are used across the time course of divergent thinking response generation. This provides new hypotheses about creative response generation strategies.
individual_differences_in_creativity_mod.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_emb.name: Causal or mechanistic account of how embodied simulations produce creative responses in DT
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_emb.description: The study is exploratory and generates hypotheses about embodied cognitive strategies in creativity, but does not establish a causal or mechanistic account of how action/body simulations produce creative ideas.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_emb.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

replication_of_embodied_dt_coding_scheme.name: Replication of embodied DT coding scheme findings in independent samples
replication_of_embodied_dt_coding_scheme.description: The current findings are based on a single previously published dataset analyzed in an exploratory manner; replication in independent samples is needed to establish robustness.
replication_of_embodied_dt_coding_scheme.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

generalization_of_embodied_coding_scheme.name: Generalization of embodied coding scheme beyond divergent thinking tasks
generalization_of_embodied_coding_scheme.description: The scheme was developed and applied specifically to divergent thinking; whether it captures embodied dimensions in other creativity paradigms or cognitive tasks remains unexamined.
generalization_of_embodied_coding_scheme.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

integration_of_embodied_coding_scheme_fi.name: Integration of embodied coding scheme findings with neurocognitive creativity models
integration_of_embodied_coding_scheme_fi.description: The authors propose that the coding scheme should serve to characterize cognitive strategies in creative endeavors; integrating these findings with broader neurocognitive and embodied cognition frameworks for creativity remains future work.
integration_of_embodied_coding_scheme_fi.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

optimal_operationalization_of_embodied_s.name: Optimal operationalization of embodied simulation dimensions for creativity research
optimal_operationalization_of_embodied_s.description: The study devises a preliminary coding scheme; what the best set of embodied dimensions is and how they map onto established creativity constructs remains to be determined.
optimal_operationalization_of_embodied_s.paper: A novel coding scheme for assessing responses in divergent thinking: An embodied approach

multilayer_network_framework_for_the_men.name: multilayer_network_framework_for_the_mental_lexicon
multilayer_network_framework_for_the_men.description: The paper presents cognitive multilayer networks as a quantitative and interpretative framework for mapping multiple types of conceptual associations (semantic, phonological, syntactic, etc.) simultaneously within the mental lexicon. This framework captures how different layers of associations co-exist and influence cognitive processing.
multilayer_network_framework_for_the_men.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

multiplex_viability_highlights_language_.name: multiplex_viability_highlights_language_kernels_and_clinical_insights
multiplex_viability_highlights_language_.description: Multiplex viability analysis reveals language kernels and facilitative effects of knowledge processing in both healthy and clinical populations, a quantitative mechanism not observable in single-layer networks. This finding emerges specifically from combining multiple lexical layers.
multiplex_viability_highlights_language_.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

multilayer_community_detection_enables_c.name: multilayer_community_detection_enables_contextual_meaning_reconstruction
multilayer_community_detection_enables_c.description: Multilayer community detection enables reconstruction of contextual meaning depending on psycholinguistic features, a capability not available in single-layer approaches. The method reveals how context modulates lexical meaning across layers.
multilayer_community_detection_enables_c.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

layer_analysis_mediates_latent_interacti.name: layer_analysis_mediates_latent_interactions_for_lexical_access
layer_analysis_mediates_latent_interacti.description: Layer analysis in multilayer lexical networks can mediate latent interactions of mediation, suppression, and facilitation relevant to lexical access. These interactions are only unveiled when multiple layers are combined.
layer_analysis_mediates_latent_interacti.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

quantitative_mechanisms_invisible_in_sin.name: quantitative_mechanisms_invisible_in_single_layer_networks_revealed_by_multilayer_approach
quantitative_mechanisms_invisible_in_sin.description: The review argues that combining multiple layers of the mental lexicon unveils quantitative mechanisms of psychological phenomena that cannot be observed in single-layer networks alone. This is positioned as the central empirical and theoretical contribution of the multilayer approach.
quantitative_mechanisms_invisible_in_sin.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

extension_of_multilayer_lexical_network_.name: extension_of_multilayer_lexical_network_findings_to_next_generation_brain_mind_models
extension_of_multilayer_lexical_network_.description: The paper outlines novel quantitative perspectives where multilayer networks can illuminate cognitive knowledge representations in next-generation brain and mind models. This integration with neuroscientific models remains an open aspiration.
extension_of_multilayer_lexical_network_.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

key_limitations_of_cognitive_multilayer_.name: key_limitations_of_cognitive_multilayer_network_approach
key_limitations_of_cognitive_multilayer_.description: The review explicitly outlines key limitations of the multilayer network framework as applied to the mental lexicon. These limitations define boundaries on current interpretability and generalizability.
key_limitations_of_cognitive_multilayer_.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

promising_future_directions_for_multilay.name: promising_future_directions_for_multilayer_lexical_network_research
promising_future_directions_for_multilay.description: The paper discusses cutting-edge future research directions where multilayer networks can further advance understanding of cognitive knowledge representations. These directions remain open for investigation.
promising_future_directions_for_multilay.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

gentle_introduction_to_multilayer_networ.name: gentle_introduction_to_multilayer_network_structure_and_formalism
gentle_introduction_to_multilayer_networ.description: The review provides an accessible introduction to the structure and formalism of multilayer networks as a foundation for psychological and cognitive science readers. This serves as a pedagogical contribution enabling broader adoption.
gentle_introduction_to_multilayer_networ.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

decades_of_evidence_for_multi_level_conc.name: decades_of_evidence_for_multi_level_conceptual_associations_in_mental_lexicon
decades_of_evidence_for_multi_level_conc.description: Psychological experiments over decades have demonstrated that conceptual associations across multiple interactive cognitive levels (semantic, phonological, syntactic, etc.) greatly influence word acquisition, storage, and processing. This motivates the multilayer approach.
decades_of_evidence_for_multi_level_conc.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

generalization_of_multilayer_lexical_fin.name: generalization_of_multilayer_lexical_findings_to_diverse_languages_and_populations
generalization_of_multilayer_lexical_fin.description: The review raises the open question of whether multilayer lexical network findings generalize beyond the datasets and languages currently studied. Broader replication across languages and populations is needed.
generalization_of_multilayer_lexical_fin.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

integration_of_syntactic_and_other_non_s.name: integration_of_syntactic_and_other_non_semantic_phonological_layers_into_multilayer_lexical_models
integration_of_syntactic_and_other_non_s.description: While the framework proposes including syntactic and other associative layers alongside semantic and phonological ones, the paper identifies this as an open challenge for future multilayer models of the mental lexicon.
integration_of_syntactic_and_other_non_s.paper: Cognitive modelling of concepts in the mental lexicon with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements, and future challenges

semantic_distance_as_a_metacognitive_cue.name: Semantic distance as a metacognitive cue for originality judgments
semantic_distance_as_a_metacognitive_cue.description: Semantic distance between a generated idea and the task stimulus contributes to originality judgments above and beyond previously known cues, functioning as a heuristic that biases self-assessments of idea originality.
semantic_distance_as_a_metacognitive_cue.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

originality_scores_and_originality_judgm.name: Originality scores and originality judgments produced by separate processes
originality_scores_and_originality_judgm.description: Initial evidence suggests that the actual frequency-based originality score and the self-assessed originality judgment are generated by distinct cognitive processes, making judgments prone to systematic biases.
originality_scores_and_originality_judgm.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

semantic_distance_biases_originality_jud.name: Semantic distance biases originality judgments gap
semantic_distance_biases_originality_jud.description: In Experiment 1, re-analysis of previous data showed that semantic distance contributed to the gap between objective originality scores and subjective originality judgments, suggesting it inflates or deflates self-assessments relative to actual originality.
semantic_distance_biases_originality_jud.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

instructional_priming_of_originality_and.name: Instructional priming of originality and semantic distance levels modulates bias
instructional_priming_of_originality_and.description: In Experiment 2, manipulating examples in task instructions to prime two levels of idea originality and two levels of semantic distance replicated the biasing role of semantic distance and revealed differences across conditions in the magnitude of the bias.
instructional_priming_of_originality_and.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

heuristic_cues_underlying_originality_ju.name: Heuristic cues underlying originality judgment biases largely unknown prior to this work
heuristic_cues_underlying_originality_ju.description: Before this study, the specific heuristic cues that cause originality judgments to deviate from actual originality scores were hardly known, representing a gap in metacognition and creativity research.
heuristic_cues_underlying_originality_ju.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

causal_account_of_how_semantic_distance_.name: Causal account of how semantic distance produces originality judgment bias
causal_account_of_how_semantic_distance_.description: While semantic distance is identified as a biasing heuristic cue, the mechanism by which it influences metacognitive originality judgments—e.g., via fluency, surprise, or associative processing—remains unspecified.
causal_account_of_how_semantic_distance_.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

generalization_of_semantic_distance_bias.name: Generalization of semantic distance bias finding beyond the current sample and stimuli
generalization_of_semantic_distance_bias.description: The two experiments rely on specific participant samples and task stimuli; whether the biasing effect of semantic distance on originality judgments generalizes to other populations, tasks, and creativity paradigms is untested.
generalization_of_semantic_distance_bias.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

practical_implications_of_semantic_dista.name: Practical implications of semantic distance bias for creativity assessment and investment decisions
practical_implications_of_semantic_dista.description: The paper frames originality judgments as consequential for decisions such as investing in companies or choosing research agendas, implying that understanding and correcting this bias has real-world value; these practical corrections remain unaddressed.
practical_implications_of_semantic_dista.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

whether_other_unacknowledged_heuristic_c.name: Whether other unacknowledged heuristic cues also bias originality judgments
whether_other_unacknowledged_heuristic_c.description: The study demonstrates semantic distance as one heuristic cue, but the broader landscape of metacognitive cues that drive biases between originality scores and judgments remains open.
whether_other_unacknowledged_heuristic_c.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

incremental_validity_of_semantic_distanc.name: Incremental validity of semantic distance over known cues for originality scores versus judgments
incremental_validity_of_semantic_distanc.description: Semantic distance was tested for additional explanatory value over previously known cues in predicting both objective originality scores and subjective originality judgments, establishing a differential pattern.
incremental_validity_of_semantic_distanc.paper: The Role of Semantic Associations as a Metacognitive Cue in Creative Idea Generation

context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_me.name: Context affects neural mechanisms for metaphor comprehension
context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_me.description: The study confirms that the context in which metaphors are encountered affects their neural processing, with narrative versus jabberwocky contexts producing distinct neural activation patterns for novel metaphors.
context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_me.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

indistinguishable_neural_correlates_of_m.name: Indistinguishable neural correlates of metaphor and literal sentences in narrative context
indistinguishable_neural_correlates_of_m.description: When embedded in a meaningful narrative, the neural correlates of processing novel metaphoric sentences and their literal counterparts are indistinguishable, both activating bilateral anterior temporal poles, middle temporal gyri, superior temporal sulci, and angular gyri.
indistinguishable_neural_correlates_of_m.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

sensorimotor_activation_for_narrative_em.name: Sensorimotor activation for narrative-embedded metaphors versus jabberwocky-embedded metaphors
sensorimotor_activation_for_narrative_em.description: Metaphors embedded in a narrative compared to identical metaphors in jabberwocky contexts show increased responses in sensorimotor areas corresponding to the modality of the literal meaning of the target word, suggesting deeper semantic processing.
sensorimotor_activation_for_narrative_em.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

appropriateness_of_metaphors_as_context_.name: Appropriateness of metaphors as context-dependent
appropriateness_of_metaphors_as_context_.description: While novelty is an inherent property of unfamiliar metaphors, appropriateness depends on the context in which they are encountered, making context a critical variable for understanding creative language.
appropriateness_of_metaphors_as_context_.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

generalization_of_context_effects_on_met.name: Generalization of context effects on metaphor processing to other creative language forms
generalization_of_context_effects_on_met.description: The current findings are specific to novel metaphors; whether context similarly modulates neural processing of other forms of creative or figurative language (e.g., idioms, similes, novel compounds) remains untested.
generalization_of_context_effects_on_met.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

mechanistic_account_of_why_narrative_con.name: Mechanistic account of why narrative context produces sensorimotor grounding during metaphor processing
mechanistic_account_of_why_narrative_con.description: The study observes increased sensorimotor activation for narrative-embedded metaphors but does not provide a mechanistic explanation for why supportive context specifically triggers modality-specific sensorimotor engagement.
mechanistic_account_of_why_narrative_con.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

replication_of_context_dependent_metapho.name: Replication of context-dependent metaphor processing findings in independent samples
replication_of_context_dependent_metapho.description: The neural findings on narrative versus jabberwocky metaphor processing are based on a single study and require replication in independent samples to establish robustness.
replication_of_context_dependent_metapho.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

jabberwocky_context_as_a_control_conditi.name: Jabberwocky context as a control condition for meaningful narrative
jabberwocky_context_as_a_control_conditi.description: The study uses matched jabberwocky contexts (semantically meaningless but syntactically structured sentences) as a control condition to isolate the effects of meaningful narrative context on metaphor processing.
jabberwocky_context_as_a_control_conditi.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

generalization_of_context_effects_on_met.name: Generalization of context effects on metaphor neural processing beyond current stimulus set and sample
generalization_of_context_effects_on_met.description: The current findings are based on a specific set of novel metaphors and a particular participant sample; it is unclear whether the results generalize to other metaphor types, languages, or populations.
generalization_of_context_effects_on_met.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

role_of_embodied_simulation_in_narrative.name: Role of embodied simulation in narrative-supported metaphor comprehension
role_of_embodied_simulation_in_narrative.description: The increased sensorimotor activation for narrative-embedded metaphors may reflect embodied simulation of the literal meaning of target words, suggesting that supportive context promotes grounded semantic processing.
role_of_embodied_simulation_in_narrative.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts

anodal_lpfc_stimulation_increases_approp.name: anodal_lpfc_stimulation_increases_appropriateness_and_decreases_novelty
anodal_lpfc_stimulation_increases_approp.description: Anodal (excitatory) transcranial direct current stimulation over the left prefrontal cortex increased the appropriateness and decreased the novelty of creative sentence completions, suggesting that enhanced cognitive control constrains novel idea generation.
anodal_lpfc_stimulation_increases_approp.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

cathodal_lpfc_stimulation_does_not_incre.name: cathodal_lpfc_stimulation_does_not_increase_novelty_in_constrained_sentence_completion
cathodal_lpfc_stimulation_does_not_incre.description: Unlike previous studies, cathodal (inhibitory) stimulation over the left prefrontal cortex did not significantly increase the novelty of responses, potentially due to the constrained nature of the sentence completion task used.
cathodal_lpfc_stimulation_does_not_incre.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

cognitive_control_as_inhibitor_of_novel_.name: cognitive_control_as_inhibitor_of_novel_idea_generation
cognitive_control_as_inhibitor_of_novel_.description: The study demonstrates that cognitive control mechanisms, as modulated by prefrontal tDCS, can inhibit novel idea generation, supporting theories that posit a negative relationship between cognitive control and creative ideation novelty.
cognitive_control_as_inhibitor_of_novel_.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

task_demand_modulation_of_cognitive_cont.name: task_demand_modulation_of_cognitive_control_effects_on_creativity
task_demand_modulation_of_cognitive_cont.description: The authors propose that the nature of the creative task (constrained vs. unconstrained) moderates whether reduced cognitive control facilitates novelty, explaining divergence from prior cathodal stimulation findings.
task_demand_modulation_of_cognitive_cont.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

replication_of_cathodal_lpfc_novelty_inc.name: replication_of_cathodal_lpfc_novelty_increase_across_task_types
replication_of_cathodal_lpfc_novelty_inc.description: Prior studies found that cathodal inhibition of the left prefrontal cortex increases creative novelty, but the current study did not replicate this; it remains open whether this discrepancy is reliably attributable to task demands or other factors.
replication_of_cathodal_lpfc_novelty_inc.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

sentence_completion_creative_ideation_ta.name: sentence_completion_creative_ideation_task_with_tDCS
sentence_completion_creative_ideation_ta.description: Participants read sentences with the final word missing and were instructed to produce uncommon but appropriate completions while undergoing anodal, cathodal, or sham tDCS over the left prefrontal cortex; responses were rated by independent raters for novelty and appropriateness.
sentence_completion_creative_ideation_ta.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

dual_role_of_cognitive_control_in_creati.name: dual_role_of_cognitive_control_in_creative_ideation_versus_evaluation
dual_role_of_cognitive_control_in_creati.description: Theories reviewed posit that cognitive control has a negative effect on creative idea generation but a positive effect on creative idea evaluation; the current findings on anodal stimulation partially support this dual-role account for the generation phase.
dual_role_of_cognitive_control_in_creati.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

generalization_of_lpfc_tdcs_novelty_find.name: generalization_of_lpfc_tDCS_novelty_findings_beyond_sentence_completion
generalization_of_lpfc_tdcs_novelty_find.description: The current findings are based on a specific constrained sentence completion task; it remains unclear whether the observed effects of anodal tDCS on novelty and appropriateness generalize to other creative ideation paradigms.
generalization_of_lpfc_tdcs_novelty_find.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

mechanism_by_which_reduced_cognitive_con.name: mechanism_by_which_reduced_cognitive_control_permits_weaker_associations
mechanism_by_which_reduced_cognitive_con.description: The paper proposes that decreased prefrontal cognitive control facilitates creative ideation by allowing access to less inhibited, weaker-related associations, thereby increasing novelty; the mechanism is invoked theoretically but not directly tested.
mechanism_by_which_reduced_cognitive_con.paper: Noninvasive brain stimulation to lateral prefrontal cortex alters the novelty of creative idea generation

nct_application_to_structural_brain_imag.name: NCT application to structural brain imaging for subclinical depression
nct_application_to_structural_brain_imag.description: Computational network control theory (NCT) was applied to diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) data from a large sample to examine how brain network controllability relates to individual differences in subclinical depression symptoms.
nct_application_to_structural_brain_imag.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

subclinical_depression_negatively_relate.name: Subclinical depression negatively related to integration ability in right anterior insula
subclinical_depression_negatively_relate.description: Higher integration ability in the right anterior insula was negatively related to subclinical depression, replicating and extending prior work on atypical DMN–ECN switching in depression.
subclinical_depression_negatively_relate.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

subclinical_depression_related_to_easy_t.name: Subclinical depression related to easy-to-reach state controllability in bilateral lingual and lateral occipital gyri
subclinical_depression_related_to_easy_t.description: Subclinical depression was associated with the ability to drive the brain into easy-to-reach neural states in bilateral lingual gyrus and lateral occipital gyrus, highlighting regions not previously well-characterized in depression.
subclinical_depression_related_to_easy_t.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

neurocognitive_mechanisms_of_rumination_.name: Neurocognitive mechanisms of rumination and impaired inhibition in depression remain unclear
neurocognitive_mechanisms_of_rumination_.description: Although rumination and impaired inhibition are considered core features of depression, the precise neurocognitive mechanisms that produce these atypical cognitive processes are not fully understood.
neurocognitive_mechanisms_of_rumination_.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

causal_account_of_how_network_controllab.name: Causal account of how network controllability produces depression symptoms
causal_account_of_how_network_controllab.description: The current work identifies associations between NCT metrics and subclinical depression but does not establish whether network controllability causally drives depression symptoms such as rumination and impaired inhibition.
causal_account_of_how_network_controllab.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

generalization_of_nct_depression_finding.name: Generalization of NCT depression findings beyond subclinical sample
generalization_of_nct_depression_finding.description: The sample examined comprises subclinical depression, raising the question of whether the identified NCT–depression relationships generalize to clinical depression populations.
generalization_of_nct_depression_finding.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

replication_of_nct_depression_findings_i.name: Replication of NCT depression findings in independent samples
replication_of_nct_depression_findings_i.description: The relationship between NCT metrics and subclinical depression is demonstrated in a single large sample; independent replication is needed to confirm the findings.
replication_of_nct_depression_findings_i.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

role_of_lingual_and_lateral_occipital_gy.name: Role of lingual and lateral occipital gyri in depression clarified via NCT
role_of_lingual_and_lateral_occipital_gy.description: NCT analysis reveals that bilateral lingual gyrus and lateral occipital gyrus play a role in driving the brain into easy-to-reach neural states associated with depression symptoms, highlighting regions less commonly studied in depression neuroscience.
role_of_lingual_and_lateral_occipital_gy.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

integration_of_nct_depression_findings_w.name: Integration of NCT depression findings with broader depression neuroscience
integration_of_nct_depression_findings_w.description: The paper calls for integrating the NCT-based findings about integration ability and easy-to-reach state controllability into broader theoretical frameworks of depression neuroscience, including accounts of DMN–ECN dysfunction.
integration_of_nct_depression_findings_w.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

extension_of_nct_depression_findings_to_.name: Extension of NCT depression findings to other depression-related cognitive processes beyond rumination and inhibition
extension_of_nct_depression_findings_to_.description: The study focuses on rumination and impaired inhibition as core depression features; whether NCT metrics also explain other cognitive and affective features of depression remains an open direction.
extension_of_nct_depression_findings_to_.paper: A computational network control theory analysis of depression symptoms

faster_and_further_phenomenology_as_key_.name: faster_and_further_phenomenology_as_key_explanandum
faster_and_further_phenomenology_as_key_.description: Higher creative individuals tend to produce ideas more quickly and with more novelty than lower creative individuals, a pattern the authors term 'faster-and-further phenomenology' that any adequate theory of creativity must explain.
faster_and_further_phenomenology_as_key_.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

associative_theory_limitation_in_explain.name: associative_theory_limitation_in_explaining_faster_and_further_phenomenology
associative_theory_limitation_in_explain.description: Although extensive research shows differences in semantic structure between lower and higher creative individuals, structural explanations alone are formally limited in their ability to account for the faster-and-further phenomenology.
associative_theory_limitation_in_explain.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

executive_theory_limitation_in_explainin.name: executive_theory_limitation_in_explaining_faster_and_further_phenomenology
executive_theory_limitation_in_explainin.description: Executive abilities correlate with creativity, but formal process models explaining how cognitive control contributes to faster-and-further phenomenology are lacking prior to this work.
executive_theory_limitation_in_explainin.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

entropy_modulation_theory_as_integrative.name: entropy_modulation_theory_as_integrative_framework
entropy_modulation_theory_as_integrative.description: Entropy modulation theory is introduced to integrate structure- and process-based creativity accounts by positing that higher and lower creative individuals differ in their executive modulation of entropy during cognitive search.
entropy_modulation_theory_as_integrative.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

entropy_modulation_as_mechanism_for_fast.name: entropy_modulation_as_mechanism_for_faster_and_further_phenomenology
entropy_modulation_as_mechanism_for_fast.description: The theory proposes that activation magnitude and variance independently enhance the entropy of target retrieval, increasing retrieval speed and producing ideas that are both faster and more novel.
entropy_modulation_as_mechanism_for_fast.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

entropy_modulation_as_explanation_for_ap.name: entropy_modulation_as_explanation_for_apparent_semantic_structure_differences
entropy_modulation_as_explanation_for_ap.description: The theory claims that observed differences in semantic memory structure between creativity groups can themselves be produced by an entropy-modulating retrieval process, rather than requiring structural differences as the primary cause.
entropy_modulation_as_explanation_for_ap.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

entropy_modulation_theory_mediating_cogn.name: entropy_modulation_theory_mediating_cognitive_flexibility_and_exploration_exploitation_trade_off
entropy_modulation_theory_mediating_cogn.description: Entropy modulation is proposed as the cognitive mechanism that tunes flexibility and mediates the exploration-exploitation trade-off during creative search.
entropy_modulation_theory_mediating_cogn.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

empirical_validation_of_entropy_modulati.name: empirical_validation_of_entropy_modulation_theory_predictions
empirical_validation_of_entropy_modulati.description: The paper introduces the theory based on broad existing evidence, but direct empirical tests specifically designed to validate the entropy modulation account and its quantitative predictions remain to be conducted.
empirical_validation_of_entropy_modulati.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

formal_process_model_of_how_entropy_modu.name: formal_process_model_of_how_entropy_modulation_produces_faster_and_further_phenomenology
formal_process_model_of_how_entropy_modu.description: Entropy modulation theory provides a formal process-level account of how executive modulation of cognitive entropy during retrieval produces the faster-and-further phenomenology, filling a gap left by prior accounts.
formal_process_model_of_how_entropy_modu.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

generalization_of_entropy_modulation_the.name: generalization_of_entropy_modulation_theory_beyond_verbal_creativity_paradigms
generalization_of_entropy_modulation_the.description: Whether the entropy modulation account generalizes beyond verbal divergent thinking and memory retrieval contexts to other creativity paradigms and cognitive domains is not established by the current paper.
generalization_of_entropy_modulation_the.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

replication_and_independent_testing_of_e.name: replication_and_independent_testing_of_entropy_modulation_theory
replication_and_independent_testing_of_e.description: The entropy modulation theory has not yet been subjected to independent replication or adversarial empirical testing by other research groups.
replication_and_independent_testing_of_e.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

integration_of_entropy_modulation_theory.name: integration_of_entropy_modulation_theory_with_existing_creativity_neuroscience
integration_of_entropy_modulation_theory.description: The paper does not fully specify how entropy modulation theory maps onto known neural substrates and network-level creativity findings, leaving integration with neuroscience accounts as a future challenge.
integration_of_entropy_modulation_theory.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

causal_direction_between_entropy_modulat.name: causal_direction_between_entropy_modulation_and_semantic_structure_differences
causal_direction_between_entropy_modulat.description: The theory proposes that entropy modulation produces apparent structural differences, but the causal direction between executive entropy modulation and semantic memory structure has not been empirically established.
causal_direction_between_entropy_modulat.paper: An entropy modulation theory of creative exploration.

combination_association_task_as_probe_fo.name: Combination Association Task as probe for remote associate convergent thinking
combination_association_task_as_probe_fo.description: The Combination Association Task (CAT) is used to probe whole-brain functional connectivity patterns related to connecting close and distant remote associates in a convergent creative thinking context. It operationalizes associative remoteness as a within-task variable.
combination_association_task_as_probe_fo.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_t.name: Connectome predictive modeling applied to CAT performance
connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_t.description: Connectome predictive modeling (CPM) is used to identify whole-brain functional connectivity patterns that predict individual differences in CAT performance, including overall performance and performance specifically tied to associative remoteness.
connectome_predictive_modeling_applied_t.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

dmn_functional_connectivity_predicts_ove.name: DMN functional connectivity predicts overall CAT performance
dmn_functional_connectivity_predicts_ove.description: Brain connectivity networks predicting overall CAT performance are dominated by contributions from the Default Mode Network, consistent with the associative processes required across all trials of the task.
dmn_functional_connectivity_predicts_ove.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

executive_control_dorsal_attention_and_s.name: Executive Control, Dorsal Attention, and Somatomotor networks predict associative remoteness in CAT
executive_control_dorsal_attention_and_s.description: The functional connectivity pattern specifically linked to higher associative remoteness in the CAT involves the Executive Control Network, Dorsal Attention Network, and Somatomotor networks, suggesting controlled processes become increasingly important as associative demands increase.
executive_control_dorsal_attention_and_s.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

shared_fc_patterns_between_cat_remotenes.name: Shared FC patterns between CAT remoteness and divergent thinking
shared_fc_patterns_between_cat_remotenes.description: The functional connectivity patterns related to the higher creative demands of the CAT (associative remoteness) show similarities with FC patterns previously found to predict divergent thinking performance, suggesting overlapping neural mechanisms for convergent and divergent remote thinking.
shared_fc_patterns_between_cat_remotenes.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

neural_mechanisms_of_combining_distant_a.name: Neural mechanisms of combining distant associates in creative thinking poorly understood
neural_mechanisms_of_combining_distant_a.description: Despite the recognized role of associative thinking in creativity, the neural mechanisms that allow individuals to combine distant associates in creative thinking tasks remain poorly understood prior to this work.
neural_mechanisms_of_combining_distant_a.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

causal_account_of_how_identified_fc_patt.name: Causal account of how identified FC patterns produce remote associative combination
causal_account_of_how_identified_fc_patt.description: The study establishes correlational FC predictors of CAT performance but does not establish a causal account of how specific connectivity patterns mechanistically produce the ability to combine remote associates.
causal_account_of_how_identified_fc_patt.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

generalization_of_cat_fc_findings_beyond.name: Generalization of CAT FC findings beyond the current sample
generalization_of_cat_fc_findings_beyond.description: The connectome predictive modeling findings are based on a single sample, and it remains open whether the identified FC patterns generalize to independent samples or diverse populations.
generalization_of_cat_fc_findings_beyond.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

extension_of_cat_findings_to_other_creat.name: Extension of CAT findings to other creativity paradigms beyond convergent thinking
extension_of_cat_findings_to_other_creat.description: The paper suggests that CAT-based FC patterns may offer insights into both convergent and divergent remote thinking, but direct extension and validation across broader creativity paradigms remains to be demonstrated.
extension_of_cat_findings_to_other_creat.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

role_of_somatomotor_network_in_remote_as.name: Role of Somatomotor network in remote associative combination for creativity
role_of_somatomotor_network_in_remote_as.description: The Somatomotor network contributes to the functional connectivity pattern predicting associative remoteness in the CAT, suggesting motor-related processes may play a role in higher-demand creative associative thinking.
role_of_somatomotor_network_in_remote_as.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

mechanistic_account_of_why_somatomotor_n.name: Mechanistic account of why somatomotor network contributes to remote associative combination
mechanistic_account_of_why_somatomotor_n.description: The functional role of the Somatomotor network in remote associative combination for creative thinking is not mechanistically explained; it is identified as a contributor but its specific cognitive role is unclear.
mechanistic_account_of_why_somatomotor_n.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

understanding_of_both_convergent_and_div.name: Understanding of both convergent and divergent remote thinking neural basis advanced
understanding_of_both_convergent_and_div.description: By identifying FC patterns common to both CAT remoteness and divergent thinking, the work potentially advances understanding of neural mechanisms shared across convergent and divergent creative thinking.
understanding_of_both_convergent_and_div.paper: Brain Connectivity-Based Prediction of Combining Remote Semantic Associates for Creative Thinking

akinator_game_as_mirror_of_human_questio.name: Akinator_game_as_mirror_of_human_question_asking
akinator_game_as_mirror_of_human_questio.description: The authors propose that the Akinator's question-asking process can be viewed as a reflection of how humans ask questions, making it a useful proxy for studying human question-asking behavior.
akinator_game_as_mirror_of_human_questio.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

exploratory_data_driven_analysis_of_akin.name: Exploratory_data_driven_analysis_of_Akinator_questions
exploratory_data_driven_analysis_of_akin.description: The paper conducts an exploratory data-driven analysis of questions asked by the Akinator online game, examining complete games and individual questions to identify strategies and patterns.
exploratory_data_driven_analysis_of_akin.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

gamification_characteristics_drive_quest.name: Gamification_characteristics_drive_question_specificity_and_type
gamification_characteristics_drive_quest.description: The authors find surprising aspects of question specificity and types that appear to be driven by gamification characteristics of the Akinator game rather than purely by information-theoretic optimality.
gamification_characteristics_drive_quest.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

coherent_topic_clusters_in_akinator_ques.name: Coherent_topic_clusters_in_Akinator_questions
coherent_topic_clusters_in_akinator_ques.description: Using topic modelling techniques, the study identified coherent topics that the Akinator draws upon when generating questions, and mapped similar questions into clusters.
coherent_topic_clusters_in_akinator_ques.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

commonalities_between_human_and_akinator.name: Commonalities_between_human_and_Akinator_question_asking_strategies
commonalities_between_human_and_akinator.description: The results highlight commonalities in question-asking strategies used by people and by the Akinator, suggesting the game encodes human-like inquiry patterns.
commonalities_between_human_and_akinator.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

little_known_about_cognitive_mechanisms_.name: Little_known_about_cognitive_mechanisms_of_informative_question_asking
little_known_about_cognitive_mechanisms_.description: The paper notes that little is known about the reasons people ask questions, what makes questions better than others, or what cognitive mechanisms enable asking informative questions.
little_known_about_cognitive_mechanisms_.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

mathematical_versus_gamification_strateg.name: Mathematical_versus_gamification_strategy_analysis_of_Akinator
mathematical_versus_gamification_strateg.description: The study examines a range of strategies for the Akinator's question-asking process, from mathematical algorithms (e.g., information-theoretic) to gamification-based considerations, applied to the game's question data.
mathematical_versus_gamification_strateg.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

validity_of_akinator_as_a_scientific_mod.name: Validity_of_Akinator_as_a_scientific_model_of_human_question_asking
validity_of_akinator_as_a_scientific_mod.description: Whether the Akinator game is a valid scientific model or proxy for human question-asking remains an open question requiring systematic validation against human behavioral data.
validity_of_akinator_as_a_scientific_mod.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

generalization_of_akinator_findings_to_o.name: Generalization_of_Akinator_findings_to_other_question_asking_contexts
generalization_of_akinator_findings_to_o.description: The findings are based on the specific Akinator game context; it is unclear whether the identified strategies and topic clusters generalize to other question-asking contexts such as education, diagnosis, or scientific inquiry.
generalization_of_akinator_findings_to_o.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

topic_modelling_as_method_for_mapping_qu.name: Topic_modelling_as_method_for_mapping_question_content
topic_modelling_as_method_for_mapping_qu.description: Topic modelling techniques were used to explore the thematic content of Akinator questions and to group similar questions into coherent clusters.
topic_modelling_as_method_for_mapping_qu.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

causal_account_of_why_gamification_shape.name: Causal_account_of_why_gamification_shapes_question_strategy
causal_account_of_why_gamification_shape.description: The paper identifies gamification as a driver of question specificity and type but does not establish a causal or mechanistic account of how gamification constraints produce the observed question patterns.
causal_account_of_why_gamification_shape.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

first_step_characterization_of_human_que.name: First_step_characterization_of_human_question_asking_via_online_game_data
first_step_characterization_of_human_que.description: The paper is framed as a first step toward investigating human question asking by leveraging a large-scale naturalistic dataset from an online game, providing an initial empirical characterization of question strategies and topics.
first_step_characterization_of_human_que.paper: A Mirror to Human Question Asking: Analyzing the Akinator Online Question Game

application_of_network_science_to_cognit.name: application_of_network_science_to_cognition_underrepresented_relative_to_brain
application_of_network_science_to_cognit.description: While network science has greatly advanced understanding of brain structure and function, its application to study cognition has been done to a much lesser extent, representing a gap in the field.
application_of_network_science_to_cognit.paper: Editors' Introduction to Networks of the Mind: How Can Network Science Elucidate Our Understanding of Cognition?

scope_of_collection_covering_memory_indi.name: scope_of_collection_covering_memory_individual_differences_communities_and_cultural_change
scope_of_collection_covering_memory_indi.description: The topic collection spans a wide range of research areas including generalist accounts of memory and encoding, individual differences, communities, and cultural and individual change, demonstrating the breadth of cognitive network science.
scope_of_collection_covering_memory_indi.paper: Editors' Introduction to Networks of the Mind: How Can Network Science Elucidate Our Understanding of Cognition?

fruitfulness_of_applying_network_science.name: fruitfulness_of_applying_network_science_to_cognition_across_diverse_research_areas
fruitfulness_of_applying_network_science.description: The editors assert that applying network science tools to cognition is fruitful across a wide scope of research areas, from memory to individual differences to cultural change.
fruitfulness_of_applying_network_science.paper: Editors' Introduction to Networks of the Mind: How Can Network Science Elucidate Our Understanding of Cognition?

cognitive_complexity_as_motivation_for_n.name: cognitive_complexity_as_motivation_for_network_science_application
cognitive_complexity_as_motivation_for_n.description: Thinking is characterized as complex, and existing psychological, cognitive, and neural methods have developed to study this complexity; network science is positioned as a quantitative complement to address this complexity.
cognitive_complexity_as_motivation_for_n.paper: Editors' Introduction to Networks of the Mind: How Can Network Science Elucidate Our Understanding of Cognition?

semnet_restructuring_associated_with_suc.name: SemNet restructuring associated with successful riddle solving
semnet_restructuring_associated_with_suc.description: Participants who successfully solved the riddle showed changes in semantic memory network metrics (restructuring) between pre- and post-solving phases, providing initial evidence that problem-related SemNet restructuring is associated with solution success.
semnet_restructuring_associated_with_suc.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

semnet_restructuring_associated_with_suc.name: SemNet restructuring associated with successful analogical transfer
semnet_restructuring_associated_with_suc.description: Restructuring of problem-related semantic memory networks during riddle solving was also associated with successful solving of an analogous riddle, suggesting restructuring supports analogical transfer.
semnet_restructuring_associated_with_suc.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

solution_relevant_and_semantically_remot.name: Solution-relevant and semantically remote concepts become more strongly related in solvers
solution_relevant_and_semantically_remot.description: In participants who successfully solved the riddle, both solution-relevant concepts and semantically remote concepts became more strongly related in the semantic memory network post-solving.
solution_relevant_and_semantically_remot.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

semantically_remote_concept_changes_inst.name: Semantically remote concept changes instrumental for active solving
semantically_remote_concept_changes_inst.description: Only changes in semantically remote concepts were instrumental in actively solving the riddle, whereas changes in solution-relevant concepts may reflect pre-exposure to the solution rather than active problem-solving.
semantically_remote_concept_changes_inst.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

network_science_methodology_for_pre_post.name: Network science methodology for pre-post SemNet estimation in problem-solving
network_science_methodology_for_pre_post.description: Semantic memory networks were estimated from participants before and after attempting to solve a riddle, allowing restructuring to be quantified as the difference in SemNet metrics across phases.
network_science_methodology_for_pre_post.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

abstract_problem_representation_as_mecha.name: Abstract problem representation as mechanism for analogical transfer
abstract_problem_representation_as_mecha.description: Restructuring is proposed to lead to an abstract problem-related representation that facilitates solving analogous problems, linking the cognitive mechanism of restructuring to analogical transfer success.
abstract_problem_representation_as_mecha.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

replication_of_semnet_restructuring_find.name: Replication of SemNet restructuring findings in independent samples
replication_of_semnet_restructuring_find.description: The current findings are described as initial evidence, implying replication in independent and larger samples is needed to confirm the association between SemNet restructuring and problem-solving or analogical transfer.
replication_of_semnet_restructuring_find.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

causal_account_of_how_semnet_restructuri.name: Causal account of how SemNet restructuring produces problem-solving success
causal_account_of_how_semnet_restructuri.description: While an association between restructuring and solving success is demonstrated, the causal direction and mechanistic account of how reorganization of semantic representations produces insight or solution generation remains unestablished.
causal_account_of_how_semnet_restructuri.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

generalization_of_semnet_restructuring_f.name: Generalization of SemNet restructuring findings beyond riddle paradigm
generalization_of_semnet_restructuring_f.description: The current work uses riddle-solving as the problem-solving paradigm; it remains open whether SemNet restructuring similarly supports other types of creative problem-solving beyond riddles.
generalization_of_semnet_restructuring_f.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

role_of_restructuring_in_broader_creativ.name: Role of restructuring in broader creative problem-solving frameworks
role_of_restructuring_in_broader_creativ.description: The paper situates restructuring as a problem-solving mechanism in daily life creative cognition, but integration of these SemNet findings with broader cognitive and neural models of creative problem-solving remains an open aspiration.
role_of_restructuring_in_broader_creativ.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

distinction_between_restructuring_as_act.name: Distinction between restructuring as active solving mechanism versus solution pre-exposure
distinction_between_restructuring_as_act.description: Changes in solution-relevant concepts may reflect pre-exposure to solution content rather than active problem-solving, while changes in remote concepts are more directly instrumental to active solving.
distinction_between_restructuring_as_act.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

optimal_semnet_metrics_for_quantifying_p.name: Optimal SemNet metrics for quantifying problem representation restructuring
optimal_semnet_metrics_for_quantifying_p.description: It is not yet established which specific graph-theoretic SemNet metrics are most sensitive or theoretically appropriate for capturing problem representation restructuring in different problem types.
optimal_semnet_metrics_for_quantifying_p.paper: Changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving and analogical transfer

semantic_network_mapping_of_beauty_and_w.name: Semantic network mapping of beauty and wellness concepts
semantic_network_mapping_of_beauty_and_w.description: The study estimated group-based semantic networks from free association responses to 47 words related to Beauty, Wellness, or Beauty+Wellness, mapping the conceptual structure of these domains across demographic groups.
semantic_network_mapping_of_beauty_and_w.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

core_associative_content_of_beauty_seman.name: Core associative content of beauty semantic network
core_associative_content_of_beauty_seman.description: Beauty was consistently associated with Elegance, Feminine, Gorgeous, Lovely, Sexy, and Stylish across participant groups, revealing a stable semantic core for the concept.
core_associative_content_of_beauty_seman.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

core_associative_content_of_wellness_sem.name: Core associative content of wellness semantic network
core_associative_content_of_wellness_sem.description: Wellness was consistently associated with Aerobics, Fitness, Health, Holistic, Lifestyle, Medical, Nutrition, and Thrive, revealing a stable semantic core oriented around health and lifestyle practices.
core_associative_content_of_wellness_sem.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

age_related_differences_in_beauty_and_we.name: Age-related differences in beauty and wellness semantic network connectivity and segregation
age_related_differences_in_beauty_and_we.description: Older generational cohorts (Generation X and Baby Boomers relative to Generation Z and Millennials) had semantic networks for beauty and wellness that were less connected and more segregated from each other.
age_related_differences_in_beauty_and_we.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

sex_differences_in_beauty_and_wellness_s.name: Sex differences in beauty and wellness semantic network organization
sex_differences_in_beauty_and_wellness_s.description: Women compared to men had more segregated and organized semantic network representations of Beauty and Wellness concepts.
sex_differences_in_beauty_and_wellness_s.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

unclear_relationship_between_beauty_and_.name: Unclear relationship between beauty and wellness as concepts
unclear_relationship_between_beauty_and_.description: Despite frequent use in common parlance, the precise meaning of beauty and wellness and their relationship to each other was unclear prior to this study, motivating the network science approach.
unclear_relationship_between_beauty_and_.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

generalization_of_beauty_and_wellness_se.name: Generalization of beauty and wellness semantic network findings beyond current sample
generalization_of_beauty_and_wellness_se.description: The findings on age and sex differences in beauty and wellness semantic networks are based on a specific sample of four generational cohorts; whether these patterns replicate in other cultural, linguistic, or demographic contexts remains unestablished.
generalization_of_beauty_and_wellness_se.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_age.name: Causal or mechanistic account of why age produces less connected beauty and wellness networks
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_age.description: The study documents that older cohorts have less connected and more segregated beauty and wellness semantic networks, but the cognitive or experiential mechanisms driving this age-related difference are not explained.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_age.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_wom.name: Causal or mechanistic account of why women have more segregated beauty and wellness networks than men
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_wom.description: The study finds that women have more segregated and organized concepts of Beauty and Wellness than men, but does not explain why this sex difference arises.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_wom.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

relationship_between_beauty_and_wellness.name: Relationship between beauty and wellness concepts across groups
relationship_between_beauty_and_wellness.description: The study examined how Beauty and Wellness semantic networks relate to each other and found varying degrees of connectivity and segregation between these two conceptual domains depending on age cohort and sex.
relationship_between_beauty_and_wellness.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

application_of_network_science_to_beauty.name: Application of network science to beauty and wellness semantic memory
application_of_network_science_to_beauty.description: This work is among the first to apply semantic network analysis methods to the domains of beauty and wellness, extending the reach of cognitive network science into consumer and wellness psychology.
application_of_network_science_to_beauty.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

practical_implications_of_beauty_and_wel.name: Practical implications of beauty and wellness semantic network findings for contemporary society
practical_implications_of_beauty_and_wel.description: The authors argue that in contemporary societies preoccupied with beauty and healthy lifestyles, the findings shed novel light on how people think about these concepts across generations and by sex.
practical_implications_of_beauty_and_wel.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

extension_of_semantic_network_findings_t.name: Extension of semantic network findings to non-creativity domains such as beauty and wellness
extension_of_semantic_network_findings_t.description: The study extends group-based semantic network analysis beyond creativity and language cognition to probe how laypeople conceptualize beauty and wellness, demonstrating broader applicability of the method.
extension_of_semantic_network_findings_t.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

understanding_of_within_domain_versus_cr.name: Understanding of within-domain versus cross-domain semantic organization of beauty and wellness
understanding_of_within_domain_versus_cr.description: It remains unclear the extent to which beauty and wellness are represented as integrated versus separate conceptual domains in semantic memory, and how this integration varies across groups.
understanding_of_within_domain_versus_cr.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

free_association_as_a_method_for_probing.name: Free association as a method for probing beauty and wellness semantic networks
free_association_as_a_method_for_probing.description: Free association responses to 47 words related to Beauty, Wellness, or Beauty+Wellness were used to estimate group-based semantic networks, enabling comparison across generational cohorts and sex.
free_association_as_a_method_for_probing.paper: Beauty and Wellness in the Semantic Memory of the Beholder

fan_effect_applied_to_divergent_thinking.name: fan_effect_applied_to_divergent_thinking
fan_effect_applied_to_divergent_thinking.description: The fan effect—whereby increasing knowledge about a concept leads to increasing interference from conceptually related information—is proposed as a mechanism by which semantic richness constrains creative idea quality during divergent thinking.
fan_effect_applied_to_divergent_thinking.paper: A Fan Effect for Creative Thought: Semantic Richness Facilitates Idea Quantity but Constrains Idea Quality

replication_of_cue_set_size_originality_.name: replication_of_cue_set_size_originality_fluency_trade_off_in_independent_sample
replication_of_cue_set_size_originality_.description: Although the trade-off is replicated across three experiments within the paper, independent replication by other labs with different populations and stimuli is still needed.
replication_of_cue_set_size_originality_.paper: A Fan Effect for Creative Thought: Semantic Richness Facilitates Idea Quantity but Constrains Idea Quality

modular_structure_prevalence_and_effects.name: Modular structure prevalence and effects in complex networks
modular_structure_prevalence_and_effects.description: Modularity—tightly-connected node groups with few interconnections—has been shown to affect evolution and stability of biological networks, scalability and efficiency of large-scale infrastructure, and development of economic and social systems.

analytical_framework_for_modularity_and_.name: Analytical framework for modularity and network vulnerability
analytical_framework_for_modularity_and_.description: Despite the importance of modularity, an analytical framework for understanding how modularity affects network vulnerability was identified as missing prior to this work.

multilayer_network_advances_as_theoretic.name: Multilayer network advances as theoretical foundation for modularity resilience study
multilayer_network_advances_as_theoretic.description: Recent advances in the understanding of multilayer networks are leveraged to develop a theoretical framework for systematically studying modularity and its effects on network vulnerability.

analytical_and_numerical_study_of_modula.name: Analytical and numerical study of modular network resilience under targeted attacks
analytical_and_numerical_study_of_modula.description: The paper studies, both analytically and numerically, the resilience of modular networks under attacks targeting high-betweenness interconnected nodes.

high_betweenness_of_interconnected_nodes.name: High betweenness of interconnected nodes as vulnerability driver
high_betweenness_of_interconnected_nodes.description: Nodes connecting modules (interconnected nodes) tend to exhibit high betweenness values and are therefore more exposed to failure or attack, making them critical vulnerabilities in modular networks.

feedback_between_structure_and_function_.name: Feedback between structure and function in modular networks
feedback_between_structure_and_function_.description: The model provides new understanding of the feedback between network structure and function in real-world systems, though the mechanistic account of this feedback is not fully elaborated in the abstract.

efficient_immunization_strategies_from_m.name: Efficient immunization strategies from modular resilience framework
efficient_immunization_strategies_from_m.description: One stated implication of the framework is the development of efficient immunization strategies for modular complex networks, but this application has not yet been demonstrated empirically.

design_of_robust_large_scale_infrastruct.name: Design of robust large-scale infrastructure using modular resilience theory
design_of_robust_large_scale_infrastruct.description: The framework is proposed to have implications for designing robust large-scale infrastructure, but practical design guidelines are not demonstrated in this work.

understanding_brain_function_via_modular.name: Understanding brain function via modular network resilience model
understanding_brain_function_via_modular.description: The paper proposes that its modular resilience framework has implications for understanding brain function, but this connection is stated as an aspiration rather than a demonstrated result.

generalization_of_modular_resilience_fra.name: Generalization of modular resilience framework beyond interconnected-node attacks
generalization_of_modular_resilience_fra.description: The current framework focuses on attacks on high-betweenness interconnected nodes; whether it generalizes to other attack or failure types (random failures, attacks on intra-module nodes) remains open.

associative_creativity_sparker_as_a_cogn.name: Associative Creativity Sparker as a cognitive network science tool for breaking creative impasse
associative_creativity_sparker_as_a_cogn.description: The ACS is an online tool that uses cognitive network science principles to suggest word recommendations that prime new creative ideas once a participant runs out of ideas in a creative generation task.
