Recommendations are directed toward or away from previous ideas and close or far from the target object, based on a conceptual space derived from participants' responses via online text analysis.
associative_creativity_sparker_as_a_cogn.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

location_of_word_recommendations_affects.name: Location of word recommendations affects fluency and originality of creative ideas
location_of_word_recommendations_affects.description: Across both Study 1 and Study 2, the direction and distance of word recommendations from previous ideas and the target object modulate the fluency and originality of subsequently generated ideas. This demonstrates that semantic positioning of cues within memory networks can break creative impasse.
location_of_word_recommendations_affects.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

differential_benefit_of_word_recommendat.name: Differential benefit of word recommendations for novice versus experienced writers
differential_benefit_of_word_recommendat.description: Study 2 examined 120 novice and 120 experienced writers and found that these groups benefit differently from the word recommendations provided by the ACS, suggesting that writing experience moderates the effectiveness of semantic cuing for creative ideation.
differential_benefit_of_word_recommendat.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

creative_block_as_rooted_in_semantic_mem.name: Creative block as rooted in semantic memory network entrapment
creative_block_as_rooted_in_semantic_mem.description: The paper frames creative block and writer's block as arising from participants becoming 'stuck' in a particular region of their semantic memory network, and positions semantic network navigation as the mechanism underlying creative impasse.
creative_block_as_rooted_in_semantic_mem.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

replication_of_acs_effects_in_independen.name: Replication of ACS effects in independent samples beyond the two reported studies
replication_of_acs_effects_in_independen.description: The current findings are based on two studies with modest sample sizes; replication in independent and more diverse samples is needed to establish the robustness of the semantic cuing effects on creative impasse.
replication_of_acs_effects_in_independen.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

generalization_of_acs_effects_beyond_alt.name: Generalization of ACS effects beyond alternative uses task to other creative generation paradigms
generalization_of_acs_effects_beyond_alt.description: The ACS was tested using an alternative uses task; it remains open whether the same semantic cuing effects on creative impasse would generalize to other creative ideation paradigms such as story generation, brainstorming, or divergent thinking tasks beyond object-based AUT.
generalization_of_acs_effects_beyond_alt.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

causal_mechanism_linking_semantic_networ.name: Causal mechanism linking semantic network position of recommendations to creative output quality
causal_mechanism_linking_semantic_networ.description: While the study shows that recommendation location affects fluency and originality, the precise cognitive or neural mechanism by which being pulled to a different region of semantic memory breaks impasse and generates higher-quality ideas is not established.
causal_mechanism_linking_semantic_networ.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

role_of_fluid_intelligence_in_moderating.name: Role of fluid intelligence in moderating ACS recommendation effects
role_of_fluid_intelligence_in_moderating.description: Study 1 included a fluid intelligence (Gf) task alongside the ACS, suggesting the study aimed to examine whether Gf moderates or interacts with the effectiveness of word recommendations, but whether this moderation is established is unclear from the abstract.
role_of_fluid_intelligence_in_moderating.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

practical_deployment_and_scalability_of_.name: Practical deployment and scalability of the ACS tool for real-world creative support
practical_deployment_and_scalability_of_.description: The paper presents ACS as an online tool intended to support creativity broadly, but questions remain about its scalability, usability outside laboratory contexts, and integration into real creative workflows such as professional writing.
practical_deployment_and_scalability_of_.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

optimal_direction_and_distance_parameter.name: Optimal direction and distance parameters for word recommendations to maximize creative output
optimal_direction_and_distance_parameter.description: The study examines recommendations toward or away from previous ideas and close or far from the target, but the optimal combination of these parameters for maximizing either fluency or originality across different individuals and tasks is not yet determined.
optimal_direction_and_distance_parameter.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

generalization_of_writing_experience_mod.name: Generalization of writing experience moderation findings beyond novice and experienced writer groups
generalization_of_writing_experience_mod.description: Study 2 examines novice versus experienced writers, but it remains open whether writing experience effects generalize to other forms of domain expertise or to populations defined by other types of creative experience.
generalization_of_writing_experience_mod.paper: Sparking creativity: Encouraging creative idea generation through automatically generated word recommendations

neural_correlates_of_semantic_relatednes.name: Neural correlates of semantic relatedness judgment vary with semantic distance
neural_correlates_of_semantic_relatednes.description: fMRI analysis revealed that brain regions where activity increased with semantic relatedness mainly overlapped with default, control, salience, semantic control, and multiple demand networks, with the default and semantic control networks showing increased involvement when evaluating more remote associations.
neural_correlates_of_semantic_relatednes.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

higher_creative_individuals_show_lower_n.name: Higher creative individuals show lower neural activity during remote relatedness judgments
higher_creative_individuals_show_lower_n.description: Higher creative people, who provided higher relatedness judgments on average, exhibited lower activity in default and semantic control network regions during the semantic relatedness task, possibly reflecting higher neural efficiency.
higher_creative_individuals_show_lower_n.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

higher_creative_individuals_rate_remote_.name: Higher creative individuals rate remote concepts as more related
higher_creative_individuals_rate_remote_.description: Higher creative individuals provided higher relatedness judgments on average for remotely related concept pairs, consistent with prior findings on associative flexibility in creativity.
higher_creative_individuals_rate_remote_.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

judging_remote_concepts_as_related_as_a_.name: Judging remote concepts as related as a cognitive mechanism underlying creativity
judging_remote_concepts_as_related_as_a_.description: The study argues that the tendency to judge remote concepts as related reflects a core cognitive mechanism of creativity, consistent with the associative theory, and that neural correlates of this mechanism have been identified.
judging_remote_concepts_as_related_as_a_.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

hd_eeg_with_connectome_modeling_predicts.name: HD-EEG with connectome modeling predicts individual creative behavior from resting-state FC
hd_eeg_with_connectome_modeling_predicts.description: Combining HD-EEG with connectome modeling successfully predicts individual creative behavior from resting-state functional connectivity, highlighting the importance of optimal activation interplay across networks in creative cognition.
hd_eeg_with_connectome_modeling_predicts.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

neural_efficiency_account_of_creativity_.name: Neural efficiency account of creativity in semantic processing
neural_efficiency_account_of_creativity_.description: The lower neural activity in default and semantic control networks observed in higher creative individuals during remote relatedness judgment is interpreted as reflecting higher neural efficiency, suggesting creative individuals process remote associations with less neural effort.
neural_efficiency_account_of_creativity_.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_def.name: Causal or mechanistic account of how default and semantic control network activity produces remote associative judgment
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_def.description: The study identifies correlational relationships between network activity and creative remote association judgment but does not establish the causal direction or mechanism by which these networks produce the creative associative tendency.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_def.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

replication_of_neural_correlates_of_sema.name: Replication of neural correlates of semantic relatedness and creativity finding in independent samples
replication_of_neural_correlates_of_sema.description: The current findings on neural correlates of semantic relatedness judgment and their relationship to creativity are based on a single sample of N=93, and independent replication has not been reported.
replication_of_neural_correlates_of_sema.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

generalization_of_neural_semantic_relate.name: Generalization of neural semantic relatedness creativity findings beyond the current sample and paradigm
generalization_of_neural_semantic_relate.description: The sample (N=93) and specific semantic relatedness judgment paradigm limit the generalizability of findings to other creativity paradigms, populations, and stimulus sets.
generalization_of_neural_semantic_relate.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

integration_of_hd_eeg_connectome_modelin.name: Integration of HD-EEG connectome modeling findings with fMRI semantic relatedness results
integration_of_hd_eeg_connectome_modelin.description: The study reports both fMRI semantic relatedness findings and HD-EEG connectome modeling results, but a fully integrated account of how resting-state connectivity and task-based semantic processing jointly support creativity remains to be developed.
integration_of_hd_eeg_connectome_modelin.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

optimal_activation_interplay_across_netw.name: Optimal activation interplay across networks as a requirement for creative cognition
optimal_activation_interplay_across_netw.description: The connectome modeling results highlight the importance of optimal activation interplay across networks in creative cognition, but the precise parameters and mechanisms of such interplay are not yet fully specified.
optimal_activation_interplay_across_netw.paper: Creative connections: the neural correlates of semantic relatedness are associated with creativity

unconventional_visual_stimuli_recruit_no.name: Unconventional visual stimuli recruit non-specialized hemisphere
unconventional_visual_stimuli_recruit_no.description: Using the split visual field paradigm, the study found that while conventional faces were better processed by the specialized right hemisphere, unconventional faces were better processed by the non-specialized left hemisphere.
unconventional_visual_stimuli_recruit_no.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

non_specialized_hemisphere_processing_of.name: Non-specialized hemisphere processing of unconventional stimuli relates to creative ability
non_specialized_hemisphere_processing_of.description: Only unconventional face processing by the non-specialized left hemisphere was significantly related to both verbal and visual measures of creative ability; conventional face processing by the specialized hemisphere did not show this relationship.
non_specialized_hemisphere_processing_of.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

verbal_creativity_relates_to_unconventio.name: Verbal creativity relates to unconventional Mooney face recognition
verbal_creativity_relates_to_unconventio.description: In Experiment 1, measures of verbal creativity showed a significant relationship with unconventional Mooney face recognition performance, linking this perceptual ability to creative cognition.
verbal_creativity_relates_to_unconventio.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

generalization_of_non_specialized_hemisp.name: Generalization of non-specialized hemisphere creativity finding beyond face stimuli
generalization_of_non_specialized_hemisp.description: The current work focuses on face stimuli (Mooney faces and conventional/unconventional face photographs); it remains open whether the role of the non-specialized hemisphere in processing unconventional stimuli and its creativity link generalizes to other stimulus categories.
generalization_of_non_specialized_hemisp.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

replication_of_non_specialized_hemispher.name: Replication of non-specialized hemisphere creativity finding in independent samples
replication_of_non_specialized_hemispher.description: The hemispheric processing and creativity findings are based on a single study sample; independent replication is needed to confirm robustness of the effect.
replication_of_non_specialized_hemispher.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

mechanistic_account_of_why_non_specializ.name: Mechanistic account of why non-specialized hemisphere processing supports creativity
mechanistic_account_of_why_non_specializ.description: The study demonstrates that non-specialized hemisphere processing of unconventional stimuli relates to creativity, but the cognitive or neural mechanism by which this relationship arises is not yet explained.
mechanistic_account_of_why_non_specializ.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

objects_silhouette_recognition_as_unconv.name: Objects silhouette recognition as unconventional visual task
objects_silhouette_recognition_as_unconv.description: Experiment 1 included an objects' silhouette recognition task alongside Mooney face recognition as a probe for unconventional visual processing; this task did not show the same significant relationship with creativity as Mooney face recognition.
objects_silhouette_recognition_as_unconv.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

split_visual_field_paradigm_for_hemisphe.name: Split visual field paradigm for hemispheric processing of conventional and unconventional faces
split_visual_field_paradigm_for_hemisphe.description: Experiment 2 employed the split visual field paradigm to present conventional and unconventional face stimuli to each visual hemifield separately, enabling assessment of hemispheric processing differences.
split_visual_field_paradigm_for_hemisphe.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

right_hemisphere_specialization_for_conv.name: Right hemisphere specialization for conventional face processing
right_hemisphere_specialization_for_conv.description: Consistent with established literature, the study found that conventional faces were better processed by the right hemisphere, confirming the right hemisphere's specialized role in face recognition.
right_hemisphere_specialization_for_conv.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

generalization_of_hemispheric_unconventi.name: Generalization of hemispheric unconventional processing finding to other modalities or creative domains
generalization_of_hemispheric_unconventi.description: Whether the relationship between non-specialized hemisphere engagement and creativity extends to non-visual modalities or to different creative domains beyond verbal and visual creativity remains unaddressed.
generalization_of_hemispheric_unconventi.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

causal_direction_between_non_specialized.name: Causal direction between non-specialized hemisphere engagement and creative ability
causal_direction_between_non_specialized.description: The correlational design of both experiments cannot establish whether non-specialized hemisphere engagement in processing unconventional stimuli causes higher creativity or whether creative individuals simply have a pre-existing tendency to recruit non-specialized hemispheric resources.
causal_direction_between_non_specialized.paper: Processing of unconventional stimuli requires the recruitment of the non-specialized hemisphere

semantic_network_restructuring_as_mechan.name: Semantic network restructuring as mechanism underlying problem-solving
semantic_network_restructuring_as_mechan.description: The study proposes and tests restructuration—reinterpretation and reorganization of problem-related representations—as a core mechanism underlying problem-solving, operationalized via changes in semantic memory network structure.
semantic_network_restructuring_as_mechan.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

local_restructuration_predicts_problem_s.name: Local restructuration predicts problem-solving success
local_restructuration_predicts_problem_s.description: Successful problem-solving was predicted specifically by local changes in semantic network structure—only in edges and nodes judged as helpful to solving the riddle—rather than global network changes.
local_restructuration_predicts_problem_s.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

scarcity_of_empirical_evidence_for_restr.name: Scarcity of empirical evidence for restructuration in problem-solving prior to this work
scarcity_of_empirical_evidence_for_restr.description: Despite restructuration being theoretically considered a key mechanism of problem-solving, the authors note that empirical evidence supporting it was scarce before this study.
scarcity_of_empirical_evidence_for_restr.paper: Dynamic changes in semantic memory structure support successful problem-solving

cyber_creativity_as_a_new_field_of_human.name: Cyber-creativity as a new field of human-AI creative collaboration
cyber_creativity_as_a_new_field_of_human.description: The paper introduces 'cyber-creativity' as a unifying term for all forms of human–AI collaboration in the creative domain, framing it as a distinct research area requiring its own theoretical and empirical foundation.
cyber_creativity_as_a_new_field_of_human.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

utopian_versus_dystopian_framing_of_ai_i.name: Utopian versus dystopian framing of AI in creativity
utopian_versus_dystopian_framing_of_ai_i.description: The paper introduces two forward-looking scenarios—a utopian vision where AI enhances human creativity and a dystopian vision where AI pre-empts human creative agency—as conceptual anchors for the research agenda.
utopian_versus_dystopian_framing_of_ai_i.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

decalogue_of_research_challenges_for_cyb.name: Decalogue of research challenges for cyber-creativity
decalogue_of_research_challenges_for_cyb.description: The paper presents ten research dimensions each with a main challenge and associated research questions, intended as a starting point for urgent future research on human–AI creativity.
decalogue_of_research_challenges_for_cyb.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

theoretical_framework_for_cyber_creativi.name: Theoretical framework for cyber-creativity
theoretical_framework_for_cyber_creativi.description: A coherent theoretical framework for cyber-creativity that accounts for how AI and human cognition interact in creative processes does not yet exist and is identified as the first research challenge.
theoretical_framework_for_cyber_creativi.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

sociocultural_perspectives_on_cyber_crea.name: Sociocultural perspectives on cyber-creativity
sociocultural_perspectives_on_cyber_crea.description: How sociocultural contexts shape and are shaped by human–AI creative collaboration is identified as an underexplored dimension requiring dedicated research.
sociocultural_perspectives_on_cyber_crea.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

characterization_of_the_cyber_creative_p.name: Characterization of the cyber-creative process
characterization_of_the_cyber_creative_p.description: The cognitive and computational processes involved when humans and AI co-create remain poorly understood and constitute a core research challenge.
characterization_of_the_cyber_creative_p.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

understanding_the_creative_agent_in_cybe.name: Understanding the creative agent in cyber-creativity
understanding_the_creative_agent_in_cybe.description: What constitutes a creative agent in human–AI collaboration—including questions of agency, intentionality, and authorship—is unresolved and identified as a research challenge.
understanding_the_creative_agent_in_cybe.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

co_creative_team_dynamics_in_human_ai_co.name: Co-creative team dynamics in human-AI collaboration
co_creative_team_dynamics_in_human_ai_co.description: How co-creative teams composed of humans and AI systems function, distribute roles, and produce creative outcomes is an open research challenge.
co_creative_team_dynamics_in_human_ai_co.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

evaluation_of_cyber_creative_products.name: Evaluation of cyber-creative products
evaluation_of_cyber_creative_products.description: Criteria and methods for evaluating the novelty, quality, and value of products co-created by humans and AI are not yet established.
evaluation_of_cyber_creative_products.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

mapping_cyber_creative_domains.name: Mapping cyber-creative domains
mapping_cyber_creative_domains.description: The extent to which cyber-creativity applies across different creative domains (e.g., visual arts, music, literature, science) and whether domain-specific dynamics differ is an open challenge.
mapping_cyber_creative_domains.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

cyber_creative_education.name: Cyber-creative education
cyber_creative_education.description: How to educate individuals to effectively and responsibly collaborate with AI in creative endeavors is identified as an unresolved research challenge requiring new pedagogical frameworks.
cyber_creative_education.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

ethical_aspects_of_cyber_creativity.name: Ethical aspects of cyber-creativity
ethical_aspects_of_cyber_creativity.description: Unresolved ethical questions surround cyber-creativity, including issues of authorship, intellectual property, bias, and accountability in human–AI creative collaboration.
ethical_aspects_of_cyber_creativity.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

dark_side_of_cyber_creativity.name: Dark side of cyber-creativity
dark_side_of_cyber_creativity.description: Potential harms of cyber-creativity—such as misuse of AI-generated content, deception, deepfakes, and erosion of human creative agency—are identified as an urgent research challenge.
dark_side_of_cyber_creativity.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

risk_of_ai_pre_empting_human_creative_ag.name: Risk of AI pre-empting human creative agency
risk_of_ai_pre_empting_human_creative_ag.description: The paper identifies a dystopian scenario in which AI systems replace rather than augment human creativity, eroding human creative agency, as a risk to be avoided through scientific and societal effort.
risk_of_ai_pre_empting_human_creative_ag.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

maximizing_benefits_while_minimizing_ris.name: Maximizing benefits while minimizing risks of AI in creativity
maximizing_benefits_while_minimizing_ris.description: The overarching goal articulated by the paper is to identify how humanity can maximize the benefits of AI for creativity while minimizing associated risks, with the decalogue serving as a research roadmap toward this goal.
maximizing_benefits_while_minimizing_ris.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

alignment_of_ai_to_human_values_in_the_c.name: Alignment of AI to human values in the creative realm
alignment_of_ai_to_human_values_in_the_c.description: The paper frames the ultimate ambition of the research agenda as contributing to the alignment of AI systems to human values specifically within the domain of creativity.
alignment_of_ai_to_human_values_in_the_c.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

state_of_the_art_review_across_ten_cyber.name: State-of-the-art review across ten cyber-creativity dimensions
state_of_the_art_review_across_ten_cyber.description: For each of the ten research challenge dimensions, the paper provides a brief review of existing literature, establishing the current knowledge base before identifying open challenges.
state_of_the_art_review_across_ten_cyber.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

non_exhaustiveness_of_the_decalogue_as_a.name: Non-exhaustiveness of the decalogue as a limitation
non_exhaustiveness_of_the_decalogue_as_a.description: The authors explicitly acknowledge that the decalogue does not represent an exhaustive classification of research challenges for cyber-creativity, which limits its completeness as a research roadmap.
non_exhaustiveness_of_the_decalogue_as_a.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

urgency_of_cyber_creativity_research_giv.name: Urgency of cyber-creativity research given AI advancement pace
urgency_of_cyber_creativity_research_giv.description: The paper frames the challenge of cyber-creativity as urgent given the astonishing pace of AI development, implying that the scientific community must act quickly before dystopian outcomes become entrenched.
urgency_of_cyber_creativity_research_giv.paper: Cyber-Creativity: A Decalogue of Research Challenges

high_knowledge_students_show_more_cluste.name: High-knowledge students show more clustered and shorter-path semantic memory networks
high_knowledge_students_show_more_cluste.description: Study 1 (N=213) found that students who scored higher on the Intro Psych Test had semantic memory networks with higher clustering and shorter distances between concepts, across both domain-specific (psychology) and domain-general (animal) fluency tasks.
high_knowledge_students_show_more_cluste.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

longitudinal_replication_and_extension_o.name: Longitudinal replication and extension of knowledge-network structure finding
longitudinal_replication_and_extension_o.description: Study 2 (N=145) replicated the Study 1 finding and additionally showed that high-knowledge students' semantic networks became more interconnected over the course of the semester, across both domain-general and domain-specific categories.
longitudinal_replication_and_extension_o.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

domain_general_semantic_network_structur.name: Domain-general semantic network structure differentiates knowledge groups
domain_general_semantic_network_structur.description: The semantic memory network advantage of high-knowledge students extended beyond the domain-specific psychology category to the domain-general animal fluency task, suggesting the effect is not limited to acquired course content.
domain_general_semantic_network_structur.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

cognitive_network_science_as_a_tool_for_.name: Cognitive network science as a tool for studying student learning variation
cognitive_network_science_as_a_tool_for_.description: The paper demonstrates the utility of semantic network analysis—via verbal fluency tasks—as a method for capturing how knowledge is organized in students, going beyond what standard multiple-choice assessments can reveal.
cognitive_network_science_as_a_tool_for_.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

standard_learning_assessments_do_not_cap.name: Standard learning assessments do not capture knowledge organization
standard_learning_assessments_do_not_cap.description: Multiple-choice tests measure what students know but not how their knowledge is structured, leaving a gap in understanding learning quality that network-based tools can address.
standard_learning_assessments_do_not_cap.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_hig.name: Causal or mechanistic account of why high-knowledge students have more connected networks
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_hig.description: The study establishes a correlation between knowledge level and semantic network structure but does not explain the mechanism by which successful learning produces denser, more clustered networks or whether network structure facilitates learning.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_hig.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

generalization_of_knowledge_network_stru.name: Generalization of knowledge-network structure findings beyond undergraduate psychology students
generalization_of_knowledge_network_stru.description: The findings are based on undergraduates in an introductory psychology course; it remains open whether the same pattern holds for other student populations, disciplines, or age groups.
generalization_of_knowledge_network_stru.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

generalization_of_knowledge_network_stru.name: Generalization of knowledge-network structure finding beyond verbal fluency estimation
generalization_of_knowledge_network_stru.description: Semantic networks were estimated via verbal fluency tasks; whether the same structural differences emerge using other network estimation methods (e.g., free association, semantic relatedness judgments) remains untested.
generalization_of_knowledge_network_stru.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

practical_educational_applications_of_se.name: Practical educational applications of semantic network analysis for learning assessment
practical_educational_applications_of_se.description: The paper highlights that cognitive network science tools could augment or complement traditional assessments in educational settings, but practical deployment and integration into curricula remain unrealized.
practical_educational_applications_of_se.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

longitudinal_trajectory_of_semantic_netw.name: Longitudinal trajectory of semantic network change across the full learning arc
longitudinal_trajectory_of_semantic_netw.description: Data were collected near the start and end of a semester, but finer-grained longitudinal tracking of how semantic network structure evolves throughout the course of learning is not addressed.
longitudinal_trajectory_of_semantic_netw.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

whether_semantic_network_structure_predi.name: Whether semantic network structure predicts future learning outcomes prospectively
whether_semantic_network_structure_predi.description: The study groups students by end-of-course test performance, but it is unclear whether baseline network structure at the start of a course predicts subsequent learning gains prospectively.
whether_semantic_network_structure_predi.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

domain_specificity_versus_domain_general.name: Domain-specificity versus domain-generality of the learning-network structure relationship
domain_specificity_versus_domain_general.description: Both domain-specific and domain-general networks differed between knowledge groups, raising the unresolved question of whether successful learning primarily reorganizes domain-specific knowledge structures or induces broader changes in general semantic organization.
domain_specificity_versus_domain_general.paper: Mapping the Memory Structure of High-Knowledge Students: A Longitudinal Semantic Network Analysis

alpha_power_higher_during_generation_of_.name: alpha_power_higher_during_generation_of_more_distant_semantic_associations
alpha_power_higher_during_generation_of_.description: Across two experiments, alpha oscillatory activity was consistently higher during the generation of more semantically distant (remote) associations compared to less distant ones, with effects widespread across both hemispheres.
alpha_power_higher_during_generation_of_.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

creative_instruction_increases_alpha_pha.name: creative_instruction_increases_alpha_phase_synchronisation_from_left_to_right_temporal_areas
creative_instruction_increases_alpha_pha.description: When participants were instructed to be creative (goal-directed associations) versus freely associating, alpha phase synchronisation increased from left to right temporal brain areas, suggesting altered information flux reflecting top-down control of semantic search.
creative_instruction_increases_alpha_pha.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

goal_directed_remote_association_relies_.name: goal_directed_remote_association_relies_on_top_down_mechanisms_more_than_free_association
goal_directed_remote_association_relies_.description: The paper concludes that generating remote associations under a creative instruction relies more heavily on top-down mechanisms compared to freely generated remote associations, based on differential alpha phase synchronisation.
goal_directed_remote_association_relies_.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_alp.name: causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_alpha_phase_synchronisation_produces_top_down_semantic_search_control
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_alp.description: While the study observes increased left-to-right temporal alpha synchronisation under creative instruction, the precise mechanism by which this synchronisation implements top-down control of semantic search processes remains unspecified.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_alp.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

replication_of_alpha_remote_association_.name: replication_of_alpha_remote_association_finding_in_independent_samples
replication_of_alpha_remote_association_.description: The alpha power increase during remote association generation was replicated across two experiments within the same paper, but replication in fully independent samples with different populations or paradigms has not been demonstrated.
replication_of_alpha_remote_association_.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

generalization_of_alpha_remote_associati.name: generalization_of_alpha_remote_association_finding_beyond_verbal_semantic_paradigms
generalization_of_alpha_remote_associati.description: The current findings are based on verbal free and goal-directed association tasks; whether alpha oscillations play a similar role in remote associations across other modalities or creative domains remains unknown.
generalization_of_alpha_remote_associati.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

distinction_between_free_and_goal_direct.name: distinction_between_free_and_goal_directed_association_at_neural_level
distinction_between_free_and_goal_direct.description: The study provides neural evidence distinguishing free associations from goal-directed (creative instruction) associations, showing that the two conditions differ in alpha phase synchronisation patterns even when both involve generating remote associations.
distinction_between_free_and_goal_direct.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

role_of_alpha_oscillations_in_selective_.name: role_of_alpha_oscillations_in_selective_attention_working_memory_and_semantic_memory
role_of_alpha_oscillations_in_selective_.description: Prior to this work, alpha oscillations were already known to play a central role in selective attention, working memory, semantic memory, and creative thinking, providing the theoretical backdrop for the current investigation.
role_of_alpha_oscillations_in_selective_.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

gap_in_understanding_alpha_role_in_remot.name: gap_in_understanding_alpha_role_in_remote_semantic_association_generation
gap_in_understanding_alpha_role_in_remot.description: Despite broad knowledge of alpha's role in higher-order cognition, little was known before this work about how alpha oscillations support the generation of more remote semantic associations specifically.
gap_in_understanding_alpha_role_in_remot.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

gap_in_understanding_how_creative_intent.name: gap_in_understanding_how_creative_intention_shapes_alpha_oscillations
gap_in_understanding_how_creative_intent.description: It was previously unclear how alpha oscillations are shaped by the explicit intention to be creative, as most creativity tasks involve such instructions but this effect had not been isolated.
gap_in_understanding_how_creative_intent.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

mechanistic_account_of_why_alpha_increas.name: mechanistic_account_of_why_alpha_increases_during_more_remote_association_generation
mechanistic_account_of_why_alpha_increas.description: The study establishes that alpha is higher for more remote associations but does not fully explain the mechanistic reason—whether alpha reflects inhibition of distractors, gating of irrelevant information, or some other process enabling access to distant semantic nodes.
mechanistic_account_of_why_alpha_increas.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

appropriate_method_for_distinguishing_ge.name: appropriate_method_for_distinguishing_genuine_alpha_oscillations_from_transient_events
appropriate_method_for_distinguishing_ge.description: The study employed a specialized analytical approach to separate genuine sustained alpha oscillatory activity from transient events, improving the validity of the alpha power comparisons across conditions.
appropriate_method_for_distinguishing_ge.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

bilateral_hemispheric_involvement_in_alp.name: bilateral_hemispheric_involvement_in_alpha_during_remote_association
bilateral_hemispheric_involvement_in_alp.description: The alpha increase during more distant semantic association generation was widespread, involving areas in both left and right hemispheres, rather than being lateralized to one hemisphere.
bilateral_hemispheric_involvement_in_alp.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

generalization_of_alpha_synchronisation_.name: generalization_of_alpha_synchronisation_creative_instruction_finding_beyond_current_sample_and_paradigm
generalization_of_alpha_synchronisation_.description: Whether the observed increase in left-to-right temporal alpha synchronisation under creative instruction generalises to other creativity paradigms, populations, or linguistic contexts is unknown.
generalization_of_alpha_synchronisation_.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

integration_of_alpha_oscillation_finding.name: integration_of_alpha_oscillation_findings_with_semantic_network_and_associative_theories_of_creativity
integration_of_alpha_oscillation_finding.description: The current findings on alpha oscillations and remote semantic associations could be integrated with cognitive network science accounts of creativity, but this theoretical synthesis has not yet been fully articulated.
integration_of_alpha_oscillation_finding.paper: The role of alpha oscillations in free‐ and goal‐directed semantic associations

extension_of_network_science_methodology.name: Extension of network science methodology to melodic patterns in improvised music
extension_of_network_science_methodology.description: The authors constructed a network model of melodic patterns from a large corpus of expert jazz improvisations, using 5-pitch sequences as nodes linked by consecutive occurrences, yielding 116,403 nodes and 157,429 edges.
extension_of_network_science_methodology.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

melodic_network_distance_predicts_relate.name: Melodic network distance predicts relatedness judgments
melodic_network_distance_predicts_relate.description: As melodic distance within the network increased, participants judged melodic sequences as less related, demonstrating human sensitivity to melodic network structure.
melodic_network_distance_predicts_relate.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

quadratic_relationship_between_melodic_n.name: Quadratic relationship between melodic network distance and reaction time
quadratic_relationship_between_melodic_n.description: The relationship between melodic distance and reaction time was quadratic: participants slowed in RT up to distance four, then accelerated, mirroring a parallel finding in language network research.
quadratic_relationship_between_melodic_n.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

similarity_between_music_and_language_as.name: Similarity between music and language as complex network systems
similarity_between_music_and_language_as.description: The authors argue that music and language show structural and behavioral parallels when modeled as networks, with melodic distance effects mirroring those found in lexical/semantic networks.
similarity_between_music_and_language_as.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

human_sensitivity_to_melodic_network_dis.name: Human sensitivity to melodic network distance as a perceptual property
human_sensitivity_to_melodic_network_dis.description: Participants were sensitive to the property of melodic distance in the improvised music network, suggesting that the hidden network structure of music is perceptually meaningful.
human_sensitivity_to_melodic_network_dis.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

incomplete_understanding_of_the_organiza.name: Incomplete understanding of the organizational structure of music as a complex system
incomplete_understanding_of_the_organiza.description: The authors note that music consists of many dimensions and hierarchically organized information whose organization is not yet fully understood, motivating the application of network science.
incomplete_understanding_of_the_organiza.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

generalization_of_melodic_network_findin.name: Generalization of melodic network findings beyond expert jazz improvisation
generalization_of_melodic_network_findin.description: The network was built from a corpus of expert improvised music; it is unclear whether the findings generalize to other musical styles, genres, or non-expert music.
generalization_of_melodic_network_findin.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

generalization_of_melodic_network_behavi.name: Generalization of melodic network behavioral findings beyond the current sample
generalization_of_melodic_network_behavi.description: The behavioral experiment was conducted with a specific participant sample; whether results replicate in independent or more diverse samples is untested.
generalization_of_melodic_network_behavi.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_mel.name: Causal or mechanistic account of why melodic network distance shapes perception
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_mel.description: The study demonstrates that melodic network distance predicts relatedness judgments and RT, but the cognitive or neural mechanisms by which listeners are sensitive to this network property remain unspecified.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_mel.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

extension_of_melodic_network_model_to_ot.name: Extension of melodic network model to other musical dimensions beyond pitch sequences
extension_of_melodic_network_model_to_ot.description: The current model focuses on 5-pitch melodic sequences; incorporating rhythm, harmony, timbre, or hierarchical structure remains a direction for future work.
extension_of_melodic_network_model_to_ot.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

corpus_based_melodic_network_as_an_empir.name: Corpus-based melodic network as an empirical resource for music cognition research
corpus_based_melodic_network_as_an_empir.description: The large-scale network of 116,403 nodes and 157,429 edges constructed from transcribed jazz improvisations constitutes a quantitative resource for studying melodic structure.
corpus_based_melodic_network_as_an_empir.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

behavioral_paradigm_for_probing_melodic_.name: Behavioral paradigm for probing melodic network structure
behavioral_paradigm_for_probing_melodic_.description: A behavioral experiment paralleling those used in language network research was designed to test whether melodic network distance relates to human relatedness judgments and reaction times.
behavioral_paradigm_for_probing_melodic_.paper: Representing melodic relationships using network science.

aesthetic_emotions_are_context_specific.name: Aesthetic emotions are context-specific
aesthetic_emotions_are_context_specific.description: The study found that aesthetic, non-aesthetic, and consumer activities evoke different emotional profiles and network structures, supporting the context-specificity of aesthetic emotions rather than their universality across all engaging activities.
aesthetic_emotions_are_context_specific.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

recalled_aesthetic_activities_rated_leas.name: Recalled aesthetic activities rated least vivid but most intense
recalled_aesthetic_activities_rated_leas.description: At the behavioral level, participants recalled aesthetic activities as less vivid than non-aesthetic or consumer activities, yet rated them as more emotionally intense.
recalled_aesthetic_activities_rated_leas.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

aesthetic_emotions_distributed_different.name: Aesthetic emotions distributed differently in multidimensional aesthetic space
aesthetic_emotions_distributed_different.description: The authors propose that aesthetic emotions occupy a distinct region of multidimensional emotional space compared to non-aesthetic engaging activities, based on observed network differences.
aesthetic_emotions_distributed_different.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

whether_aesthetic_emotions_are_unique_to.name: Whether aesthetic emotions are unique to aesthetic activities
whether_aesthetic_emotions_are_unique_to.description: The debate over whether aesthetic emotions are exclusive to aesthetic contexts or common to all engaging activities remains a central open question that the paper addresses but does not fully resolve.
whether_aesthetic_emotions_are_unique_to.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

psychometric_network_analysis_of_aesthet.name: Psychometric network analysis of aesthetic emotion space
psychometric_network_analysis_of_aesthet.description: A computational psychometric network approach was applied to represent the co-occurrence and relationships among 75 candidate aesthetic emotions recalled across three activity conditions, enabling comparison of emotional network structure.
psychometric_network_analysis_of_aesthet.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

activity_recall_paradigm_for_comparing_a.name: Activity-recall paradigm for comparing aesthetic and non-aesthetic emotional profiles
activity_recall_paradigm_for_comparing_a.description: Participants recalled engaging aesthetic, non-aesthetic, or consumer activities and rated the degree to which 75 emotions were evoked, providing a controlled paradigm for between-condition comparison.
activity_recall_paradigm_for_comparing_a.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

generalization_of_aesthetic_emotion_netw.name: Generalization of aesthetic emotion network findings beyond the current sample
generalization_of_aesthetic_emotion_netw.description: The study's findings on network differences in aesthetic emotion profiles are based on a single large sample using a recall paradigm, leaving open whether results replicate across different populations, cultures, or methodologies.
generalization_of_aesthetic_emotion_netw.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

replication_of_aesthetic_emotion_network.name: Replication of aesthetic emotion network differences in independent samples
replication_of_aesthetic_emotion_network.description: The current findings on community structure and core node differences across aesthetic, non-aesthetic, and consumer conditions require independent replication to confirm robustness.
replication_of_aesthetic_emotion_network.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

mechanistic_account_of_why_aesthetic_con.name: Mechanistic account of why aesthetic context produces distinct emotional network structure
mechanistic_account_of_why_aesthetic_con.description: The study demonstrates that aesthetic activities produce different emotional network profiles, but does not explain the cognitive or affective mechanisms responsible for this distinctiveness.
mechanistic_account_of_why_aesthetic_con.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

candidate_set_of_75_aesthetic_emotions_a.name: Candidate set of 75 aesthetic emotions as empirical resource
candidate_set_of_75_aesthetic_emotions_a.description: The study operationalized a set of 75 candidate aesthetic emotions for rating and network analysis, providing a resource for future empirical aesthetics research.
candidate_set_of_75_aesthetic_emotions_a.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

extension_of_psychometric_network_analys.name: Extension of psychometric network analysis to aesthetic emotion research
extension_of_psychometric_network_analys.description: The study applies computational psychometric network methods—previously used in personality and psychopathology research—to the domain of aesthetic emotions, enabling quantitative comparison of emotional structures across contexts.
extension_of_psychometric_network_analys.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

generalization_of_aesthetic_emotion_find.name: Generalization of aesthetic emotion findings beyond recall paradigm to online or naturalistic aesthetic experiences
generalization_of_aesthetic_emotion_find.description: The use of a memory-recall paradigm may introduce biases (e.g., vividness differences) that limit conclusions about aesthetic emotions as they occur in situ during real aesthetic engagement.
generalization_of_aesthetic_emotion_find.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

community_and_core_node_structure_differ.name: Community and core-node structure differs across aesthetic and non-aesthetic emotion networks
community_and_core_node_structure_differ.description: Network analysis revealed differences in the cluster (community) organization and the identity of central (core) nodes among the three activity conditions, indicating structural divergence in how aesthetic emotions are organized.
community_and_core_node_structure_differ.paper: Aesthetic emotions are affected by context: a psychometric network analysis

reduced_taxonomic_and_thematic_semantic_.name: Reduced taxonomic and thematic semantic distance in aphasia narratives
reduced_taxonomic_and_thematic_semantic_.description: Narratives produced by individuals with chronic post-stroke aphasia showed significantly reduced word-by-word taxonomic and thematic semantic distance compared to age-matched controls, indicating that controlled lexical-semantic retrieval deficits compress the semantic range of narrative output.
reduced_taxonomic_and_thematic_semantic_.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

semantic_flow_metrics_predict_offline_me.name: Semantic flow metrics predict offline measures of semantic impairment and aphasia severity
semantic_flow_metrics_predict_offline_me.description: Both taxonomic and thematic semantic distance metrics computed from running word pairs in narrative transcripts were strongly predictive of standardized offline measures of semantic impairment and overall aphasia severity.
semantic_flow_metrics_predict_offline_me.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

group_differences_in_semantic_distance_p.name: Group differences in semantic distance persist after excluding perseverative repetitions
group_differences_in_semantic_distance_p.description: When repetitions were excluded from transcripts, group differences in both taxonomic and thematic semantic distance remained significant, and thematic distance retained predictive power for semantic impairment, though some ancillary findings changed.
group_differences_in_semantic_distance_p.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

word_by_word_semantic_flow_as_a_novel_di.name: Word-by-word semantic flow as a novel discourse-level metric for aphasia
word_by_word_semantic_flow_as_a_novel_di.description: The study introduces a method of computing inter-word taxonomic and thematic semantic distances across every running word pair in narrative transcripts as a novel metric for characterizing discourse and lexical-semantic access impairment at multiword levels in aphasia.
word_by_word_semantic_flow_as_a_novel_di.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

cumulative_impact_of_controlled_retrieva.name: Cumulative impact of controlled retrieval deficits over narrative production
cumulative_impact_of_controlled_retrieva.description: The authors argue that deficits in controlled word retrieval compound across the course of longer narrative production, producing measurable reductions in semantic distance that would not be apparent from single-word paradigms alone.
cumulative_impact_of_controlled_retrieva.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

generalization_of_semantic_flow_findings.name: Generalization of semantic flow findings beyond post-stroke aphasia
generalization_of_semantic_flow_findings.description: The current sample is restricted to chronic post-stroke aphasia; it is unknown whether reduced semantic flow generalizes to other acquired language disorders or aphasia etiologies.
generalization_of_semantic_flow_findings.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

replication_of_semantic_flow_group_diffe.name: Replication of semantic flow group differences in independent aphasia samples
replication_of_semantic_flow_group_diffe.description: The group differences in taxonomic and thematic semantic distance between people with aphasia and controls have not yet been replicated in an independent dataset.
replication_of_semantic_flow_group_diffe.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

mechanistic_account_of_how_controlled_re.name: Mechanistic account of how controlled retrieval deficits reduce semantic distance in narrative
mechanistic_account_of_how_controlled_re.description: While the study documents reduced semantic distance in aphasia narratives, the precise cognitive mechanism by which impaired controlled lexical-semantic retrieval produces this compression of semantic flow remains unspecified.
mechanistic_account_of_how_controlled_re.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

causal_role_of_perseverative_repetitions.name: Causal role of perseverative repetitions versus retrieval failure in reducing semantic distance
causal_role_of_perseverative_repetitions.description: The additional analyses excluding repetitions reveal that repetitions contribute to but do not fully explain the reduced semantic distance in aphasia; however, the relative causal contribution of repetition versus retrieval failure remains unclear.
causal_role_of_perseverative_repetitions.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

extension_of_semantic_flow_methodology_t.name: Extension of semantic flow methodology to other discourse disorders beyond aphasia
extension_of_semantic_flow_methodology_t.description: The authors frame semantic flow as a novel discourse-level metric with potential applications beyond aphasia, but no such extension has yet been demonstrated.
extension_of_semantic_flow_methodology_t.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

relationship_between_semantic_flow_measu.name: Relationship between semantic flow measures and functional communication outcomes in aphasia
relationship_between_semantic_flow_measu.description: The study links semantic flow metrics to standardized clinical measures of impairment and severity, but it is unknown whether these metrics relate to real-world functional communication ability or treatment response.
relationship_between_semantic_flow_measu.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

taxonomic_versus_thematic_semantic_dista.name: Taxonomic versus thematic semantic distance as differentially sensitive to aphasia features
taxonomic_versus_thematic_semantic_dista.description: The study examines both taxonomic and thematic semantic distance and notes differential patterns (e.g., thematic distance remained predictive after repetition exclusion while some taxonomic findings changed), suggesting the two metrics capture partially distinct aspects of lexical-semantic impairment.
taxonomic_versus_thematic_semantic_dista.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

optimal_narrative_elicitation_paradigm_f.name: Optimal narrative elicitation paradigm for semantic flow analysis in aphasia
optimal_narrative_elicitation_paradigm_f.description: The study uses storytelling narratives but does not establish whether this paradigm is optimal compared to other discourse elicitation methods for detecting semantic flow deficits.
optimal_narrative_elicitation_paradigm_f.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

large_scale_narrative_corpus_from_people.name: Large-scale narrative corpus from people with aphasia and controls as empirical resource
large_scale_narrative_corpus_from_people.description: The study provides a corpus of 232,490 word transitions from transcribed narratives of 259 individuals with aphasia and 203 age-matched controls, enabling quantitative discourse-level analysis.
large_scale_narrative_corpus_from_people.paper: Semantic flow and its relation to controlled semantic retrieval deficits in the narrative production of people with aphasia

bigram_semantic_distance_as_index_of_con.name: bigram_semantic_distance_as_index_of_continuous_semantic_flow
bigram_semantic_distance_as_index_of_con.description: The paper introduces bigram semantic distance computed over continuous language samples as a novel metric for measuring conceptual cohesion and topic flow as language unfolds, extending single-word semantic distance methods to the discourse level.
bigram_semantic_distance_as_index_of_con.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

semdistflow_r_package_as_tool_for_bigram.name: semdistflow_r_package_as_tool_for_bigram_semantic_distance_analysis
semdistflow_r_package_as_tool_for_bigram.description: The authors provide an R package ('semdistflow') that transforms any user-specified language transcript into an ordered vector of bigrams and appends two metrics of semantic distance to each pair, enabling reproducible analysis.
semdistflow_r_package_as_tool_for_bigram.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

validation_of_bigram_distance_on_simulat.name: validation_of_bigram_distance_on_simulated_verbal_fluency_data
validation_of_bigram_distance_on_simulat.description: Bigram semantic distance metrics were validated on a continuous stream of simulated verbal fluency data, where they successfully assigned predicted switch markers between alternating semantic clusters (animals, musical instruments, fruit).
validation_of_bigram_distance_on_simulat.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

bigram_distance_norms_on_large_text_samp.name: bigram_distance_norms_on_large_text_sample
bigram_distance_norms_on_large_text_samp.description: The authors generated bigram semantic distance norms on a large sample of text, providing an empirical baseline for interpreting semantic distances in continuous language.
bigram_distance_norms_on_large_text_samp.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

sentence_boundary_punctuated_by_semantic.name: sentence_boundary_punctuated_by_semantic_distance_jumps
sentence_boundary_punctuated_by_semantic.description: An application to Jack London's 'To Build a Fire' showed that bigrams spanning sentence boundaries are punctuated by jumps in semantic distance, revealing a measurable discourse-level structural property.
sentence_boundary_punctuated_by_semantic.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

application_to_characterizing_semantic_p.name: application_to_characterizing_semantic_processing_in_real_world_narratives
application_to_characterizing_semantic_p.description: The paper articulates a promise for using bigram semantic distance to characterize semantic processing in real-world narratives, bridging single-word findings with macroscale discourse analyses, but full empirical validation across diverse narrative types remains to be done.
application_to_characterizing_semantic_p.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

extension_of_bigram_semantic_distance_be.name: extension_of_bigram_semantic_distance_beyond_single_literary_text
extension_of_bigram_semantic_distance_be.description: The current applications are demonstrated primarily on one literary text and simulated data; generalization to other real-world language samples, genres, or speakers remains an open direction.
extension_of_bigram_semantic_distance_be.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

clinical_or_cognitive_applications_of_bi.name: clinical_or_cognitive_applications_of_bigram_semantic_distance_in_language_disorders
clinical_or_cognitive_applications_of_bi.description: The paper discusses the promise of the technique for clinical and cognitive research (e.g., characterizing semantic processing in aphasia or other language disorders), but empirical clinical validation is not yet provided.
clinical_or_cognitive_applications_of_bi.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

two_metrics_of_semantic_distance_per_big.name: two_metrics_of_semantic_distance_per_bigram
two_metrics_of_semantic_distance_per_big.description: Two distinct metrics of semantic distance are appended to each bigram, providing complementary quantifications of conceptual cohesion between adjacent words in a language stream.
two_metrics_of_semantic_distance_per_big.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

bridging_single_word_and_macroscale_disc.name: bridging_single_word_and_macroscale_discourse_levels_via_bigram_distance
bridging_single_word_and_macroscale_disc.description: The authors claim that bigram semantic distance can bridge findings at the single-word semantic level with macroscale discourse analyses, offering a continuous and ordered metric of semantic flow.
bridging_single_word_and_macroscale_disc.paper: Bigram Semantic Distance as an Index of Continuous Semantic Flow in Natural Language: Theory, Tools, and Applications

environmental_scene_and_body_posture_int.name: Environmental scene and body posture interaction on AUT performance
environmental_scene_and_body_posture_int.description: The study demonstrates that environmental context and body posture interact to shape performance on the alternative uses task, such that body posture facilitates fluent responding under high fixedness environmental contexts.
environmental_scene_and_body_posture_int.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

away_from_body_actions_produce_more_orig.name: Away-from-body actions produce more original AUT responses
away_from_body_actions_produce_more_orig.description: Describing actions directed away from the body results in more original responses on the alternative uses task compared to actions directed toward the body.
away_from_body_actions_produce_more_orig.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

replication_of_embodied_strategy_differe.name: Replication of embodied strategy differences between high and low creative individuals
replication_of_embodied_strategy_differe.description: The study replicates prior findings that higher-creative individuals use different embodied strategies than lower-creative individuals when generating creative uses for objects.
replication_of_embodied_strategy_differe.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

higher_creative_individuals_access_and_n.name: Higher-creative individuals access and navigate an abstract action space
higher_creative_individuals_access_and_n.description: The authors propose that higher-creative individuals differentially access and navigate an abstract action space necessary for simulating creative uses of objects, explaining their superior embodied strategy use.
higher_creative_individuals_access_and_n.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

embodied_cognition_framework_applied_to_.name: Embodied cognition framework applied to AUT with environmental and postural manipulations
embodied_cognition_framework_applied_to_.description: The study manipulates environmental scene context and body posture while participants perform the AUT under general or action-direction instructions, operationalizing embodied cognition predictions for creative thinking.
embodied_cognition_framework_applied_to_.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

causal_mechanism_linking_body_posture_an.name: Causal mechanism linking body posture and environmental context to embodied simulation in creativity
causal_mechanism_linking_body_posture_an.description: It remains unclear how exactly body posture and environmental context produce their effects on AUT performance via neural simulation processes; the study is theoretically motivated but the mechanistic pathway is not fully established.
causal_mechanism_linking_body_posture_an.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

generalization_of_environmental_and_post.name: Generalization of environmental and postural embodied effects beyond the AUT
generalization_of_environmental_and_post.description: The current findings are limited to the alternative uses task; whether environmental scene and body posture effects on embodied strategies generalize to other creative paradigms or object types remains unaddressed.
generalization_of_environmental_and_post.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

generalization_of_embodied_strategy_crea.name: Generalization of embodied strategy creativity findings beyond current sample and stimuli
generalization_of_embodied_strategy_crea.description: The replication of individual differences in embodied strategy use and the new environmental and postural findings require testing in independent samples and with diverse object stimuli to establish generalizability.
generalization_of_embodied_strategy_crea.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

role_of_high_fixedness_environmental_con.name: Role of high fixedness environmental context in constraining creative fluency
role_of_high_fixedness_environmental_con.description: High fixedness environmental contexts specifically constrain AUT fluency, and body posture manipulations can facilitate responding under these constraining conditions.
role_of_high_fixedness_environmental_con.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

integration_of_environmental_and_postura.name: Integration of environmental and postural embodied findings with neurocognitive creativity models
integration_of_environmental_and_postura.description: The study's findings regarding environmental scene and body posture effects on creative thinking need to be integrated into broader neurocognitive and embodied creativity models, including how abstract action space maps onto neural substrates.
integration_of_environmental_and_postura.paper: The Effects of Environmental Scene and Body Posture on Embodied Strategies in Creative Thinking

age_related_decrease_in_creative_perform.name: age_related_decrease_in_creative_performance_associated_with_semantic_network_maturation
age_related_decrease_in_creative_perform.description: Older adults (M=77.8 years) showed lower creative performance on divergent thinking tasks compared to younger adults (M=20.3 years), and this decrease was associated with the typical maturation pattern of older adults' semantic memory networks toward greater rigidity.
age_related_decrease_in_creative_perform.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

higher_creative_older_adults_preserve_se.name: higher_creative_older_adults_preserve_semantic_network_flexibility
higher_creative_older_adults_preserve_se.description: Older adults with higher creative performance exhibited preserved overall semantic memory flexibility compared to lower-creative older adults, with their flexibility levels resembling those of lower-creative younger adults, suggesting creativity buffers age-related network rigidification.
higher_creative_older_adults_preserve_se.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

protective_role_of_creativity_in_maintai.name: protective_role_of_creativity_in_maintaining_semantic_memory_flexibility_during_ageing
protective_role_of_creativity_in_maintai.description: The study argues that creativity plays a propaedeutic and protective role in active ageing by helping maintain a flexible organization of and access to semantic memory structures, potentially counteracting normative age-related rigidification.
protective_role_of_creativity_in_maintai.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

semantic_memory_network_estimation_from_.name: semantic_memory_network_estimation_from_verbal_fluency_and_free_association_tasks
semantic_memory_network_estimation_from_.description: Semantic memory networks were estimated from four verbal production tasks (two verbal fluency and two free association tasks) completed by both younger and older adult participants, enabling individual-level network comparison across age and creativity groups.
semantic_memory_network_estimation_from_.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_cre.name: causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_creativity_preserves_semantic_network_flexibility_in_ageing
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_cre.description: The study establishes an association between higher creativity and preserved semantic network flexibility in older adults but does not establish whether creativity causally produces this preservation, or whether a shared underlying factor (e.g., lifelong intellectual engagement) explains both.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_cre.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

generalization_of_creativity_semantic_ne.name: generalization_of_creativity_semantic_network_flexibility_preservation_finding_beyond_current_sample
generalization_of_creativity_semantic_ne.description: The finding that higher creativity preserves semantic network flexibility in ageing is based on a specific sample of older and younger adults; its generalizability to other populations, cultures, and broader age ranges remains untested.
generalization_of_creativity_semantic_ne.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

longitudinal_account_of_creativity_as_pr.name: longitudinal_account_of_creativity_as_protective_factor_for_semantic_memory_across_the_lifespan
longitudinal_account_of_creativity_as_pr.description: The current cross-sectional design limits causal inference about whether engaging in creative activities over a lifetime produces the observed preservation of semantic flexibility; longitudinal studies are needed to confirm the protective role of creativity in ageing.
longitudinal_account_of_creativity_as_pr.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

divergent_thinking_as_measure_of_creativ.name: divergent_thinking_as_measure_of_creative_performance_in_ageing_context
divergent_thinking_as_measure_of_creativ.description: Two Alternative Uses Task (AUT) variants were used to assess creative performance, providing a standardized divergent thinking measure that can be compared across younger and older adult groups.
divergent_thinking_as_measure_of_creativ.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

extension_of_semantic_network_flexibilit.name: extension_of_semantic_network_flexibility_creativity_link_to_ageing_context
extension_of_semantic_network_flexibilit.description: This study extends prior findings that creative individuals have more flexible semantic memory structures by showing that this relationship operates within the ageing context, where creativity is associated with resistance to the normative rigidification of semantic networks.
extension_of_semantic_network_flexibilit.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

whether_creativity_supports_active_agein.name: whether_creativity_supports_active_ageing_through_semantic_memory_maintenance
