whether_creativity_supports_active_agein.description: The study raises the broader question of whether creativity, by preserving semantic memory flexibility, constitutes a general cognitive resource supporting active and healthy ageing beyond the domains examined here.
whether_creativity_supports_active_agein.paper: Creativity, the fountain of youth: Association between creativity and semantic memory networks across the lifespan.

connector_hubs_facilitate_more_novel_and.name: connector_hubs_facilitate_more_novel_and_remote_creative_ideas_than_provincial_hubs
connector_hubs_facilitate_more_novel_and.description: Across four experiments, behavioral and neural evidence showed that connector hubs (C-hubs) in a modular semantic network elicit more novel and remote ideas in the alternative uses task compared to provincial hubs (P-hubs).
connector_hubs_facilitate_more_novel_and.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

c_hub_advantage_concentrated_in_early_st.name: c_hub_advantage_concentrated_in_early_stage_of_creative_thinking_process
c_hub_advantage_concentrated_in_early_st.description: The facilitatory effect of connector hubs on creative thinking is predominantly observed in the early stage of the creative thinking process, as indexed by changes in brain activation and functional connectivity.
c_hub_advantage_concentrated_in_early_st.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

c_hub_creativity_advantage_mediated_by_d.name: c_hub_creativity_advantage_mediated_by_dmn_and_fpn_interactions
c_hub_creativity_advantage_mediated_by_d.description: Superior creative performance with C-hubs relies on stronger interactions among default mode network and frontoparietal network regions including dorsolateral prefrontal cortex, angular gyrus, and precuneus, reflecting interplay of spreading activation, controlled retrieval, and attentional regulation.
c_hub_creativity_advantage_mediated_by_d.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

modular_semantic_network_structure_as_fr.name: modular_semantic_network_structure_as_framework_for_hub_classification
modular_semantic_network_structure_as_fr.description: A large-scale semantic network was analyzed to identify connector hubs (high intermodule connectivity) and provincial hubs (high intramodule connectivity) based on their intra- and intermodule connection patterns, providing the stimulus basis for experimental cue words.
modular_semantic_network_structure_as_fr.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

modular_structure_of_semantic_network_as.name: modular_structure_of_semantic_network_as_facilitating_routine_retrieval_but_impeding_creativity
modular_structure_of_semantic_network_as.description: The modular organization of the semantic network, with dense within-module and sparse between-module connections, is proposed to facilitate routine information storage and retrieval while potentially impeding creative thinking.
modular_structure_of_semantic_network_as.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

mechanism_linking_c_hub_spreading_activa.name: mechanism_linking_c_hub_spreading_activation_controlled_retrieval_and_attentional_regulation
mechanism_linking_c_hub_spreading_activa.description: The neural findings are interpreted as showing that C-hub superiority depends on stronger interactions between automatic spreading activation, controlled semantic retrieval, and attentional regulation of salient information.
mechanism_linking_c_hub_spreading_activa.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

causal_account_of_how_connector_hub_stru.name: causal_account_of_how_connector_hub_structure_produces_creativity_advantage
causal_account_of_how_connector_hub_stru.description: The study provides correlational and neurofunctional evidence but does not establish a causal mechanism explaining precisely why intermodule connectivity of C-hubs produces enhanced novelty and remoteness in creative outputs.
causal_account_of_how_connector_hub_stru.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

replication_of_c_hub_creativity_finding_.name: replication_of_c_hub_creativity_finding_in_independent_samples
replication_of_c_hub_creativity_finding_.description: The four experiments are reported within a single study; independent replication across separate labs or diverse populations has not yet been demonstrated.
replication_of_c_hub_creativity_finding_.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

generalization_of_c_hub_creativity_findi.name: generalization_of_c_hub_creativity_finding_beyond_the_alternative_uses_task
generalization_of_c_hub_creativity_findi.description: The C-hub advantage was demonstrated using the alternative uses task; whether this effect extends to other creativity paradigms (e.g., convergent thinking, remote associates, real-world creative production) remains untested.
generalization_of_c_hub_creativity_findi.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

generalization_of_c_hub_creativity_findi.name: generalization_of_c_hub_creativity_finding_to_later_stages_of_creative_thinking
generalization_of_c_hub_creativity_findi.description: The C-hub effect is predominantly early-stage; whether connector hubs differentially support or hinder later stages of idea elaboration and evaluation remains an open question.
generalization_of_c_hub_creativity_findi.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

individual_differences_moderating_c_hub_.name: individual_differences_moderating_c_hub_versus_p_hub_creativity_effect
individual_differences_moderating_c_hub_.description: The study does not address whether individual differences in semantic memory structure, baseline creativity, or cognitive control moderate the advantage of C-hub over P-hub cues.
individual_differences_moderating_c_hub_.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

integration_of_hub_type_findings_with_br.name: integration_of_hub_type_findings_with_broader_semantic_network_and_creativity_theory
integration_of_hub_type_findings_with_br.description: The study calls for integrating the connector-hub framework with existing theories of semantic memory, modular network organization, and creative cognition to form a unified theoretical account.
integration_of_hub_type_findings_with_br.paper: Connector hubs in semantic network contribute to creative thinking.

forward_flow_conceptual_and_measurement_.name: Forward flow conceptual and measurement concerns raised by Rossiter
forward_flow_conceptual_and_measurement_.description: Rossiter (2020) raised conceptual and measurement concerns about the forward flow measure as a quantification of free thought and predictor of creativity. The authors regard these concerns as resting on fundamental misunderstandings.
forward_flow_conceptual_and_measurement_.paper: Clarifying what forward flow is (and isn't): Reply to Rossiter (2020).

clarification_of_what_forward_flow_is_an.name: Clarification of what forward flow is and is not
clarification_of_what_forward_flow_is_an.description: The reply explicitly clarifies the nature of forward flow as a measure of free thought that predicts creativity, distinguishing it from what it is not, in response to Rossiter's critique.
clarification_of_what_forward_flow_is_an.paper: Clarifying what forward flow is (and isn't): Reply to Rossiter (2020).

validity_of_rossiter_s_concerns_about_fo.name: Validity of Rossiter's concerns about forward flow
validity_of_rossiter_s_concerns_about_fo.description: The authors claim that Rossiter's concerns are specious and stem from fundamental misunderstandings of the aims and approach of the forward flow measure.
validity_of_rossiter_s_concerns_about_fo.paper: Clarifying what forward flow is (and isn't): Reply to Rossiter (2020).

creativity_as_predictor_of_academic_lear.name: creativity_as_predictor_of_academic_learning_outcomes
creativity_as_predictor_of_academic_lear.description: The source establishes creativity as a consistent predictor of academic learning outcomes, motivating the investigation of underlying cognitive mechanisms. This is framed as background context supported by prior decades of research.
creativity_as_predictor_of_academic_lear.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

associative_thinking_as_mechanism_linkin.name: associative_thinking_as_mechanism_linking_creativity_to_associative_learning
associative_thinking_as_mechanism_linkin.description: The studies found that associative thinking—the ability to generate novel word associations—mediates the relationship between creativity and associative learning, suggesting a shared cognitive capacity for making new connections underlies both.
associative_thinking_as_mechanism_linkin.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

novel_word_associations_predict_foreign_.name: novel_word_associations_predict_foreign_language_learning
novel_word_associations_predict_foreign_.description: In Study 1, students who generated more novel word associations learned more foreign language vocabulary words on a test 24 hours later, establishing a link between associative thinking and associative learning outcomes.
novel_word_associations_predict_foreign_.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

replication_and_extension_of_associative.name: replication_and_extension_of_associative_thinking_learning_link_to_naturalistic_creativity_tasks
replication_and_extension_of_associative.description: Study 2 replicated the Study 1 effect and extended it by using naturalistic creativity tasks (writing short stories, sketching line drawings), with associative thinking mediating the creativity–learning relationship in this broader context.
replication_and_extension_of_associative.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

gap_in_understanding_cognitive_mechanism.name: gap_in_understanding_cognitive_mechanisms_of_creativity_learning_relationship
gap_in_understanding_cognitive_mechanism.description: Despite decades of research on creativity and learning, the cognitive mechanisms underlying their relationship have remained unknown prior to this work. The source identifies this as the gap motivating the two studies.
gap_in_understanding_cognitive_mechanism.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

generalization_of_associative_thinking_m.name: generalization_of_associative_thinking_mediation_to_non_verbal_learning_domains
generalization_of_associative_thinking_m.description: The mediation findings are demonstrated for foreign language (verbal) associative learning; whether associative thinking similarly mediates the creativity–learning relationship in non-verbal or other academic domains remains untested.
generalization_of_associative_thinking_m.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

causal_direction_of_associative_thinking.name: causal_direction_of_associative_thinking_creativity_learning_pathway
causal_direction_of_associative_thinking.description: The two studies demonstrate correlational and mediation evidence; whether the pathway is truly causal—i.e., whether experimentally increasing associative thinking would increase associative learning—has not been established.
causal_direction_of_associative_thinking.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

replication_of_associative_thinking_medi.name: replication_of_associative_thinking_mediation_in_independent_and_diverse_samples
replication_of_associative_thinking_medi.description: The findings are based on two studies in student samples; independent replication in more diverse populations (e.g., different age groups, educational contexts) is needed to establish generalizability.
replication_of_associative_thinking_medi.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

role_of_associative_thinking_in_non_asso.name: role_of_associative_thinking_in_non_associative_forms_of_academic_learning
role_of_associative_thinking_in_non_asso.description: The learning outcome measured is associative (foreign language vocabulary pairing); whether associative thinking also supports conceptual, procedural, or other non-associative forms of academic learning is not addressed by the source.
role_of_associative_thinking_in_non_asso.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

control_for_general_intelligence_in_crea.name: control_for_general_intelligence_in_creativity_learning_relationship
control_for_general_intelligence_in_crea.description: Both studies controlled for general intelligence to isolate the specific contribution of creativity and associative thinking to learning outcomes, ruling out intelligence as a confounding explanation.
control_for_general_intelligence_in_crea.paper: Creativity supports learning through associative thinking

question_asking_as_mechanism_supporting_.name: question_asking_as_mechanism_supporting_creative_ideation
question_asking_as_mechanism_supporting_.description: The paper argues that question asking is a key cognitive mechanism supporting creative ideation by helping define problems and facilitate creative problem solving. This claim organizes the motivation for applying text mining to questions in creativity research.
question_asking_as_mechanism_supporting_.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

text_mining_application_to_human_questio.name: text_mining_application_to_human_question_asking_in_creativity
text_mining_application_to_human_questio.description: The paper proposes, implements, and applies a novel text mining approach to measure the cognitive potential of questions across five datasets, considering question type, complexity, and answer content.
text_mining_application_to_human_questio.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

history_of_question_mining_in_creativity.name: history_of_question_mining_in_creativity_research_reviewed
history_of_question_mining_in_creativity.description: The paper summarizes the history of question mining as part of creativity research, situating the new approach within prior work and relevant NLP methods.
history_of_question_mining_in_creativity.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

multi_dimensional_operationalization_of_.name: multi_dimensional_operationalization_of_question_cognitive_potential
multi_dimensional_operationalization_of_.description: The paper operationalizes the cognitive potential of questions along at least three dimensions: question type, question complexity, and the content of the answer, enabling quantitative analysis.
multi_dimensional_operationalization_of_.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

nlp_has_a_role_in_creativity_research_on.name: nlp_has_a_role_in_creativity_research_on_questions
nlp_has_a_role_in_creativity_research_on.description: The experimental results from applying the NLP approach to five datasets suggest that natural language processing methods can meaningfully contribute to the study of question asking in creativity research.
nlp_has_a_role_in_creativity_research_on.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

incomplete_understanding_of_exact_role_o.name: incomplete_understanding_of_exact_role_of_questions_in_creativity
incomplete_understanding_of_exact_role_o.description: The paper acknowledges that much remains unknown about the exact role questions play in the creative process, positioning this as a motivating gap the work begins to address.
incomplete_understanding_of_exact_role_o.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

generalization_of_text_mining_question_a.name: generalization_of_text_mining_question_approach_beyond_five_datasets
generalization_of_text_mining_question_a.description: The approach is demonstrated on five datasets, but broader generalization to other populations, domains, and question-asking contexts remains to be established.
generalization_of_text_mining_question_a.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

causal_account_of_how_question_asking_pr.name: causal_account_of_how_question_asking_produces_creative_ideation
causal_account_of_how_question_asking_pr.description: While question asking is proposed as a mechanism for creative ideation, the paper does not establish a causal account of how or why formulating questions leads to novel and effective ideas.
causal_account_of_how_question_asking_pr.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

optimal_nlp_metrics_for_capturing_cognit.name: optimal_nlp_metrics_for_capturing_cognitive_potential_of_questions
optimal_nlp_metrics_for_capturing_cognit.description: The paper proposes dimensions for measuring cognitive potential of questions but does not fully resolve which NLP metrics are optimal for predicting creative outcomes.
optimal_nlp_metrics_for_capturing_cognit.paper: Applying Text Mining to Analyze Human Question Asking in Creativity Research

semantic_memory_network_structure_predic.name: Semantic memory network structure predicts objective originality in AUT
semantic_memory_network_structure_predic.description: Objective originality in the Alternative Uses Task was predicted by broadly connected semantic memory networks that avoided tightly knit clusters, indicating that global connectivity and low clustering support the generation of genuinely original ideas.

semantic_memory_network_integration_pred.name: Semantic memory network integration predicts originality self-judgments in AUT
semantic_memory_network_integration_pred.description: Individuals' subjective judgments of the originality of their own AUT responses were predicted by highly integrated and efficient semantic memory networks, a pattern distinct from the predictor of objective originality.

dissociation_between_objective_originali.name: Dissociation between objective originality and originality self-judgment in semantic memory predictors
dissociation_between_objective_originali.description: The study reveals a dissociation: objective originality is predicted by broadly connected, low-clustering networks, while self-assessed originality is predicted by highly integrated, efficient networks, suggesting different metacognitive and performance processes.

idea_fluency_as_consistent_predictor_of_.name: Idea fluency as consistent predictor of both creative performance and originality self-judgment
idea_fluency_as_consistent_predictor_of_.description: The number of ideas generated in the AUT was a consistent and strong predictor of both objective creative performance and individuals' self-assessments of originality, suggesting a common role of fluency across performance and metacognition.

individual_level_semantic_memory_network.name: Individual-level semantic memory network construction via relatedness judgment task
individual_level_semantic_memory_network.description: Participants' semantic memory networks were constructed at the individual level using a relatedness judgment task, yielding metrics such as efficiency, local connectivity, and community structure for N=106 participants.

metacognitive_perspective_on_creative_id.name: Metacognitive perspective on creative idea evaluation understudied
metacognitive_perspective_on_creative_id.description: While research has focused on creative idea generation, relatively little is known about creative idea evaluation from a metacognitive perspective, leaving a gap in understanding how knowledge structures relate to metacognitive processes in creative thinking.

causal_account_of_how_semantic_network_s.name: Causal account of how semantic network structure produces dissociation between originality performance and self-judgment
causal_account_of_how_semantic_network_s.description: The study demonstrates an associative dissociation between objective and subjective originality predictors but does not establish the causal or mechanistic pathway by which different network structures produce different metacognitive versus performance outcomes.

generalization_of_semantic_memory_metaco.name: Generalization of semantic memory metacognitive dissociation beyond AUT
generalization_of_semantic_memory_metaco.description: It is unknown whether the dissociation between semantic memory predictors of objective originality versus self-assessed originality generalizes beyond the AUT to other creativity paradigms or domains.

replication_of_semantic_memory_metacogni.name: Replication of semantic memory metacognitive dissociation finding in independent samples
replication_of_semantic_memory_metacogni.description: The finding that different semantic network metrics predict objective versus subjective originality requires independent replication to confirm its robustness and generalizability.

role_of_semantic_memory_structure_in_cre.name: Role of semantic memory structure in creative metacognition as a distinct research focus
role_of_semantic_memory_structure_in_cre.description: This work introduces the study of how semantic memory network structure relates to metacognitive originality judgments as a distinct research direction, extending cognitive network science beyond creative performance to creative self-assessment.

touch_imagery_vividness_correlates_with_.name: touch_imagery_vividness_correlates_with_creative_writing_performance
touch_imagery_vividness_correlates_with_.description: A behavioral study found a positive correlation between the vividness of touch imagery and creative performance in a creative writing task, establishing touch imagery as a relevant predictor of creative output.
touch_imagery_vividness_correlates_with_.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

semantic_integration_and_reorganization_.name: semantic_integration_and_reorganization_mediate_touch_imagery_creativity_link
semantic_integration_and_reorganization_.description: Using semantic feature indicators derived from writing texts and mediation models, the study found that touch imagery vividness facilitates creative writing through semantic integration and reorganization as mediating mechanisms.
semantic_integration_and_reorganization_.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

semantic_reorganization_plays_critical_r.name: semantic_reorganization_plays_critical_role_beyond_semantic_integration_in_imagery_enhanced_creativity
semantic_reorganization_plays_critical_r.description: A second behavioral study comparing mental imagery versus semantic understanding strategies confirmed that semantic reorganization, beyond semantic integration, is particularly critical in how mental imagery enhances creativity.
semantic_reorganization_plays_critical_r.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

sensorimotor_network_facilitates_sensori.name: sensorimotor_network_facilitates_sensorimotor_simulations_during_imagery_involved_creative_writing
sensorimotor_network_facilitates_sensori.description: The fMRI study found that the sensorimotor network supports sensorimotor simulations during creative writing under mental imagery conditions, linking body-based simulation to creative cognition.
sensorimotor_network_facilitates_sensori.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

dorsal_attention_and_salience_networks_c.name: dorsal_attention_and_salience_networks_collaboratively_support_creative_writing_via_attention
dorsal_attention_and_salience_networks_c.description: The fMRI study identified that the dorsal attention and salience networks collaboratively support the creative writing process by maintaining goal-directed attention and reorienting attention during imagery-involved creative cognition.
dorsal_attention_and_salience_networks_c.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

limbic_network_supports_multimodal_seman.name: limbic_network_supports_multimodal_semantic_processing_and_novel_associations_in_creative_writing
limbic_network_supports_multimodal_seman.description: The fMRI study found that the limbic network supports multimodal semantic processing and the formation of novel associations during imagery-involved creative writing.
limbic_network_supports_multimodal_seman.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

frontoparietal_control_and_default_mode_.name: frontoparietal_control_and_default_mode_networks_contribute_to_information_integration_in_creative_writing
frontoparietal_control_and_default_mode_.description: The fMRI study found that the frontoparietal control network and the default mode network contribute to information integration during imagery-involved creative cognition.
frontoparietal_control_and_default_mode_.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

dmn_subnetwork_integrates_semantic_infor.name: dmn_subnetwork_integrates_semantic_information_related_to_objects_and_actions
dmn_subnetwork_integrates_semantic_infor.description: A subnetwork of the default mode network was identified as playing a special role in integrating semantic information related to objects and actions during mental imagery-supported creative writing.
dmn_subnetwork_integrates_semantic_infor.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

mental_imagery_role_in_creativity_via_se.name: mental_imagery_role_in_creativity_via_semantic_memory_processing_as_underexplored
mental_imagery_role_in_creativity_via_se.description: Although the role of mental imagery in creative cognition is acknowledged in the literature, the specific cognitive and neural mechanisms linking mental imagery to creativity through semantic memory remain underexplored prior to this study.
mental_imagery_role_in_creativity_via_se.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

replication_of_touch_imagery_creativity_.name: replication_of_touch_imagery_creativity_mediation_finding_in_independent_samples
replication_of_touch_imagery_creativity_.description: The mediation finding linking touch imagery vividness to creative writing via semantic integration and reorganization has not been replicated in independent or more diverse samples beyond those reported in the source.
replication_of_touch_imagery_creativity_.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

generalization_of_imagery_creativity_sem.name: generalization_of_imagery_creativity_semantic_mechanism_beyond_touch_imagery
generalization_of_imagery_creativity_sem.description: The study focuses on touch imagery vividness; it remains open whether other imagery modalities (e.g., visual, auditory) similarly facilitate creative cognition via semantic integration and reorganization.
generalization_of_imagery_creativity_sem.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

generalization_of_imagery_creativity_fmr.name: generalization_of_imagery_creativity_fmri_findings_beyond_creative_writing
generalization_of_imagery_creativity_fmr.description: The neural findings on brain network edge communities are derived from a creative writing paradigm under imagery versus semantic understanding conditions; whether the same network profiles emerge in other creative tasks remains unaddressed.
generalization_of_imagery_creativity_fmr.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

causal_account_of_how_mental_imagery_pro.name: causal_account_of_how_mental_imagery_produces_semantic_reorganization_in_creative_cognition
causal_account_of_how_mental_imagery_pro.description: The study establishes correlational and mediational evidence but does not provide a full mechanistic or causal account of why mental imagery specifically produces semantic reorganization rather than only semantic integration.
causal_account_of_how_mental_imagery_pro.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

neural_substrates_of_semantic_integratio.name: neural_substrates_of_semantic_integration_versus_reorganization_distinction
neural_substrates_of_semantic_integratio.description: While the fMRI study identifies broad network contributions to creative writing under imagery, the distinct neural substrates specifically underpinning semantic integration versus semantic reorganization as separate processes are not fully delineated.
neural_substrates_of_semantic_integratio.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

functional_brain_network_edge_community_.name: functional_brain_network_edge_community_analysis_as_method_for_creative_cognition_research
functional_brain_network_edge_community_.description: The study uses edge community analysis of functional brain network connectivity during creative writing under imagery and semantic understanding conditions as a methodological approach to map network-level contributions to creative cognition.
functional_brain_network_edge_community_.paper: Cognitive and neural mechanisms of mental imagery supporting creative cognition

within_subject_neural_dynamics_of_creati.name: within_subject_neural_dynamics_of_creative_generation_versus_evaluation
within_subject_neural_dynamics_of_creati.description: The study is the first to show within-subject differences in neural dynamics related to generating versus evaluating creative and non-creative ideas, using a novel two-session fMRI design.
within_subject_neural_dynamics_of_creati.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

creative_idea_generation_produces_higher.name: creative_idea_generation_produces_higher_network_reconfiguration_than_non_creative_generation
creative_idea_generation_produces_higher.description: Generating creative ideas (alternative uses task) led to significantly higher brain network reconfiguration compared to generating non-creative ideas (common characteristics task).
creative_idea_generation_produces_higher.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

creative_and_non_creative_evaluation_pro.name: creative_and_non_creative_evaluation_produce_similar_network_integration
creative_and_non_creative_evaluation_pro.description: Evaluating creative and non-creative ideas led to similar levels of network integration, contrasting with the differential reconfiguration observed during generation.
creative_and_non_creative_evaluation_pro.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

dmn_ecn_salience_network_dynamic_pattern.name: dmn_ecn_salience_network_dynamic_patterns_differ_across_generation_and_evaluation
dmn_ecn_salience_network_dynamic_pattern.description: Differential neural dynamics during generation versus evaluation were attributable to distinct dynamic patterns of activity across the executive control, default mode, and salience networks.
dmn_ecn_salience_network_dynamic_pattern.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

two_session_fmri_within_subject_design_f.name: two_session_fmri_within_subject_design_for_generation_and_evaluation_of_creative_ideas
two_session_fmri_within_subject_design_f.description: Participants completed two fMRI sessions a week apart: the first for generating creative or non-creative responses, the second for evaluating their own previously generated responses to the same objects.
two_session_fmri_within_subject_design_f.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

causal_account_of_how_network_reconfigur.name: causal_account_of_how_network_reconfiguration_versus_integration_maps_onto_generation_versus_evaluation
causal_account_of_how_network_reconfigur.description: Although the study demonstrates that generation and evaluation recruit different neural dynamic patterns, the causal mechanisms by which network reconfiguration supports creative generation and integration supports evaluation remain unexplained.
causal_account_of_how_network_reconfigur.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

generalization_of_generation_evaluation_.name: generalization_of_generation_evaluation_neural_dynamic_findings_beyond_alternative_uses_task
generalization_of_generation_evaluation_.description: The generation condition used the alternative uses task and the evaluation condition assessed those responses; it is unknown whether the same neural dynamic patterns hold for other creative tasks or domains.
generalization_of_generation_evaluation_.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

replication_of_generation_evaluation_neu.name: replication_of_generation_evaluation_neural_dynamic_findings_in_independent_samples
replication_of_generation_evaluation_neu.description: The within-subject neural dynamics finding is novel and based on a single study; independent replication is needed to establish its robustness.
replication_of_generation_evaluation_neu.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

incomplete_understanding_of_neural_dynam.name: incomplete_understanding_of_neural_dynamics_underlying_generation_and_evaluation_of_creative_ideas
incomplete_understanding_of_neural_dynam.description: Prior to this study, how different brain systems are recruited dynamically during the generation and evaluation phases of the creative process remained far from understood.
incomplete_understanding_of_neural_dynam.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

role_of_salience_network_in_distinguishi.name: role_of_salience_network_in_distinguishing_generation_from_evaluation_neural_dynamics
role_of_salience_network_in_distinguishi.description: The salience network, alongside the executive control and default mode networks, showed differential dynamic patterns that contributed to distinguishing creative generation from evaluation.
role_of_salience_network_in_distinguishi.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

integration_of_generation_evaluation_neu.name: integration_of_generation_evaluation_neural_dynamic_findings_with_broader_creativity_neuroscience
integration_of_generation_evaluation_neu.description: The study's findings on differential reconfiguration and integration during generation and evaluation need to be integrated with existing theories and findings on how executive control, DMN, and salience networks support creative ability.
integration_of_generation_evaluation_neu.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

generalization_of_creative_generation_ev.name: generalization_of_creative_generation_evaluation_neural_dynamics_to_non_verbal_creative_domains
generalization_of_creative_generation_ev.description: The paradigm used verbal creative tasks (alternative uses); whether the same reconfiguration-integration distinction holds for figural, musical, or other non-verbal creative domains is unknown.
generalization_of_creative_generation_ev.paper: Neural Dynamics During the Generation and Evaluation of Creative and Non-Creative Ideas

cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_finding.name: cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_finding
cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_finding.description: LLM responses mirror other LLM responses far more than human responses mirror other human responses, even after controlling for key confounding variables. This establishes that creative homogeneity is a property of LLMs as a class, not merely of any single model.
cross_llm_creative_homogeneity_finding.paper: Large language models are homogeneously creative

single_llm_narrowing_versus_class_level_.name: single_llm_narrowing_versus_class_level_homogeneity_distinction
single_llm_narrowing_versus_class_level_.description: Prior studies showed that interacting with a single LLM narrows creative outputs, but left open whether this was due to that particular model's limited range or to LLMs in general. The present work establishes the latter: the homogeneity is a cross-LLM phenomenon, not model-specific.
single_llm_narrowing_versus_class_level_.paper: Large language models are homogeneously creative

practical_risk_of_llm_creative_assistanc.name: practical_risk_of_llm_creative_assistance_converging_users_across_models
practical_risk_of_llm_creative_assistanc.description: Because today's LLMs behave similarly to each other at the population level, using any of them as a creative partner—regardless of which model is chosen—may drive users toward a shared, limited creative output space. This risk is stated as an implication but not yet empirically closed for real-world use.
practical_risk_of_llm_creative_assistanc.paper: Large language models are homogeneously creative

standardized_creativity_task_comparison_.name: standardized_creativity_task_comparison_of_llms_and_humans_as_method
standardized_creativity_task_comparison_.description: The study elicits creative responses from many humans and many LLMs using standardized creativity tasks and compares population-level response diversity, enabling a many-to-many cross-group comparison rather than a single-model analysis.
standardized_creativity_task_comparison_.paper: Large language models are homogeneously creative

replication_and_extension_of_cross_llm_h.name: replication_and_extension_of_cross_llm_homogeneity_beyond_standardized_tasks
replication_and_extension_of_cross_llm_h.description: The current finding relies on standardized creativity tasks; it remains open whether the same cross-LLM homogeneity holds in more naturalistic, open-ended, or domain-specific creative contexts beyond the paradigms tested.
replication_and_extension_of_cross_llm_h.paper: Large language models are homogeneously creative

causal_mechanism_producing_cross_llm_cre.name: causal_mechanism_producing_cross_llm_creative_homogeneity
causal_mechanism_producing_cross_llm_cre.description: The paper documents that LLMs are homogeneously creative but does not identify the mechanism responsible—whether shared training data, architectural similarities, alignment procedures, or other factors drive convergence across models.
causal_mechanism_producing_cross_llm_cre.paper: Large language models are homogeneously creative

creative_performance_stability_in_older_.name: creative_performance_stability_in_older_adulthood
creative_performance_stability_in_older_.description: Previous research indicates that creative performance shows age-related stability in older adulthood, rather than a straightforward decline. This stability is a key empirical observation motivating the study.
creative_performance_stability_in_older_.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

intelligence_as_mechanism_for_creative_s.name: intelligence_as_mechanism_for_creative_stability_in_aging
intelligence_as_mechanism_for_creative_s.description: Mediation analyses demonstrate that intelligence measures serve as underlying cognitive mechanisms explaining the stability of creative thinking in older age. This provides a specific mechanistic account of why creativity is preserved.
intelligence_as_mechanism_for_creative_s.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

cognitive_basis_of_creative_stability_ac.name: cognitive_basis_of_creative_stability_across_lifespan
cognitive_basis_of_creative_stability_ac.description: While creative performance stability in older adulthood is established, the specific underlying cognitive basis for this invariability across the lifespan remains an open question beyond the intelligence mediation reported here.
cognitive_basis_of_creative_stability_ac.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

associative_thinking_role_in_creative_st.name: associative_thinking_role_in_creative_stability_in_aging
associative_thinking_role_in_creative_st.description: The paper frames associative thinking as relevant to creative ability in older adulthood, but the specific contribution of associative thinking mechanisms to age-related creative stability remains to be fully characterized relative to intelligence.
associative_thinking_role_in_creative_st.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

practical_benefits_of_creativity_for_hea.name: practical_benefits_of_creativity_for_healthy_aging
practical_benefits_of_creativity_for_hea.description: The paper asserts that successful problem-solving and enhanced creative ability may improve physical health, cognitive well-being, and overall independence of older adults, framing creativity as beneficial for active aging.
practical_benefits_of_creativity_for_hea.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

replication_of_intelligence_mediation_of.name: replication_of_intelligence_mediation_of_creative_stability_in_larger_diverse_samples
replication_of_intelligence_mediation_of.description: The intelligence-as-mediator finding for creative stability in aging should be replicated in independent and more diverse older adult samples to confirm generalizability beyond the current study.
replication_of_intelligence_mediation_of.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

longitudinal_account_of_creativity_and_i.name: longitudinal_account_of_creativity_and_intelligence_in_aging
longitudinal_account_of_creativity_and_i.description: The cross-sectional mediation findings leave open whether longitudinal change in intelligence tracks longitudinal preservation of creativity, requiring prospective studies across the aging lifespan.
longitudinal_account_of_creativity_and_i.paper: Associative thinking and creative ability in older adulthood

abstract_words_alter_semantic_network_st.name: abstract_words_alter_semantic_network_structure
abstract_words_alter_semantic_network_st.description: Adding abstract words to semantic memory estimations significantly altered network structures, producing more interconnected networks (higher clustering coefficients, lower modularity) and more efficient networks (shorter path lengths) compared to concrete-word-only networks.
abstract_words_alter_semantic_network_st.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

abstract_words_more_resilient_under_simu.name: abstract_words_more_resilient_under_simulated_network_attacks
abstract_words_more_resilient_under_simu.description: Abstract words remained connected in the networks longer than concrete words under simulated percolation-style attacks, indicating greater structural resilience of abstract concepts in semantic memory networks.
abstract_words_more_resilient_under_simu.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

abstract_words_minimize_age_related_diff.name: abstract_words_minimize_age_related_differences_in_semantic_networks
abstract_words_minimize_age_related_diff.description: When abstract words were included in semantic network estimations, age-related differences in network structure were substantially reduced or minimized, whereas group-based analyses with predominantly concrete concepts reproduced the classic pattern of less efficiency and more segregation in older adults.
abstract_words_minimize_age_related_diff.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

independent_effects_of_semantic_diversit.name: independent_effects_of_semantic_diversity_and_concreteness_on_network_connectivity
independent_effects_of_semantic_diversit.description: Semantic diversity and concreteness each independently predicted node connectivity in semantic networks, with more semantically diverse or more abstract words being more connected in the networks.
independent_effects_of_semantic_diversit.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

discrepancy_between_individual_based_and.name: discrepancy_between_individual_based_and_group_based_network_age_differences
discrepancy_between_individual_based_and.description: Individual-based semantic memory network analyses showed minimal age-related differences when abstract words were included, while group-based network analyses revealed more prominent age differences consistent with prior literature, highlighting the importance of the analytical approach chosen.
discrepancy_between_individual_based_and.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

prior_semantic_memory_aging_research_foc.name: prior_semantic_memory_aging_research_focused_predominantly_on_concrete_concepts
prior_semantic_memory_aging_research_foc.description: Most previous research on semantic memory networks and aging focused on concrete concepts, leaving the role of abstract concepts in age-related network changes understudied despite abstract concepts comprising a significant portion of human knowledge.
prior_semantic_memory_aging_research_foc.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

generalization_of_abstract_word_aging_fi.name: generalization_of_abstract_word_aging_finding_beyond_current_sample
generalization_of_abstract_word_aging_fi.description: The study examined 47 younger and 49 older adults, and it is unclear whether the minimization of age differences when including abstract words generalizes to broader age ranges, other languages, or more diverse populations.
generalization_of_abstract_word_aging_fi.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_abs.name: causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_abstract_words_reduce_age_related_network_differences
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_abs.description: The study documents that abstract words minimize age-related semantic network differences but does not establish the cognitive or neural mechanisms by which abstract concepts confer this resilience or why they are more highly connected regardless of age.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_why_abs.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

importance_of_analyzing_semantic_network.name: importance_of_analyzing_semantic_networks_with_multiple_approaches
importance_of_analyzing_semantic_network.description: The study demonstrates that using both individual-based and group-based network analyses, and including words varying in concreteness and semantic diversity, substantially changes conclusions about age-related semantic memory differences.
importance_of_analyzing_semantic_network.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

semantic_diversity_as_a_word_property_in.name: semantic_diversity_as_a_word_property_influencing_network_connectivity
semantic_diversity_as_a_word_property_in.description: Beyond concreteness, semantic diversity—the degree to which a word appears in varied semantic contexts—independently predicted how connected words were within semantic memory networks, pointing to semantic diversity as a distinct lexical property shaping mental lexicon organization.
semantic_diversity_as_a_word_property_in.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

extension_of_semantic_network_aging_find.name: extension_of_semantic_network_aging_findings_to_abstract_and_semantically_diverse_words
extension_of_semantic_network_aging_find.description: The study aims to advance understanding of age-related differences in semantic memory by incorporating abstract and semantically diverse words into network estimation—a gap not addressed by prior aging literature.
extension_of_semantic_network_aging_find.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

generalization_of_abstract_word_network_.name: generalization_of_abstract_word_network_findings_to_other_semantic_domains_or_tasks
generalization_of_abstract_word_network_.description: The current findings are based on semantic relatedness judgments; it remains unknown whether the structural advantages of abstract words in semantic networks would generalize to other semantic tasks (e.g., verbal fluency, free association) or to non-noun or non-English word classes.
generalization_of_abstract_word_network_.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

role_of_abstract_words_in_moderating_age.name: role_of_abstract_words_in_moderating_age_related_semantic_network_decline
role_of_abstract_words_in_moderating_age.description: The authors claim that including abstract words in semantic network analyses is critical because they are more interconnected, more resilient to degradation, and their inclusion substantially changes the picture of how aging affects the mental lexicon.
role_of_abstract_words_in_moderating_age.paper: Semantic memory network resilience in aging: The role of abstract and semantically diverse words.

mvt_application_to_semantic_memory_searc.name: MVT_application_to_semantic_memory_search_in_creativity
mvt_application_to_semantic_memory_searc.description: The study applies the Marginal Value Theorem (MVT) from optimal foraging theory to semantic memory search during an associative fluency task, providing a novel theoretical lens for understanding clustering and switching dynamics in creativity research.
mvt_application_to_semantic_memory_searc.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

most_responses_align_with_mvt_prediction.name: most_responses_align_with_MVT_predictions_in_associative_fluency
most_responses_align_with_mvt_prediction.description: During an associative fluency task with polysemous words, the majority of participant responses followed MVT predictions, indicating that optimal foraging principles broadly govern semantic memory search.
most_responses_align_with_mvt_prediction.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

subset_of_responses_deviate_from_mvt_pre.name: subset_of_responses_deviate_from_MVT_predictions
subset_of_responses_deviate_from_mvt_pre.description: A subset of semantic memory search responses systematically deviated from MVT predictions, suggesting that an alternative cognitive policy operates alongside optimal foraging in associative fluency.
subset_of_responses_deviate_from_mvt_pre.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

functional_brain_connectivity_predicts_m.name: functional_brain_connectivity_predicts_MVT_deviant_patterns
functional_brain_connectivity_predicts_m.description: Connectome-based modeling showed that functional brain connectivity predicted the MVT-deviant response patterns observed during the associative fluency task.
functional_brain_connectivity_predicts_m.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

brain_connectivity_creativity_relationsh.name: brain_connectivity_creativity_relationship_mediated_by_MVT_deviant_patterns
brain_connectivity_creativity_relationsh.description: Connectome-based modeling revealed that MVT-deviant search patterns mediated the relationship between functional brain connectivity and creative performance, identifying a neurocognitive pathway linking brain organization to creativity.
brain_connectivity_creativity_relationsh.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

cognitive_policy_for_creativity_differs_.name: cognitive_policy_for_creativity_differs_from_policy_optimizing_fluency
cognitive_policy_for_creativity_differs_.description: The study concludes that the cognitive policy favoring creative performance (MVT-deviant search) differs from the policy that optimizes fluency (MVT-conforming search), suggesting dissociable strategic modes of semantic memory search.
cognitive_policy_for_creativity_differs_.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

novel_measures_of_semantic_search_introd.name: novel_measures_of_semantic_search_introduced
novel_measures_of_semantic_search_introd.description: The study introduces novel quantitative measures of semantic memory search derived from the MVT framework, capturing both conforming and deviant foraging patterns during associative fluency.
novel_measures_of_semantic_search_introd.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

replication_of_mvt_behavioral_findings_i.name: replication_of_MVT_behavioral_findings_in_independent_sample
replication_of_mvt_behavioral_findings_i.description: The behavioral findings regarding MVT-conforming and MVT-deviant response patterns were replicated in an independent participant sample, supporting the robustness of the original results.
replication_of_mvt_behavioral_findings_i.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

causal_account_of_how_mvt_deviant_patter.name: causal_account_of_how_MVT_deviant_patterns_produce_creative_advantage
causal_account_of_how_mvt_deviant_patter.description: It remains unclear whether MVT-deviant search patterns causally produce creative advantages or whether both are jointly caused by an underlying brain organization, as the current mediation analysis is correlational.
causal_account_of_how_mvt_deviant_patter.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

generalization_of_mvt_framework_beyond_p.name: generalization_of_MVT_framework_beyond_polysemous_word_fluency_task
generalization_of_mvt_framework_beyond_p.description: The study relied on a specific associative fluency task using polysemous words; whether MVT-based measures of semantic search generalize to other creativity paradigms or tasks remains untested.
generalization_of_mvt_framework_beyond_p.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

generalization_of_mvt_connectivity_media.name: generalization_of_MVT_connectivity_mediation_beyond_current_sample
generalization_of_mvt_connectivity_media.description: The connectome-based mediation findings were obtained in a specific sample; independent replication in larger or more diverse samples is needed to confirm generalizability.
generalization_of_mvt_connectivity_media.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

mvt_as_theoretical_framework_for_semanti.name: MVT_as_theoretical_framework_for_semantic_memory_search_in_creativity
mvt_as_theoretical_framework_for_semanti.description: The study introduces the Marginal Value Theorem as a theoretical framework for modeling the dynamics of semantic memory search in the context of creative cognition, integrating optimal foraging theory with creativity research.
mvt_as_theoretical_framework_for_semanti.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

integration_of_mvt_framework_with_existi.name: integration_of_MVT_framework_with_existing_clustering_switching_account
integration_of_mvt_framework_with_existi.description: The study integrates the MVT framework with the established clustering-switching framework of semantic memory search, providing a more quantitative and principled account of how switching behavior relates to creativity.
integration_of_mvt_framework_with_existi.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

mechanistic_account_of_why_mvt_deviant_s.name: mechanistic_account_of_why_MVT_deviant_search_supports_creativity
mechanistic_account_of_why_mvt_deviant_s.description: The neural and cognitive mechanisms through which MVT-deviant patterns confer a creative advantage remain to be specified; the current findings identify the pattern and its brain connectivity correlates but do not explain the mechanism.
mechanistic_account_of_why_mvt_deviant_s.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

optimal_foraging_theory_as_bridging_fram.name: optimal_foraging_theory_as_bridging_framework_for_neurocognitive_creativity_research
optimal_foraging_theory_as_bridging_fram.description: The study argues that optimal foraging theory, via the MVT, provides a principled bridge between behavioral, cognitive, and neural levels of analysis in creativity research.
optimal_foraging_theory_as_bridging_fram.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

extent_to_which_mvt_deviation_is_domain_.name: extent_to_which_MVT_deviation_is_domain_general_or_creativity_specific
extent_to_which_mvt_deviation_is_domain_.description: It is unclear whether MVT-deviant foraging patterns reflect a domain-general cognitive style or a process specific to creative search; this distinction has implications for theories of creativity.
extent_to_which_mvt_deviation_is_domain_.paper: Switching, fast and slow: Deciphering the dynamics of memory search, its brain connectivity patterns, and its role in creativity

creativeable_ai_creativity_training_prog.name: Creativeable AI creativity training program
creativeable_ai_creativity_training_prog.description: An AI-powered creativity training program providing automated feedback and adaptive difficulty adjustment for creative story writing tasks without human intervention, evaluated across 385 participants in four conditions.
creativeable_ai_creativity_training_prog.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

creativity_improvements_across_all_creat.name: Creativity improvements across all Creativeable conditions
creativity_improvements_across_all_creat.description: Participants showed creativity improvements across all four training conditions as measured by the alternative uses task, though the degree of improvement varied by condition.
creativity_improvements_across_all_creat.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

feedback_with_constant_difficulty_as_mos.name: Feedback with constant difficulty as most effective Creativeable condition
feedback_with_constant_difficulty_as_mos.description: The F/CL condition (feedback with constant difficulty) produced the most notable creativity gains, followed by NF/CL and NF/VL, while F/VL showed comparatively smaller improvements.
feedback_with_constant_difficulty_as_mos.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

tension_between_feedback_and_increasing_.name: Tension between feedback and increasing task complexity for creative growth
tension_between_feedback_and_increasing_.description: Combining adaptive difficulty with feedback (F/VL) led to comparatively smaller improvements, suggesting that escalating task complexity may interfere with the benefits of structured feedback for sustained creative growth.
tension_between_feedback_and_increasing_.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

ai_as_scalable_tool_for_democratizing_cr.name: AI as scalable tool for democratizing creativity training
ai_as_scalable_tool_for_democratizing_cr.description: The study argues that AI-powered systems like Creativeable can democratize creativity training by offering scalable, personalized interventions without the need for human trainers.
ai_as_scalable_tool_for_democratizing_cr.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

optimal_balance_of_feedback_and_adaptive.name: Optimal balance of feedback and adaptive difficulty for creativity training
optimal_balance_of_feedback_and_adaptive.description: The study raises the open question of what the ideal combination of structured feedback and task difficulty progression is to maximize sustained creative growth in AI-powered training systems.
optimal_balance_of_feedback_and_adaptive.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

generalization_of_creativeable_effective.name: Generalization of Creativeable effectiveness beyond the current sample and paradigm
generalization_of_creativeable_effective.description: The study's findings are based on a single sample of 385 participants completing creative story writing tasks; generalizability to other populations, creativity domains, and training durations remains unestablished.
generalization_of_creativeable_effective.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

longitudinal_sustainability_of_creativit.name: Longitudinal sustainability of creativity gains from Creativeable
longitudinal_sustainability_of_creativit.description: The study measures pre-post changes across five training rounds but does not assess whether creativity gains are maintained over longer time periods after training ends.
longitudinal_sustainability_of_creativit.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

causal_mechanism_linking_adaptive_diffic.name: Causal mechanism linking adaptive difficulty to reduced training effectiveness
causal_mechanism_linking_adaptive_diffic.description: It remains unclear why adaptive difficulty (variable level) conditions produced smaller gains, particularly when combined with feedback, and what cognitive mechanism underlies this pattern.
causal_mechanism_linking_adaptive_diffic.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

role_of_semantically_distant_word_prompt.name: Role of semantically distant word prompts in driving creativity gains
role_of_semantically_distant_word_prompt.description: The training program uses semantically distant word prompts for story writing, but the specific contribution of semantic distance in word prompts to creativity improvement relative to other training elements is not isolated.
role_of_semantically_distant_word_prompt.paper: Creativeable: Leveraging AI for Personalized Creativity Enhancement

emotion_concepts_have_distinct_featural_.name: emotion_concepts_have_distinct_featural_structure_from_concrete_and_nonemotion_abstract_concepts
emotion_concepts_have_distinct_featural_.description: Analyses revealed statistically significant differences in the distribution of features among emotion, nonemotion abstract, and concrete conceptual categories, indicating that emotion concepts occupy a distinct representational niche relative to both concrete and other abstract concepts.

interoceptive_states_as_distinguishing_d.name: interoceptive_states_as_distinguishing_dimension_of_emotion_concept_representation
interoceptive_states_as_distinguishing_d.description: The study hypothesized that the relative importance of interoceptive states in emotional experience would drive distinctions in how emotion concepts are represented compared to other abstract concepts such as equality or tyranny.

emotion_concepts_show_less_variability_t.name: emotion_concepts_show_less_variability_than_expected_under_constructionist_views
emotion_concepts_show_less_variability_t.description: Contrary to the prediction based on constructionist theories of emotion, emotion concepts were associated with less variability in generated features compared to concrete and nonemotion abstract concepts.

large_normative_feature_dataset_for_emot.name: large_normative_feature_dataset_for_emotion_concepts_as_empirical_resource
large_normative_feature_dataset_for_emot.description: The study derived a large set of normative feature data for emotion concepts, providing an empirical resource for studying the conceptual representation of emotions alongside concrete and nonemotion abstract concepts.

historical_neglect_of_emotion_concepts_r.name: historical_neglect_of_emotion_concepts_relative_to_concrete_concepts_in_semantic_organization_research
historical_neglect_of_emotion_concepts_r.description: Theories of semantic organization have historically prioritized concrete concepts pertaining to inanimate objects and natural kinds, leaving accounts of emotion concept representation almost exclusively framed through contrast with concrete rather than other abstract concepts.

validation_of_emotion_concept_featural_s.name: validation_of_emotion_concept_featural_structure_findings_across_languages
validation_of_emotion_concept_featural_s.description: The authors note that findings were obtained from a sample restricted to native English speakers and emphasize the importance of validating results across speakers of different languages.

generalization_of_emotion_concept_featur.name: generalization_of_emotion_concept_featural_structure_beyond_native_english_speakers
generalization_of_emotion_concept_featur.description: The sample restriction to native English speakers limits the generalizability of findings about how emotion concepts are featurally represented, warranting cross-linguistic validation.

constructionist_view_of_emotion_as_theor.name: constructionist_view_of_emotion_as_theoretical_framework_for_feature_variability_prediction
constructionist_view_of_emotion_as_theor.description: Based on constructionist views of emotion, the authors predicted that emotion concepts would demonstrate greater variability in their representation than concrete and other abstract concepts, a prediction that was not supported by the data.

feature_listing_task_as_method_for_deriv.name: feature_listing_task_as_method_for_deriving_conceptual_structure_across_concept_types
feature_listing_task_as_method_for_deriv.description: Participants listed features for emotion, nonemotion abstract, and concrete concepts; these were coded into discrete categories and contrasted, enabling comparison of featural representation across conceptual types.

dsi_applied_to_scientific_abstracts_and_.name: DSI_applied_to_scientific_abstracts_and_titles
dsi_applied_to_scientific_abstracts_and_.description: Divergent Semantic Integration (DSI), a computational measure correlating with originality from creativity science, was applied to 99,557 scientific abstracts and titles from the Web of Science to examine originality at scale in academic literature.
dsi_applied_to_scientific_abstracts_and_.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

dsi_positively_predicts_citation_count.name: DSI_positively_predicts_citation_count
dsi_positively_predicts_citation_count.description: Modeling the base-10 logarithm of citation count after 5 years with DSI yields a statistically significant positive correlation across all fields of research, with an adjusted R² of 0.13.
dsi_positively_predicts_citation_count.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

dsi_varies_by_subject_and_field_of_resea.name: DSI_varies_by_subject_and_field_of_research
dsi_varies_by_subject_and_field_of_resea.description: Statistically significant differences in DSI are observed between scientific subjects and fields of research, suggesting that originality as measured by DSI is not uniform across disciplines.
dsi_varies_by_subject_and_field_of_resea.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

dsi_shows_slight_rise_over_time.name: DSI_shows_slight_rise_over_time
dsi_shows_slight_rise_over_time.description: A slight temporal increase in DSI is observed across the corpus, suggesting that originality in scientific writing has grown modestly over the period sampled.
dsi_shows_slight_rise_over_time.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

causal_account_of_how_dsi_predicts_citat.name: causal_account_of_how_DSI_predicts_citation_count
causal_account_of_how_dsi_predicts_citat.description: The study establishes a correlation between DSI and citation count but does not establish a causal mechanism explaining why more original titles and abstracts attract more citations.
causal_account_of_how_dsi_predicts_citat.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

generalization_of_dsi_citation_finding_b.name: generalization_of_DSI_citation_finding_beyond_current_corpus_and_fields
generalization_of_dsi_citation_finding_b.description: The finding is based on a specific corpus from Web of Science; whether the DSI–citation relationship generalizes to other databases, time periods, or non-covered fields remains untested.
generalization_of_dsi_citation_finding_b.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

practical_utility_of_dsi_for_predicting_.name: practical_utility_of_DSI_for_predicting_scientific_impact
practical_utility_of_dsi_for_predicting_.description: The paper is described as research-in-progress, suggesting the ultimate goal of developing DSI as a practical predictive tool for scientific impact has not yet been fully realized or validated.
practical_utility_of_dsi_for_predicting_.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

explanatory_power_of_dsi_for_citation_va.name: explanatory_power_of_DSI_for_citation_variance
explanatory_power_of_dsi_for_citation_va.description: The adjusted R² of 0.13 indicates that DSI explains only a modest portion of variance in citation counts, leaving the majority of variance unexplained and pointing to the need for richer models.
explanatory_power_of_dsi_for_citation_va.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

dsi_as_corpus_level_measure_of_scientifi.name: DSI_as_corpus_level_measure_of_scientific_originality
dsi_as_corpus_level_measure_of_scientifi.description: DSI is operationalized as a computational measure applicable to text corpora, enabling quantification of originality in scientific writing at large scale without manual scoring.
dsi_as_corpus_level_measure_of_scientifi.paper: Originality in scientific titles and abstracts can predict citation count

age_differences_in_semantic_network_stru.name: age_differences_in_semantic_network_structure_via_free_recall
age_differences_in_semantic_network_stru.description: Older adults displayed higher average shortest path length and modularity values (less integrated networks), while younger adults showed higher clustering coefficients (more interconnected networks), as derived from a single-list free recall paradigm with 318 participants.
age_differences_in_semantic_network_stru.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

temporal_contiguity_effect_with_forward_.name: temporal_contiguity_effect_with_forward_asymmetry_in_both_age_groups
temporal_contiguity_effect_with_forward_.description: Both younger and older adults showed a temporal contiguity effect with forward asymmetry in retrieval dynamics, indicating that sequential temporal associations guide recall in both groups.
temporal_contiguity_effect_with_forward_.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

attenuated_temporal_contiguity_effect_in.name: attenuated_temporal_contiguity_effect_in_older_adults
attenuated_temporal_contiguity_effect_in.description: The temporal contiguity effect with forward asymmetry was less pronounced in older adults compared to younger adults, suggesting a diminished capacity to reinstate temporal context for retrieval with age.
attenuated_temporal_contiguity_effect_in.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

subgroup_diverging_from_average_retrieva.name: subgroup_diverging_from_average_retrieval_dynamics
subgroup_diverging_from_average_retrieva.description: A subset of participants deviated from average retrieval dynamic curves: one subgroup relied on non-temporal mechanisms and another employed a backward direction in memory search, revealing heterogeneity in retrieval strategies.
subgroup_diverging_from_average_retrieva.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

forward_temporal_association_predicts_hi.name: forward_temporal_association_predicts_highest_recall_performance
forward_temporal_association_predicts_hi.description: Participants who relied on forward temporal associations during retrieval demonstrated the highest free recall performance compared to those using non-temporal or backward strategies.
forward_temporal_association_predicts_hi.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

free_recall_paradigm_for_semantic_networ.name: free_recall_paradigm_for_semantic_network_and_retrieval_dynamics_analysis
free_recall_paradigm_for_semantic_networ.description: A single-list free recall paradigm was used with 318 participants divided into younger (25–55 years) and older (61–88 years) groups; semantic memory networks were constructed by analyzing co-occurrence and sequence of recalled words.
free_recall_paradigm_for_semantic_networ.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

older_adult_semantic_networks_less_flexi.name: older_adult_semantic_networks_less_flexible_small_world_like_structure
older_adult_semantic_networks_less_flexi.description: Older adults' semantic memory networks appear to exhibit a less flexible, small-world-like structure compared to younger adults, as evidenced by higher path length and modularity alongside lower clustering.
older_adult_semantic_networks_less_flexi.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_red.name: causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_reduced_temporal_context_reinstatement_produces_lower_recall_in_aging
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_red.description: The study links attenuated temporal contiguity effects to lower free recall in older adults but does not establish the causal mechanism by which diminished temporal context reinstatement produces this recall deficit.
causal_or_mechanistic_account_of_how_red.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

generalization_of_free_recall_semantic_n.name: generalization_of_free_recall_semantic_network_aging_findings_beyond_current_sample
generalization_of_free_recall_semantic_n.description: The study's findings on age-related network structure and retrieval dynamics are based on a specific sample; replication in independent and more diverse samples is needed to confirm generalizability.
generalization_of_free_recall_semantic_n.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

relationship_between_retrieval_strategy_.name: relationship_between_retrieval_strategy_subgroup_membership_and_semantic_network_structure
relationship_between_retrieval_strategy_.description: The study identifies retrieval strategy subgroups (forward temporal, non-temporal, backward) and age-related network differences, but the relationship between individual network structure and subgroup membership is not fully established.
relationship_between_retrieval_strategy_.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

longitudinal_account_of_age_related_chan.name: longitudinal_account_of_age_related_changes_in_semantic_network_and_retrieval_dynamics
longitudinal_account_of_age_related_chan.description: The current study is cross-sectional; a longitudinal design would be needed to establish whether the observed age differences reflect developmental change over time within individuals.
longitudinal_account_of_age_related_chan.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

generalization_of_retrieval_subgroup_fin.name: generalization_of_retrieval_subgroup_findings_to_other_memory_paradigms
generalization_of_retrieval_subgroup_fin.description: The identification of forward-temporal, non-temporal, and backward retrieval subgroups is based on a single-list free recall paradigm; whether these subgroups emerge in other memory paradigms remains unknown.
generalization_of_retrieval_subgroup_fin.paper: How Retrieval Processes Change with Age: Exploring Age Differences in Semantic Network and Retrieval Dynamics

domain_specific_question_asking_improves.name: domain_specific_question_asking_improves_with_knowledge_acquisition
domain_specific_question_asking_improves.description: Undergraduate students who completed a semester-long Intro to Psychology course showed improvement in domain-specific question-asking from the start to the end of the semester, indicating that acquired knowledge reshapes domain-specific inquiry abilities.
domain_specific_question_asking_improves.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

general_question_asking_remains_stable_o.name: general_question_asking_remains_stable_or_declines_over_a_semester
general_question_asking_remains_stable_o.description: In contrast to domain-specific question-asking, general question-asking ability did not improve and either remained stable or declined over the course of the semester, suggesting knowledge acquisition does not broadly transfer to general inquiry skills.
general_question_asking_remains_stable_o.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

question_asking_abilities_predict_academ.name: question_asking_abilities_predict_academic_outcomes_differentially_by_assessment_type
question_asking_abilities_predict_academ.description: Both general and domain-specific question-asking abilities were negatively related to closed-ended test performance and positively related to open-ended project performance, highlighting that assessment format moderates the educational value of question-asking skills.
question_asking_abilities_predict_academ.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

longitudinal_design_for_studying_questio.name: longitudinal_design_for_studying_question_asking_change_over_time
