longitudinal_design_for_studying_questio.description: The study employed a within-subject pre/post design across one academic semester, assessing question-asking at the start and end of the course to capture longitudinal change in inquiry abilities in response to knowledge acquisition.
longitudinal_design_for_studying_questio.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

knowledge_acquisition_as_mechanism_shapi.name: knowledge_acquisition_as_mechanism_shaping_domain_specific_question_asking
knowledge_acquisition_as_mechanism_shapi.description: The study frames domain-specific knowledge gained through a psychology course as the driver of improvements in domain-specific question-asking, but the causal direction has not been experimentally established.
knowledge_acquisition_as_mechanism_shapi.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

generalization_of_question_asking_academ.name: generalization_of_question_asking_academic_outcome_findings_beyond_introductory_psychology
generalization_of_question_asking_academ.description: The findings on how question-asking relates to academic performance were obtained in a single undergraduate Intro to Psychology course, leaving open whether these relationships generalize to other disciplines, course levels, or educational settings.
generalization_of_question_asking_academ.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

educational_value_of_fostering_question_.name: educational_value_of_fostering_question_asking_skills
educational_value_of_fostering_question_.description: The study highlights the practical implication that educational interventions fostering stronger question-asking skills could improve open-ended academic performance, but direct instructional interventions have not yet been tested.
educational_value_of_fostering_question_.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

differential_trajectory_of_general_versu.name: differential_trajectory_of_general_versus_domain_specific_question_asking
differential_trajectory_of_general_versu.description: The study reveals a dissociation between general and domain-specific question-asking trajectories over a semester, with domain-specific improving and general remaining stable or declining, suggesting knowledge effects on questioning are domain-bounded.
differential_trajectory_of_general_versu.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

replication_of_question_asking_academic_.name: replication_of_question_asking_academic_outcome_finding_in_independent_samples
replication_of_question_asking_academic_.description: The relationship between question-asking abilities and both closed- and open-ended academic outcomes was observed in a single sample of 68 undergraduates, and replication in larger and more diverse samples is needed to establish robustness.
replication_of_question_asking_academic_.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

mechanisms_linking_question_asking_impro.name: mechanisms_linking_question_asking_improvement_to_knowledge_acquisition
mechanisms_linking_question_asking_impro.description: The study establishes that domain-specific question-asking improves over a semester but does not explain the cognitive or pedagogical mechanisms by which knowledge acquisition reshapes inquiry ability.
mechanisms_linking_question_asking_impro.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

negative_relationship_between_question_a.name: negative_relationship_between_question_asking_and_closed_ended_test_performance
negative_relationship_between_question_a.description: Both general and domain-specific question-asking abilities were negatively associated with closed-ended multiple-choice exam scores, suggesting that students with stronger questioning skills may be less oriented toward or advantaged by conventional testing formats.
negative_relationship_between_question_a.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

question_asking_originality_and_complexi.name: question_asking_originality_and_complexity_as_measurable_constructs
question_asking_originality_and_complexi.description: The study operationalizes originality and complexity of questions as quantifiable constructs assessed via both general and domain-specific tasks, providing a methodological basis for studying question-asking longitudinally in educational contexts.
question_asking_originality_and_complexi.paper: Knowledge reshapes inquiry by changing question asking ability and impacting academic assessment

incomplete_understanding_of_neural_dynam.name: Incomplete understanding of neural dynamics underlying generation and evaluation of creative ideas
incomplete_understanding_of_neural_dynam.description: Despite the findings, the authors note that how the networks are recruited dynamically during generation and evaluation remains 'far from understood,' indicating that the current work is a partial step rather than a complete resolution.
incomplete_understanding_of_neural_dynam.paper: Neural dynamics during the generation and evaluation of creative and non-creative ideas

generalization_of_creative_generation_ev.name: Generalization of creative generation-evaluation neural dynamic findings beyond alternative uses task
generalization_of_creative_generation_ev.description: The paradigm uses responses to common objects (likely an alternative uses-type task), leaving open whether the identified neural dynamic patterns generalize to other creative generation and evaluation paradigms.
generalization_of_creative_generation_ev.paper: Neural dynamics during the generation and evaluation of creative and non-creative ideas

spot_the_spy_online_game_as_empirical_to.name: Spot the Spy online game as empirical tool for studying question-asking
spot_the_spy_online_game_as_empirical_to.description: The authors developed an online game called Spot the Spy in which players must identify a hidden spy by asking a chatbot questions, providing a naturalistic gamified environment for studying question-asking behavior empirically.
spot_the_spy_online_game_as_empirical_to.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

intelligence_predicts_effective_question.name: Intelligence predicts effective questioning strategies and game performance
intelligence_predicts_effective_question.description: Across both studies, higher intelligence scores were associated with more effective questioning strategies and better overall performance in the Spot the Spy game.
intelligence_predicts_effective_question.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

scarcity_of_empirical_research_on_questi.name: Scarcity of empirical research on question-asking and its cognitive correlates
scarcity_of_empirical_research_on_questi.description: Despite the cognitive and social importance of question-asking, the paper identifies a lack of empirical research on how people ask questions and how question-asking relates to creativity, curiosity, and intelligence.
scarcity_of_empirical_research_on_questi.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

optimal_question_asking_strategies_in_th.name: Optimal question-asking strategies in the Spot the Spy game
optimal_question_asking_strategies_in_th.description: The studies identified specific question-asking strategies associated with optimal performance in the game, revealing patterns of effective information-seeking behavior in a naturalistic setting.
optimal_question_asking_strategies_in_th.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

relationship_between_question_asking_and.name: Relationship between question-asking and creativity in the game context
relationship_between_question_asking_and.description: While creativity was measured alongside intelligence and curiosity, the paper does not clearly establish whether and how creative ability specifically predicts question-asking behavior or game performance.
relationship_between_question_asking_and.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

relationship_between_curiosity_and_quest.name: Relationship between curiosity and question-asking in the game context
relationship_between_curiosity_and_quest.description: Curiosity was included as a measured cognitive variable, but the paper does not clearly establish whether curiosity specifically and independently predicts question-asking effectiveness or game performance.
relationship_between_curiosity_and_quest.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

generalization_of_spot_the_spy_findings_.name: Generalization of Spot the Spy findings beyond the current paradigm and sample
generalization_of_spot_the_spy_findings_.description: The findings on intelligence and questioning strategies are based on specific samples in a particular gamified setting, and it is unclear whether they generalize to other question-asking contexts, populations, or cognitive tasks.
generalization_of_spot_the_spy_findings_.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

causal_account_of_how_intelligence_produ.name: Causal account of how intelligence produces better questioning strategies
causal_account_of_how_intelligence_produ.description: The study establishes a correlational link between intelligence and questioning effectiveness, but does not provide a mechanistic or causal account of why higher intelligence leads to more effective question-asking strategies.
causal_account_of_how_intelligence_produ.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

potential_of_gamified_environments_for_s.name: Potential of gamified environments for studying and training cognitive processes
potential_of_gamified_environments_for_s.description: The paper argues that digital serious games offer significant advantages for studying cognitive processes in natural settings and can advance development of educational and training tools for strategic thinking and question-asking.
potential_of_gamified_environments_for_s.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

educational_and_training_applications_of.name: Educational and training applications of the Spot the Spy game framework
educational_and_training_applications_of.description: The paper aspires to use insights from the game to develop educational tools that foster strategic thinking and question-asking capacities, but such applications have not yet been built or evaluated.
educational_and_training_applications_of.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

two_study_design_with_exploratory_and_re.name: Two-study design with exploratory and replication phases
two_study_design_with_exploratory_and_re.description: Study 1 (N=103, online) focused on game development and exploratory validation, while Study 2 (N=100, in-lab) focused on replication and extension of Study 1 findings.
two_study_design_with_exploratory_and_re.paper: Spot the spy: An online game for exploring question-asking in the wild

bfmn_methodology_for_comparing_human_and.name: BFMN_methodology_for_comparing_human_and_LLM_STEM_mindsets
bfmn_methodology_for_comparing_human_and.description: Behavioural forma mentis networks (BFMNs) are used to quantify cognitive and emotional associations around STEM concepts for 177 human participants and 177 GPT-3.5-simulated artificial humans split into trainees, experts, and academics. This network approach operationalizes mindset structure as graph-theoretic properties of conceptual association patterns.
bfmn_methodology_for_comparing_human_and.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

human_bfmn_higher_clustering_than_gpt_3_.name: human_BFMN_higher_clustering_than_GPT_3_5
human_bfmn_higher_clustering_than_gpt_3_.description: Human forma mentis networks exhibited significantly higher clustering coefficients than those produced by GPT-3.5, indicating that humans tend to form and close triads of conceptual associations when recollecting STEM ideas, whereas GPT-3.5 produces sparser networks with weaker clustering.
human_bfmn_higher_clustering_than_gpt_3_.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

expert_bfmn_robust_clustering_reflects_s.name: expert_BFMN_robust_clustering_reflects_STEM_concept_integration
expert_bfmn_robust_clustering_reflects_s.description: Human experts specifically demonstrated robust clustering coefficients relative to trainees and academics, reflecting stronger integration of STEM concepts into their cognitive networks. This indicates that expertise level modulates mindset network structure.
expert_bfmn_robust_clustering_reflects_s.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

neutral_positive_framing_of_mathematics_.name: neutral_positive_framing_of_mathematics_in_human_and_GPT_mindsets
neutral_positive_framing_of_mathematics_.description: Both human participants and GPT-3.5 simulations framed 'mathematics' in neutral or positive terms, contrasting with findings from other works sampling STEM high-schoolers, researchers, and other large language models where more negative or different framings were observed.
neutral_positive_framing_of_mathematics_.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

gpt_3_5_limitations_in_replicating_human.name: GPT_3_5_limitations_in_replicating_human_like_STEM_mindsets
gpt_3_5_limitations_in_replicating_human.description: GPT-3.5 produced sparser networks with weaker clustering compared to humans, highlighting its limitations in replicating human-like mindset structure around STEM concepts. This suggests LLM-simulated cognitive networks diverge meaningfully from human associative patterns.
gpt_3_5_limitations_in_replicating_human.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

expertise_level_influence_on_stem_mindse.name: expertise_level_influence_on_STEM_mindset_network_structure
expertise_level_influence_on_stem_mindse.description: The study compares trainees, experts, and academics to assess how expertise shapes STEM-focused mindset structure as captured by BFMN properties. Expertise level is found to modulate clustering and conceptual integration in the networks.
expertise_level_influence_on_stem_mindse.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

causal_account_of_why_gpt_3_5_produces_s.name: causal_account_of_why_GPT_3_5_produces_sparser_STEM_networks
causal_account_of_why_gpt_3_5_produces_s.description: While GPT-3.5 is shown to produce sparser, less clustered BFMN networks than humans, the cognitive or architectural mechanism explaining why LLM-simulated associations fail to replicate human triadic closure in STEM mindsets remains unaddressed. Understanding this would clarify the nature of the human-LLM divergence.
causal_account_of_why_gpt_3_5_produces_s.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

generalization_of_bfmn_stem_mindset_find.name: generalization_of_BFMN_STEM_mindset_findings_beyond_current_sample_and_LLM
generalization_of_bfmn_stem_mindset_find.description: The findings are based on 177 human participants and GPT-3.5 specifically; it is unclear whether the observed differences in clustering and mathematics framing generalize to other populations, expertise domains, or more recent/different LLMs.
generalization_of_bfmn_stem_mindset_find.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

comparison_of_bfmn_stem_mindset_findings.name: comparison_of_BFMN_STEM_mindset_findings_with_prior_population_samples
comparison_of_bfmn_stem_mindset_findings.description: The study situates its mathematics framing results within a broader landscape by contrasting with findings from STEM high-schoolers, researchers, and other LLMs sampled in prior works, providing a cross-study comparative framework for mindset research.
comparison_of_bfmn_stem_mindset_findings.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

cognitive_insights_about_memory_structur.name: cognitive_insights_about_memory_structure_from_STEM_mindset_networks
cognitive_insights_about_memory_structur.description: The BFMN approach is positioned as providing cognitive insights about memory structure by mapping how individuals associate and perceive STEM ideas, bridging mindset research and cognitive network science applied to educational and professional contexts.
cognitive_insights_about_memory_structur.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

replication_of_bfmn_findings_across_inde.name: replication_of_BFMN_findings_across_independent_samples_and_expertise_levels
replication_of_bfmn_findings_across_inde.description: The current study is a single-sample investigation; independent replication with different participant pools and expertise domains is needed to confirm that the clustering coefficient differences and mathematics framing patterns are robust and generalizable.
replication_of_bfmn_findings_across_inde.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

llm_simulated_participants_as_artificial.name: LLM_simulated_participants_as_artificial_humans_methodology
llm_simulated_participants_as_artificial.description: The study employs GPT-3.5 to simulate 177 artificial human participants matched to the human sample size, using the model's outputs to construct BFMNs for comparison with genuine human networks. This constitutes a methodological contribution for cognitive simulation research.
llm_simulated_participants_as_artificial.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

integration_of_bfmn_findings_with_broade.name: integration_of_BFMN_findings_with_broader_cognitive_network_science_of_LLMs
integration_of_bfmn_findings_with_broade.description: The authors position BFMN analysis as contributing to understanding machine limitations and human-AI differences, but full integration with broader cognitive network science frameworks for LLM evaluation remains an aspiration rather than a completed synthesis.
integration_of_bfmn_findings_with_broade.paper: Cognitive networks highlight differences and similarities in the STEM mindsets of human and LLM-simulated trainees, experts and academics

knowledge_modelling_as_interdisciplinary.name: knowledge_modelling_as_interdisciplinary_field
knowledge_modelling_as_interdisciplinary.description: Knowledge modelling is identified as a growing interdisciplinary field at the intersection of computer science, psychology, and network science. The source frames this convergence as a productive and expanding research space.
knowledge_modelling_as_interdisciplinary.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

cognitive_networks_as_framework_for_know.name: cognitive_networks_as_framework_for_knowledge_modelling_and_learning
cognitive_networks_as_framework_for_know.description: Cognitive network science is proposed as a framework for modelling knowledge and understanding learning processes. The source positions network-based representations as central tools for capturing how knowledge is structured and acquired.
cognitive_networks_as_framework_for_know.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

open_questions_in_knowledge_modelling_vi.name: open_questions_in_knowledge_modelling_via_cognitive_networks
open_questions_in_knowledge_modelling_vi.description: The source, as a collection spanning memory, individual differences, communities, and cultural change, leaves open numerous questions about how cognitive network approaches can be further developed, validated, and applied to model knowledge and learning across diverse contexts.
open_questions_in_knowledge_modelling_vi.paper: Knowledge Modelling and Learning through Cognitive Networks

extension_of_multilayer_lexical_findings.name: extension_of_multilayer_lexical_findings_to_diverse_languages_and_populations
extension_of_multilayer_lexical_findings.description: The review implies a need to extend multilayer network insights beyond the current datasets to diverse linguistic communities and clinical populations.
extension_of_multilayer_lexical_findings.paper: Cognitive modelling with multilayer networks: Insights, advancements and future challenges

semantic_memory_network_integration_pred.name: semantic_memory_network_integration_predicts_originality_across_tasks
semantic_memory_network_integration_pred.description: Semantic memory network integration consistently predicted originality of creative output across both the CPS task (Study 1) and the alternative uses task (Study 2), indicating a stable cross-task relationship.
semantic_memory_network_integration_pred.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

ideation_fluency_predicts_originality_ac.name: ideation_fluency_predicts_originality_across_tasks
ideation_fluency_predicts_originality_ac.description: Ideation fluency consistently predicted originality of creative output across both the CPS and divergent thinking (AUT) tasks, suggesting it is a stable predictor of creative performance.
ideation_fluency_predicts_originality_ac.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

usefulness_less_reliably_predicted_by_se.name: usefulness_less_reliably_predicted_by_semantic_memory_and_fluency
usefulness_less_reliably_predicted_by_se.description: Unlike originality, usefulness of creative output was not consistently predicted across tasks; semantic memory network properties predicted usefulness primarily in the CPS task, revealing task-specific associations.
usefulness_less_reliably_predicted_by_se.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

self_evaluations_diverge_from_objective_.name: self_evaluations_diverge_from_objective_creative_outcomes
self_evaluations_diverge_from_objective_.description: Participants' self-evaluations of their creative performance often diverged from objective assessments of originality and usefulness, reflecting metacognitive biases driven by heuristic cues.
self_evaluations_diverge_from_objective_.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

distinct_predictors_of_objective_perform.name: distinct_predictors_of_objective_performance_versus_self_evaluation_in_creativity
distinct_predictors_of_objective_perform.description: The study reveals that semantic memory network integration and ideation fluency drive objective originality, whereas self-evaluations are shaped by separate metacognitive heuristic processes, suggesting dissociable mechanisms.
distinct_predictors_of_objective_perform.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

within_subjects_comparison_of_cps_and_au.name: within_subjects_comparison_of_cps_and_aut_creative_output_and_self_evaluation
within_subjects_comparison_of_cps_and_au.description: Study 2 used a within-subjects design in which participants completed both an alternative uses task and a CPS task, enabling direct comparison of creative output quality and self-evaluation patterns across task types.
within_subjects_comparison_of_cps_and_au.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

semantic_memory_network_estimation_via_r.name: semantic_memory_network_estimation_via_relatedness_judgment_in_creativity_study
semantic_memory_network_estimation_via_r.description: Individual semantic memory networks were estimated from a relatedness judgment task, enabling graph-theoretic properties such as network integration to be linked to creative performance and self-evaluation.
semantic_memory_network_estimation_via_r.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

task_specific_versus_stable_mechanisms_i.name: task_specific_versus_stable_mechanisms_in_creative_performance_and_self_evaluation
task_specific_versus_stable_mechanisms_i.description: The authors argue that some cognitive and metacognitive mechanisms underlying creative performance and self-evaluation are stable across tasks (e.g., fluency and integration for originality), while others are context-sensitive (e.g., semantic properties for usefulness).
task_specific_versus_stable_mechanisms_i.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

generalization_of_semantic_memory_origin.name: generalization_of_semantic_memory_originality_link_beyond_aut_to_cps
generalization_of_semantic_memory_origin.description: The study extends prior findings linking semantic memory network structure to originality in the AUT by demonstrating the same relationship holds in a creative problem-solving task, broadening the generalizability of the link.
generalization_of_semantic_memory_origin.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

causal_mechanisms_underlying_metacogniti.name: causal_mechanisms_underlying_metacognitive_heuristic_biases_in_self_evaluation_of_creativity
causal_mechanisms_underlying_metacogniti.description: While the study demonstrates that self-evaluations diverge from objective performance due to heuristic cues, the specific cognitive mechanisms by which these heuristics produce biased self-evaluation remain unspecified.
causal_mechanisms_underlying_metacogniti.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

generalization_of_usefulness_prediction_.name: generalization_of_usefulness_prediction_findings_beyond_cps_to_other_creativity_tasks
generalization_of_usefulness_prediction_.description: Usefulness was predicted by semantic memory properties in CPS but not reliably in the AUT; whether this task-specificity generalizes to other creative paradigms beyond these two tasks remains open.
generalization_of_usefulness_prediction_.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

replication_of_cross_task_originality_fl.name: replication_of_cross_task_originality_fluency_and_integration_findings_in_independent_samples
replication_of_cross_task_originality_fl.description: The two-study design provides initial convergent evidence, but independent replication in new samples is needed to confirm that ideation fluency and semantic network integration robustly predict originality across creativity tasks.
replication_of_cross_task_originality_fl.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

role_of_metacognitive_awareness_in_moder.name: role_of_metacognitive_awareness_in_moderating_self_evaluation_accuracy_in_creativity
role_of_metacognitive_awareness_in_moder.description: The study reveals metacognitive biases in self-evaluation of creativity but does not address whether individual differences in metacognitive awareness moderate the accuracy of self-evaluations across tasks.
role_of_metacognitive_awareness_in_moder.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

identification_of_specific_heuristic_cue.name: identification_of_specific_heuristic_cues_biasing_originality_self_evaluation
identification_of_specific_heuristic_cue.description: The study attributes self-evaluation divergence to heuristic cues but the specific cues (e.g., fluency, familiarity, effort) that drive biased self-evaluation of originality versus usefulness are not fully characterized.
identification_of_specific_heuristic_cue.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

extension_of_usefulness_semantic_network.name: extension_of_usefulness_semantic_network_link_to_domains_beyond_verbal_creativity
extension_of_usefulness_semantic_network.description: The task-specific relationship between semantic memory network properties and usefulness observed in CPS may or may not generalize to non-verbal or domain-specific creative tasks, leaving an open question for future research.
extension_of_usefulness_semantic_network.paper: Semantic Memory Structure and Self-Evaluation of Creativity: Evidence Across Tasks and Dimensions

fragmentation_as_barrier_to_consensus_in.name: fragmentation_as_barrier_to_consensus_in_creative_cognition
fragmentation_as_barrier_to_consensus_in.description: The field of creative cognition faces fragmentation that hinders consensus and theoretical integration across research groups and paradigms. This collaborative agenda was developed specifically to address this barrier.
fragmentation_as_barrier_to_consensus_in.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

challenge_1_theoretical_definitions_of_c.name: challenge_1_theoretical_definitions_of_core_creative_cognition_constructs
challenge_1_theoretical_definitions_of_c.description: There is a lack of agreed-upon definitions for core research terms including divergent thinking, emotions, and metacognition that are central to creative cognition research. Challenge 1 of the agenda targets this theoretical concern.
challenge_1_theoretical_definitions_of_c.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

challenge_2_role_of_knowledge_types_and_.name: challenge_2_role_of_knowledge_types_and_domains_in_creative_performance
challenge_2_role_of_knowledge_types_and_.description: It remains unclear how different types and domains of knowledge influence creative performance. Challenge 2 of the agenda calls for systematic investigation of knowledge's role in creativity.
challenge_2_role_of_knowledge_types_and_.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

challenge_3_measuring_dynamic_cognitive_.name: challenge_3_measuring_dynamic_cognitive_affective_metacognitive_interactions
challenge_3_measuring_dynamic_cognitive_.description: Research lacks adequate methods to measure the dynamic interactions between cognitive, affective, and metacognitive processes as they unfold over time during creative cognition. Challenge 3 of the agenda emphasizes this measurement gap.
challenge_3_measuring_dynamic_cognitive_.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

challenge_4_environmental_interpersonal_.name: challenge_4_environmental_interpersonal_and_embodied_contexts_of_creativity
challenge_4_environmental_interpersonal_.description: Creative cognition research has insufficiently incorporated environmental and interpersonal contexts including collaboration, technology use, and embodied cognition. Challenge 4 calls for integrating these factors.
challenge_4_environmental_interpersonal_.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

collaborative_research_agenda_for_creati.name: collaborative_research_agenda_for_creative_cognition
collaborative_research_agenda_for_creati.description: A collaborative workshop of 50 European researchers at the Lorentz Centre in Leiden produced a structured research agenda identifying four critical challenges for advancing creative cognition research. This agenda represents a community-level effort to reduce fragmentation.
collaborative_research_agenda_for_creati.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

need_for_standardized_measures_in_creati.name: need_for_standardized_measures_in_creative_cognition
need_for_standardized_measures_in_creati.description: The agenda calls for the development of standardized measures to enable synthesis of existing findings and comparability across studies. This is identified as a key output of collaborative effort.
need_for_standardized_measures_in_creati.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

methodological_innovation_for_creative_c.name: methodological_innovation_for_creative_cognition_complexity
methodological_innovation_for_creative_c.description: The agenda emphasizes that methodological innovations are needed to embrace rather than reduce the inherent complexity of creativity. This is framed as a collective aspiration for the field.
methodological_innovation_for_creative_c.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

synthesis_of_creative_cognition_findings.name: synthesis_of_creative_cognition_findings_across_paradigms
synthesis_of_creative_cognition_findings.description: The agenda calls for systematic synthesis of existing findings across the fragmented creative cognition literature to build more comprehensive theories. This requires collaborative cross-group efforts.
synthesis_of_creative_cognition_findings.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

comprehensive_theory_of_creative_cogniti.name: comprehensive_theory_of_creative_cognition_foundations
comprehensive_theory_of_creative_cogniti.description: The ultimate aim of the research agenda is to build more comprehensive theories of creativity's cognitive foundations by addressing the four identified challenges collaboratively. This goal remains aspirational.
comprehensive_theory_of_creative_cogniti.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

creative_cognition_emerges_from_ordinary.name: creative_cognition_emerges_from_ordinary_cognitive_processes
creative_cognition_emerges_from_ordinary.description: The agenda asserts that creative cognition research has demonstrated how extraordinary creative outcomes emerge from ordinary cognitive processes, framing this as an established insight of the field.
creative_cognition_emerges_from_ordinary.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

collaborative_workshop_as_mechanism_for_.name: collaborative_workshop_as_mechanism_for_research_agenda_development
collaborative_workshop_as_mechanism_for_.description: A workshop of 50 European researchers at the Lorentz Centre in Leiden was used as the mechanism for identifying and articulating the four challenges in this collaborative research agenda.
collaborative_workshop_as_mechanism_for_.paper: Creative Cognition: A Collaborative Research Agenda

martian_game_as_ecological_tool_for_stud.name: Martian Game as ecological tool for studying open-ended question asking
martian_game_as_ecological_tool_for_stud.description: The Martian Game is an open-ended online question-asking game simulating creative problem solving in realistic contexts, designed to overcome challenges in studying question asking naturally. It provides an ecologically valid, interdisciplinary tool for examining open-ended cognition beyond the laboratory.
martian_game_as_ecological_tool_for_stud.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

two_stage_game_structure_linking_questio.name: Two-stage game structure linking question asking to solution production
two_stage_game_structure_linking_questio.description: The game proceeds in two stages: a problem-finding phase where players ask an AI chatbot questions to gather information, and a solution-planning phase where players produce written and visual designs for a Martian solar energy system. This design connects question complexity to downstream solution quality.
two_stage_game_structure_linking_questio.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

coding_scheme_for_question_complexity_or.name: Coding scheme for question complexity, originality, and relevance
coding_scheme_for_question_complexity_or.description: Questions produced during gameplay are coded for complexity, originality, and relevance, while solutions are rated for originality and appropriateness. This provides a multi-dimensional operationalization of question cognitive potential in an open-ended context.
coding_scheme_for_question_complexity_or.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

hypothesis_that_complex_questions_promot.name: Hypothesis that complex questions promote effective problem solving
hypothesis_that_complex_questions_promot.description: The Martian Game is designed to support the hypothesis that asking more complex questions leads to more effective problem solving. This is presented as a guiding hypothesis supported by the game's structure, but full empirical confirmation across participants awaits larger studies.
hypothesis_that_complex_questions_promot.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

pilot_study_validation_of_the_martian_ga.name: Pilot study validation of the Martian Game
pilot_study_validation_of_the_martian_ga.description: A pilot study was conducted to validate the Martian Game's potential for examining open-ended cognition beyond the lab. The pilot supports feasibility but does not constitute a full-scale empirical validation.
pilot_study_validation_of_the_martian_ga.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

cognitive_mechanisms_of_open_ended_quest.name: Cognitive mechanisms of open-ended question asking remain poorly understood
cognitive_mechanisms_of_open_ended_quest.description: Despite question asking being a crucial human skill, its cognitive mechanisms are poorly understood due to challenges in studying it naturally. The Martian Game is proposed as a step toward addressing this gap.
cognitive_mechanisms_of_open_ended_quest.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

ecological_validity_of_the_martian_game_.name: Ecological validity of the Martian Game beyond laboratory paradigms
ecological_validity_of_the_martian_game_.description: The Martian Game is positioned as a more ecologically valid alternative to typical laboratory question-asking paradigms, embedding question asking within a realistic creative problem-solving context. Full external validation with diverse populations remains to be established.
ecological_validity_of_the_martian_game_.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

generalization_of_martian_game_findings_.name: Generalization of Martian Game findings beyond pilot sample
generalization_of_martian_game_findings_.description: The pilot study validates feasibility but findings about question complexity and problem-solving relationships have not yet been replicated or generalized to larger or more diverse samples beyond the initial pilot.
generalization_of_martian_game_findings_.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

question_asking_as_influence_on_social_c.name: Question asking as influence on social cognition, creative problem solving, and information seeking
question_asking_as_influence_on_social_c.description: The paper asserts that question asking is a crucial human skill influencing social cognition, creative problem solving, and information seeking, motivating the development of the Martian Game as a study tool.
question_asking_as_influence_on_social_c.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

integration_of_ai_chatbot_as_information.name: Integration of AI chatbot as information source in creative problem solving game
integration_of_ai_chatbot_as_information.description: The Martian Game incorporates an AI chatbot ('Mark') as the interlocutor through which players ask questions, simulating realistic human-AI interaction in an information-seeking context embedded within creative problem solving.
integration_of_ai_chatbot_as_information.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

full_empirical_testing_of_question_compl.name: Full empirical testing of question complexity to solution quality relationship in Martian Game
full_empirical_testing_of_question_compl.description: A key aspiration of the Martian Game framework is to empirically test whether more complex and original questions lead to higher originality and appropriateness in solutions, which requires a full-scale study beyond the pilot.
full_empirical_testing_of_question_compl.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

extension_of_martian_game_to_other_creat.name: Extension of Martian Game to other creative problem solving domains beyond solar energy design
extension_of_martian_game_to_other_creat.description: The Martian Game focuses on designing a solar energy system for a Martian city, but its authors frame it as an interdisciplinary tool with potential applicability to other open-ended problem-solving contexts.
extension_of_martian_game_to_other_creat.paper: The Martian Game: Exploring Open-Ended Question-Asking via an Online Gaming Environment

network_science_applied_to_experimental_.name: network_science_applied_to_experimental_psychology_special_issue
network_science_applied_to_experimental_.description: This paper introduces a special issue on network science in experimental psychology, summarizing how complex networks are applied across diverse psychological topics using nodes and connections to represent entities and relationships.

complex_networks_reveal_information_unav.name: complex_networks_reveal_information_unavailable_via_conventional_approaches
complex_networks_reveal_information_unav.description: The introduction asserts that network science metrics reveal information about psychological systems that could not be found using contemporary and conventional approaches, motivating the special issue.

breadth_of_node_and_connection_definitio.name: breadth_of_node_and_connection_definitions_in_psychological_network_science
breadth_of_node_and_connection_definitio.description: The special issue illustrates that nodes can represent people, words, or brain regions, and connections can represent friendships, semantic similarity, or co-activation, demonstrating the generality of the network science framework across psychological subfields.

encouragement_of_broader_adoption_of_net.name: encouragement_of_broader_adoption_of_network_science_in_psychology
encouragement_of_broader_adoption_of_net.description: The guest editors and authors explicitly aim to encourage other researchers to apply network science computational techniques to their own questions of interest, signaling an aspirational scope beyond the current special issue.

weblike_structure_influences_processes_o.name: weblike_structure_influences_processes_operating_in_psychological_systems
weblike_structure_influences_processes_o.description: The introduction states that the overall web-like structure of complex networks influences the processes that operate within the system, framing structure-process interaction as a core theoretical principle.

scope_of_topics_addressed_by_network_sci.name: scope_of_topics_addressed_by_network_science_in_experimental_psychology
scope_of_topics_addressed_by_network_sci.description: The special issue covers a range of psychological topics, demonstrating that network science is applicable across diverse subfields rather than being confined to a narrow area.

open_question_of_which_psychological_dom.name: open_question_of_which_psychological_domains_most_benefit_from_network_science
open_question_of_which_psychological_dom.description: While the special issue demonstrates breadth of application, it leaves open which psychological domains or questions gain the greatest novel insight from network science relative to conventional methods.

cognitive_multiplex_network_as_model_of_.name: cognitive_multiplex_network_as_model_of_mental_lexicon
cognitive_multiplex_network_as_model_of_.description: A multidimensional cognitive multiplex network model of the mental lexicon is used to operationalize mental navigation via verbal fluency tasks. This model captures multiple layers of lexical structure simultaneously to quantify how individuals traverse semantic space.
cognitive_multiplex_network_as_model_of_.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

mental_navigation_as_mechanism_underlyin.name: mental_navigation_as_mechanism_underlying_high_level_cognition
mental_navigation_as_mechanism_underlyin.description: Mental navigation—operationalized via verbal fluency tasks modeled on a cognitive multiplex network—is proposed as a common mechanism underlying individual differences in creativity, intelligence, and openness to experience. The paper demonstrates that navigation measures significantly predict these high-level cognitive outcomes.
mental_navigation_as_mechanism_underlyin.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

mental_navigation_predicts_creativity_in.name: mental_navigation_predicts_creativity_intelligence_and_openness
mental_navigation_predicts_creativity_in.description: Quantitative measures of mental navigation derived from multiplex network modeling of verbal fluency responses significantly predict individual differences in creativity, intelligence, and openness to experience in a sample of N=479 participants.
mental_navigation_predicts_creativity_in.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

replication_of_mental_navigation_predict.name: replication_of_mental_navigation_predictions_across_two_fluency_tasks
replication_of_mental_navigation_predict.description: The predictive relationship between mental navigation measures and high-level cognition replicates across both the animal fluency task and the hot synonyms fluency task, supporting the generalizability of the approach within the study.
replication_of_mental_navigation_predict.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

high_level_cognitive_prediction_online_t.name: high_level_cognitive_prediction_online_tool
high_level_cognitive_prediction_online_t.description: An online tool—the High-level Cognitive Prediction tool—was developed to capitalize on the multiplex network navigation approach, enabling applied prediction of high-level cognition from verbal fluency performance.
high_level_cognitive_prediction_online_t.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

generalization_of_multiplex_navigation_p.name: generalization_of_multiplex_navigation_predictions_beyond_current_sample
generalization_of_multiplex_navigation_p.description: The findings are based on a single sample of N=479 participants; whether the predictive relationships generalize to other populations, languages, or fluency tasks beyond animal and hot synonyms fluency remains to be established.
generalization_of_multiplex_navigation_p.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

causal_account_of_how_mental_navigation_.name: causal_account_of_how_mental_navigation_produces_high_level_cognition
causal_account_of_how_mental_navigation_.description: While the study demonstrates that mental navigation measures predict creativity, intelligence, and openness to experience, the causal direction and mechanism by which navigation patterns produce these cognitive outcomes is not established.
causal_account_of_how_mental_navigation_.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

identification_of_specific_multiplex_net.name: identification_of_specific_multiplex_network_navigation_measures_driving_predictions
identification_of_specific_multiplex_net.description: Specific quantitative measures of mental navigation derived from the multiplex network (e.g., clustering, switching, or path-length metrics) are used in regression models to predict high-level cognition, identifying which navigation features are most predictive.
identification_of_specific_multiplex_net.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

integration_of_multiplex_network_navigat.name: integration_of_multiplex_network_navigation_findings_with_broader_creativity_and_intelligence_theory
integration_of_multiplex_network_navigat.description: The paper positions mental navigation as a common mechanism for creativity, intelligence, and openness, but integration of these findings with existing theoretical frameworks (e.g., associative theory, network controllability, DMN accounts) remains to be fully elaborated.
integration_of_multiplex_network_navigat.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

verbal_fluency_as_probe_for_multiplex_me.name: verbal_fluency_as_probe_for_multiplex_mental_navigation
verbal_fluency_as_probe_for_multiplex_me.description: Two verbal fluency tasks—a 2-minute animal fluency task and a 2-minute generating synonyms of the word hot task—are used to operationalize mental navigation within the multiplex network model for a sample of N=479 participants.
verbal_fluency_as_probe_for_multiplex_me.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

extension_of_multiplex_navigation_approa.name: extension_of_multiplex_navigation_approach_to_clinical_or_developmental_populations
extension_of_multiplex_navigation_approa.description: The current work focuses on a general adult sample; applying the multiplex network navigation approach to clinical populations (e.g., aphasia, cochlear implants, dementia) or developmental samples is an open direction.
extension_of_multiplex_navigation_approa.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

practical_utility_of_high_level_cognitiv.name: practical_utility_of_high_level_cognitive_prediction_tool_in_applied_settings
practical_utility_of_high_level_cognitiv.description: The online High-level Cognitive Prediction tool is developed, but its practical utility, reliability, and validity in real-world educational, clinical, or assessment settings has not been empirically established beyond the current study.
practical_utility_of_high_level_cognitiv.paper: A cognitive multiplex network approach to investigate mental navigation and predict high-level cognition

stable_emotion_co_occurrence_network_str.name: Stable emotion co-occurrence network structure across situations and time
stable_emotion_co_occurrence_network_str.description: Analysis of large-scale Japanese social media data reveals that the overall rank ordering and structure of emotion co-occurrence links remains highly stable across crisis events (earthquakes, COVID-19 vaccination periods) and non-crisis periods over a decade at the population level.
stable_emotion_co_occurrence_network_str.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

strengthening_of_tension_associated_emot.name: Strengthening of Tension-associated emotion links during crises
strengthening_of_tension_associated_emot.description: Certain emotion links, particularly those associated with Tension, are significantly strengthened in terms of link strength during earthquake and pre-vaccination periods compared to non-crisis periods, even though the overall rank structure remains stable.
strengthening_of_tension_associated_emot.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

challenge_to_context_dependency_assumpti.name: Challenge to context-dependency assumption of emotion co-occurrence
challenge_to_context_dependency_assumpti.description: The findings challenge the prevailing assumption that emotion co-occurrence is primarily context-based, suggesting instead that emotions have an intrinsic stable structure at the population level that persists across varied situations.
challenge_to_context_dependency_assumpti.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

novel_computational_network_framework_fo.name: Novel computational network framework for emotion co-occurrence in social media
novel_computational_network_framework_fo.description: The study proposes a computational approach grounded in network theory that uses large-scale textual social media data and co-occurrence of emotion-related words to construct and analyze emotion networks systematically and scalably.
novel_computational_network_framework_fo.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

generalization_of_stable_emotion_network.name: Generalization of stable emotion network structure beyond Japanese social media
generalization_of_stable_emotion_network.description: It remains open whether the stable emotion co-occurrence network structure identified in Japanese social media generalizes to other languages, cultures, or social media platforms beyond the current dataset.
generalization_of_stable_emotion_network.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

causal_account_of_why_tension_links_stre.name: Causal account of why Tension links strengthen during crises
causal_account_of_why_tension_links_stre.description: The study identifies that Tension-associated links strengthen during crises but does not establish a causal or mechanistic explanation for why these specific links are most affected while overall rank structure is preserved.
causal_account_of_why_tension_links_stre.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

extension_of_emotion_network_framework_t.name: Extension of emotion network framework to other crisis types beyond earthquakes and vaccination
extension_of_emotion_network_framework_t.description: The current analysis covers earthquakes and COVID-19 vaccination periods; it remains open whether the same patterns of link strengthening with stable rank structure hold for other crisis types such as pandemics, economic crises, or political events.
extension_of_emotion_network_framework_t.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

application_of_emotion_network_framework.name: Application of emotion network framework to individual-level versus population-level analysis
application_of_emotion_network_framework.description: The stable emotion co-occurrence structure is identified at the population level, leaving open whether similar stability or distinct patterns would emerge at the individual level of analysis.
application_of_emotion_network_framework.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

integration_of_emotion_network_findings_.name: Integration of emotion network findings with psychological theory of emotion structure
integration_of_emotion_network_findings_.description: The findings open new avenues for integrating the empirically observed stable network structure of emotions with existing psychological and affective science theories, but this integration is not completed in the current work.
integration_of_emotion_network_findings_.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

validation_of_emotion_word_co_occurrence.name: Validation of emotion word co-occurrence as proxy for psychological emotion co-occurrence
validation_of_emotion_word_co_occurrence.description: The method relies on co-occurrence of emotion-related words in social media text as a proxy for the actual co-occurrence of emotions in human experience; the degree to which this linguistic proxy accurately captures psychological emotion dynamics remains to be validated.
validation_of_emotion_word_co_occurrence.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

longitudinal_trajectory_of_emotion_link_.name: Longitudinal trajectory of emotion link rank stability over extended or different time windows
longitudinal_trajectory_of_emotion_link_.description: While the study spans a decade, it is open whether the rank stability of emotion links would hold over even longer timescales or across different historical periods with different societal contexts.
longitudinal_trajectory_of_emotion_link_.paper: Stable Emotional Co-occurrence Patterns Revealed by Network Analysis of Social Media

scibert_enhanced_dsi_for_scientific_text.name: SciBERT_enhanced_DSI_for_scientific_text_originality
scibert_enhanced_dsi_for_scientific_text.description: The authors adapt Divergent Semantic Integration (DSI) for scientific texts by replacing the original BERT model with SciBERT, a model trained on scientific corpora, to better capture domain-specific semantic relationships in abstracts and titles.
scibert_enhanced_dsi_for_scientific_text.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_applied_to_51200_scientific_abstract.name: DSI_applied_to_51200_scientific_abstracts_and_titles
dsi_applied_to_51200_scientific_abstract.description: The study applies DSI to a corpus of 51,200 scientific abstracts and titles sourced from the Web of Science, constituting a large-scale empirical application of the measure to scientific literature.
dsi_applied_to_51200_scientific_abstract.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_positively_predicts_5_year_citation_.name: DSI_positively_predicts_5_year_citation_counts_in_scientific_papers
dsi_positively_predicts_5_year_citation_.description: Both BERT-DSI and SciBERT-DSI are statistically significant predictors of the logarithm of 5-year citation counts alongside field, publication year, and author count, with adjusted R-squared values of 0.103 and 0.101 respectively.
dsi_positively_predicts_5_year_citation_.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_citation_correlation_declines_over_t.name: DSI_citation_correlation_declines_over_time
dsi_citation_correlation_declines_over_t.description: The correlation between DSI and citation counts is not stable over time; the study observes a declining relationship between DSI and citation accrual as time progresses after publication.
dsi_citation_correlation_declines_over_t.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_citation_effect_stronger_for_papers_.name: DSI_citation_effect_stronger_for_papers_with_fewer_authors
dsi_citation_effect_stronger_for_papers_.description: DSI plays a more pronounced role in accruing early citations for papers with fewer authors, suggesting that solo or small-team work benefits more from conceptual originality in attracting attention.
dsi_citation_effect_stronger_for_papers_.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_varies_substantially_across_scientif.name: DSI_varies_substantially_across_scientific_subjects_and_fields
dsi_varies_substantially_across_scientif.description: DSI scores differ substantially across research subjects and fields, indicating that conceptual originality as measured by DSI is not uniformly distributed across the scientific landscape.
dsi_varies_substantially_across_scientif.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

scibert_dsi_versus_bert_dsi_comparative_.name: SciBERT_DSI_versus_BERT_DSI_comparative_performance
scibert_dsi_versus_bert_dsi_comparative_.description: SciBERT-DSI and BERT-DSI yield very similar adjusted R-squared values (0.101 vs 0.103) in predicting citation counts, suggesting comparable predictive utility despite SciBERT's domain-specific training.
scibert_dsi_versus_bert_dsi_comparative_.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

causal_account_of_why_dsi_citation_corre.name: causal_account_of_why_DSI_citation_correlation_declines_over_time
causal_account_of_why_dsi_citation_corre.description: The study documents a declining correlation between DSI and citation counts over time but does not establish a causal or mechanistic account of why this temporal pattern occurs.
causal_account_of_why_dsi_citation_corre.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

generalization_of_scibert_dsi_findings_b.name: generalization_of_SciBERT_DSI_findings_beyond_Web_of_Science_corpus
generalization_of_scibert_dsi_findings_b.description: The findings are based on a specific corpus of 51,200 abstracts from Web of Science; whether results generalize to other databases, publication types, or languages remains untested.
generalization_of_scibert_dsi_findings_b.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

limited_explanatory_power_of_dsi_for_cit.name: limited_explanatory_power_of_DSI_for_citation_variance
limited_explanatory_power_of_dsi_for_cit.description: The adjusted R-squared values of approximately 0.10 indicate that DSI accounts for only a modest proportion of citation variance, leaving the majority of citation differences unexplained.
limited_explanatory_power_of_dsi_for_cit.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

integration_of_scibert_dsi_with_other_sc.name: integration_of_SciBERT_DSI_with_other_scientometric_measures
integration_of_scibert_dsi_with_other_sc.description: The authors position DSI as complementary to existing scientometric measures that rarely assess textual originality; full integration with bibliometric and structural measures remains a future direction.
integration_of_scibert_dsi_with_other_sc.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

dsi_as_direct_measure_of_conceptual_orig.name: DSI_as_direct_measure_of_conceptual_originality_in_scientific_writing
dsi_as_direct_measure_of_conceptual_orig.description: The authors argue that DSI provides a valuable means of directly quantifying conceptual originality embedded in scientific writing, contrasting it with existing measures that focus on citation patterns or co-authorship networks rather than textual content.
dsi_as_direct_measure_of_conceptual_orig.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

causal_account_of_why_fewer_author_paper.name: causal_account_of_why_fewer_author_papers_show_stronger_DSI_citation_effect
causal_account_of_why_fewer_author_paper.description: The finding that DSI predicts early citation accrual more strongly for fewer-author papers is documented but no mechanistic explanation is provided for why team size moderates the originality-impact relationship.
causal_account_of_why_fewer_author_paper.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

replication_of_scibert_dsi_citation_find.name: replication_of_SciBERT_DSI_citation_finding_in_independent_corpus
replication_of_scibert_dsi_citation_find.description: The DSI-citation relationship is established on a single Web of Science corpus; replication in an independent corpus is needed to confirm robustness of the findings.
replication_of_scibert_dsi_citation_find.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

optimal_language_model_for_dsi_computati.name: optimal_language_model_for_DSI_computation_in_scientific_domains
optimal_language_model_for_dsi_computati.description: While SciBERT is used as an improvement over BERT for scientific text, it remains open whether other domain-specific or more recent language models would yield superior DSI-citation predictions.
optimal_language_model_for_dsi_computati.paper: Investigating the originality of scientific papers across time and domain: A quantitative analysis

associative_thought_enhances_scene_gist_.name: Associative thought enhances scene-gist memory but hampers scene-detail memory
associative_thought_enhances_scene_gist_.description: In an online experiment (N=75), participants who generated chained-free associations after viewing scenes showed better gist memory but worse detail memory compared to those performing phonological processing, indicating that associative thought promotes contextual encoding over detail encoding.
associative_thought_enhances_scene_gist_.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

scene_labeling_mediates_associative_thou.name: Scene labeling mediates associative thought effect on scene-gist memory
scene_labeling_mediates_associative_thou.description: A follow-up experiment (N=74) found that when participants explicitly labeled scenes before either associative or phonological processing, scene-gist memory was prioritized and the memory differences between conditions were eliminated, implicating implicit scene labeling as the mediating mechanism.
scene_labeling_mediates_associative_thou.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

implicit_scene_labeling_during_associati.name: Implicit scene labeling during associative thought as mechanism for gist memory benefit
implicit_scene_labeling_during_associati.description: The authors propose that associative thought facilitates scene-gist memory because it promotes implicit labeling of perceived scenes, which encourages contextual encoding; this is supported by the mediation finding but the implicit nature of the labeling during free association is inferred rather than directly observed.
implicit_scene_labeling_during_associati.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

creativity_positively_correlates_with_se.name: Creativity positively correlates with semantic distances between scene-based associations
creativity_positively_correlates_with_se.description: Across both experiments, individual creativity scores were positively correlated with the semantic distances between participants' scene-based free associations, extending prior findings linking creativity to broader associative processes.
creativity_positively_correlates_with_se.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

creativity_not_correlated_with_scene_mem.name: Creativity not correlated with scene memory performance
creativity_not_correlated_with_scene_mem.description: In both experiments, creativity scores showed no significant correlation with either scene-gist or scene-detail memory, suggesting creativity does not directly enhance or impair scene memory encoding.
creativity_not_correlated_with_scene_mem.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

semantic_distance_of_scene_based_associa.name: Semantic distance of scene-based associations is independent of scene memory benefit
semantic_distance_of_scene_based_associa.description: The enhancement of scene-gist memory by associative thought was found regardless of the conceptual (semantic) distances between responses, suggesting the memory benefit is not driven by the remoteness of associations but rather by the associative process itself.
semantic_distance_of_scene_based_associa.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

generalization_of_associative_thought_sc.name: Generalization of associative thought scene-gist memory benefit beyond current online sample
generalization_of_associative_thought_sc.description: Both experiments were conducted online with relatively small samples (N=75 and N=74), and it remains unknown whether the gist memory benefit of associative thought generalizes to in-laboratory settings, other populations, or different scene types.
generalization_of_associative_thought_sc.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

post_perceptual_associative_processes_in.name: Post-perceptual associative processes influence memory consolidation and creative thought
post_perceptual_associative_processes_in.description: The authors argue that the findings highlight the likely effect of post-perceptual associative processes on higher-order cognitive functions such as memory consolidation and creative thought, framing associative thought as a bridge between perception and cognition.
post_perceptual_associative_processes_in.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

causal_mechanism_linking_associative_tho.name: Causal mechanism linking associative thought to contextual encoding via labeling
causal_mechanism_linking_associative_tho.description: While scene labeling mediates the gist memory effect, it remains unclear how exactly free association promotes implicit scene labeling and whether the associative chaining process itself drives the labeling or whether other aspects of the task (e.g., semantic activation, attention) are responsible.
causal_mechanism_linking_associative_tho.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

intermediate_creativity_task_reliance_on.name: Intermediate creativity task reliance on associative processes
intermediate_creativity_task_reliance_on.description: The authors note that the intermediate creativity task used between scene study and memory test also relies on associative processes, which is relevant to interpreting results but is stated as an assumption rather than directly tested in this study.
intermediate_creativity_task_reliance_on.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

generalization_of_scene_labeling_mediati.name: Generalization of scene labeling mediation beyond explicit labeling instruction
generalization_of_scene_labeling_mediati.description: The mediation finding used an explicit labeling instruction as a proxy for implicit labeling during associative thought; it remains open whether implicit labeling during natural associative thought operates via the same cognitive mechanism as explicit labeling.
generalization_of_scene_labeling_mediati.paper: Spontaneous associative thought may facilitate scene-gist memory via implicit scene-labeling

context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_me.name: context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_metaphor_comprehension
context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_me.description: Neural activations differ for the same metaphoric sentences depending on whether they appear in meaningful narrative versus jabberwocky contexts, confirming context modulates metaphor comprehension mechanisms.
context_affects_neural_mechanisms_for_me.paper: Context matters: Novel metaphors in supportive and non-supportive contexts (Preprint)
